reformulated_task:
Title: Regime-Based Volatility Trading on a Single Asset (e.g., AAPL)  
Main idea: Preserve the spirit of PCA regimes via proxies on a single asset. Replace PC1 with a composite volatility index CVI, PC2 with momentum/regime shift via MACD/RSI, PC3 as observed “noise” but not a signal. Depending on the regime (low/high volatility), change the entry type and position size.  
Indicators: ATR(14), BollingerBands(20,2), EMA(ATR,50), EMA(BB_width,50), EMA(10), EMA(30), SMA(50), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), CrossOver.  
Proxy “PC1” (volatility regime):  
- BB_width = upperBB − lowerBB;  
- CVI = 0.5 * (ATR / EMA(ATR,50)) + 0.5 * (BB_width / EMA(BB_width,50)).  
- Regimes: low volatility if CVI < 0.9; high volatility if CVI > 1.1; intermediate zone — no new entries.  
Proxy “PC2” (shift in dominance/momentum):  
- Shift signal: CVI crossing the 1.0 level together with the MACD histogram crossing 0 in the same direction (momentum confirmation). Additional filter: RSI crosses 50 in the direction of the signal.  
Proxy “PC3” (idiosyncratic volatility — no signals):  
- Observed noise metric J = ATR / BB_width (used to monitor market “choppiness”, not included in the strategy).  
Entry conditions:  
1) Low volatility regime (CVI < 0.9) — trend logic:  
- Long: CrossOver(EMA(10), EMA(30)) upward, Close > SMA(50), RSI > 50.  
- Short (if allowed): opposite conditions.  
2) High volatility regime (CVI > 1.1) — mean reversion:  
- Long: touch/break of the lower Bollinger band + RSI < 35; target — the middle band (mid) or RSI > 55.  
- Short (if allowed): touch/break of the upper band + RSI > 65; target — mid or RSI < 45.  
3) Entry on regime shift (PC2-like trigger):  
- When CVI crosses 1.0 and the MACD histogram crosses 0 in the same direction (RSI confirms >/< 50), open a position in the direction of momentum. Additional filter: Close on the correct side of SMA(50).  
Exit conditions:  
- Regime change: close positions if CVI crosses back through the 1.0 zone (for high-volatility trades — dropping below 1.0; for low-volatility trades — rising above 1.0).  
- Technical take-profit/stop: ATR stops (in low volatility 1.5*ATR, in high — 2.5*ATR); for mean reversion — exit at the mid Bollinger band or by RSI conditions.  
- Time-based: close all positions at the end of the trading day.  
Risk management and position sizing:  
- Base risk R% per trade; position size is inversely proportional to CVI:  
  • CVI < 0.9: 1.3x base size  
  • 0.9 ≤ CVI ≤ 1.1: 1.0x  
  • CVI > 1.1: 0.7x  
- Maximum 1 simultaneous position (1 asset).  
Data/fields: OHLCV only; all calculations via available indicators (ATR, BollingerBands, EMA, SMA, RSI, MACD, CrossOver).
description:
Title: Regime-Based Volatility Trading on a Single Asset (e.g., AAPL)  
Main idea: Preserve the spirit of PCA regimes via proxies on a single asset. Replace PC1 with a composite volatility index CVI, PC2 with momentum/regime shift via MACD/RSI, PC3 as observed “noise” but not a signal. Depending on the regime (low/high volatility), change the entry type and position size.  
Indicators: ATR(14), BollingerBands(20,2), EMA(ATR,50), EMA(BB_width,50), EMA(10), EMA(30), SMA(50), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), CrossOver.  
Proxy “PC1” (volatility regime):  
- BB_width = upperBB − lowerBB;  
- CVI = 0.5 * (ATR / EMA(ATR,50)) + 0.5 * (BB_width / EMA(BB_width,50)).  
- Regimes: low volatility if CVI < 0.9; high volatility if CVI > 1.1; intermediate zone — no new entries.  
Proxy “PC2” (shift in dominance/momentum):  
- Shift signal: CVI crossing the 1.0 level together with the MACD histogram crossing 0 in the same direction (momentum confirmation). Additional filter: RSI crosses 50 in the direction of the signal.  
Proxy “PC3” (idiosyncratic volatility — no signals):  
- Observed noise metric J = ATR / BB_width (used to monitor market “choppiness”, not included in the strategy).  
Entry conditions:  
1) Low volatility regime (CVI < 0.9) — trend logic:  
- Long: CrossOver(EMA(10), EMA(30)) upward, Close > SMA(50), RSI > 50.  
- Short (if allowed): opposite conditions.  
2) High volatility regime (CVI > 1.1) — mean reversion:  
- Long: touch/break of the lower Bollinger band + RSI < 35; target — the middle band (mid) or RSI > 55.  
- Short (if allowed): touch/break of the upper band + RSI > 65; target — mid or RSI < 45.  
3) Entry on regime shift (PC2-like trigger):  
- When CVI crosses 1.0 and the MACD histogram crosses 0 in the same direction (RSI confirms >/< 50), open a position in the direction of momentum. Additional filter: Close on the correct side of SMA(50).  
Exit conditions:  
- Regime change: close positions if CVI crosses back through the 1.0 zone (for high-volatility trades — dropping below 1.0; for low-volatility trades — rising above 1.0).  
- Technical take-profit/stop: ATR stops (in low volatility 1.5*ATR, in high — 2.5*ATR); for mean reversion — exit at the mid Bollinger band or by RSI conditions.  
- Time-based: close all positions at the end of the trading day.  
Risk management and position sizing:  
- Base risk R% per trade; position size is inversely proportional to CVI:  
  • CVI < 0.9: 1.3x base size  
  • 0.9 ≤ CVI ≤ 1.1: 1.0x  
  • CVI > 1.1: 0.7x  
- Maximum 1 simultaneous position (1 asset).  
Data/fields: OHLCV only; all calculations via available indicators (ATR, BollingerBands, EMA, SMA, RSI, MACD, CrossOver).

reformulated_task: Swing breakout strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) based on OHLCV and indicators SMA(200), BollingerBands(20,2), ATR(14) and SMA(volume,20). 1) Trend filter: entries are allowed only if Close > SMA(200). 2) Consolidation filter (replacing flags/triangles/rectangles with quantitative features): the low-volatility conditions are met simultaneously — ATR(14)/Close < 0.02 and the relative Bollinger Bands width (Upper-BB − Lower-BB)/Middle-BB < 0.04 over the last 10 bars (at least 8 out of 10). 3) Pattern levels: Resistance = maximum over N=20 bars, Support = minimum over N=20 bars. 4) Entry signal (Breakout): place a buy stop order slightly above resistance at the price Entry = HHV(20) + max(0.001*Close, 0.25*ATR(14)); close confirmation is not required (use a stop order for the breakout). 5) Immediate overall stop-loss: Stop = LLV(20) − 0.25*ATR(14). 6) Exits and position management: the position is split into three equal parts: T1 — sell 1/3 at +2% from Entry; T2 — sell 1/3 at +7% from Entry; T3 — hold 1/3 with a Chandelier-type trailing stop: Trail = HighestCloseSinceEntry − 3*ATR(14), recalculate on each bar and trail only upward. 7) Volume confirmation (optional): entry is allowed if Volume > SMA(Volume,20) * 1.3 on the breakout bar. 8) Trade cancellation: if the daily Open > Entry (gap up above the entry price) — cancel the stop order; if before the stop order is filled ATR(14)/Close jumps to ≥ 0.025 or the Bollinger Bands width becomes ≥ 0.06 — cancel the stop order (avoid breakouts on volatility). 9) If the overall stop-loss level is reached before the entry is filled — the trade is not opened. 10) Position sizing: no more than one active position; no pyramiding; splitting strictly 1/3 + 1/3 + 1/3.

reformulated_task: Synthetic “covered call” on a single asset (OHLCV): hold a long-term core long position. Every 21 bars we start a “cycle” and set a virtual strike K as max(Close * 1.15, upper Bollinger(20, 2σ) band) to reflect the idea of 10–20% OTM and current volatility. Rules: 1) Reaching K within the cycle — lock in gains: sell 30–50% of the position (analog of assignment), then buy back that portion upon a return to SMA(20) or upon a touch of the lower Bollinger band. 2) If ≤2 bars remain until the end of the cycle — do not open new sells (except a roll). 3) “Deep ITM” (roll up and out): if Close > K + ATR(14) and ≤5 bars remain until the end of the cycle — sell a larger portion (up to 70%), immediately start a new cycle and recalculate K from the current price. 4) If K is not reached by the end of the cycle — treat the “option” as expiring OTM: do not change the position, simply start a new cycle and recalculate K. Risk management: always keep a minimum of 50–70% of the position “core”; trade only one asset and use SMA, BollingerBands, ATR, and a bar counter for timing.

reformulated_task: SPT — RSI Bullish Divergence + Breakout with Volume Confirmation (4H). Long entry: (1) on the 4-hour chart, an RSI(14) bullish divergence forms: price makes a lower low relative to the previous local low, while RSI makes a higher low; (2) then a resistance breakout occurs: a close above the high of the last 20 bars; (3) the current bar’s volume is above average — volume > SMA(volume, 20). Exit: stop-loss 2% below the entry price; take-profit 6% above; alternative exit — on RSI bearish divergence (price makes a higher high, RSI makes a lower high) or a close below the low of the last 10 bars after entry. Risk management: risk 1% of capital per trade; position size = 1% of capital / (0.02 * entry price). Time filter: signals are accepted only in November–December 2025.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Gap Momentum” on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) as a proxy for news gaps, using only OHLCV and standard indicators. Indicators: ATR(14), SMA(volume, 20). Filters: (1) Liquidity — SMA(volume, 20) ≥ 1,000,000; (2) Volatility — ATR(14)/Close[-1] ≥ 1%. Gap trigger: “overnight” gap between Open[0] and Close[-1] ≥ 1.5 × ATR(14) and, for a long, Open[0] > High[-1] (breakaway), for a short, Open[0] < Low[-1]. Momentum strength confirmation: Volume[0] > 1.2 × SMA(volume, 20) and a directional candle on the gap day (for a long Close[0] > Open[0], for a short Close[0] < Open[0]). Entry: on the next candle (next bar open) after the gap day in the direction of the gap. Exit: take-profit +2% from the entry price, stop-loss −1% from the entry price, or time-based exit after N bars (e.g., 12 bars for 5-minute data). Risk/money management: risk per trade 1% of capital; position size = 1% of capital / (stop in points). Maximum 1 simultaneous position.

reformulated_task:
Profit-Backed Expansion (Spot instead of LEAPS) on a single asset with OHLCV and standard indicators. Idea: increase exposure using already earned profit on the base long position, without risking the initial capital.

Entry (additional long tranche): when (1) price is within 10% of the 52-week high: Close >= 0.9 * Highest(High, 252); (2) RSI(14) > 50; (3) return of the base position >= +40%: Close / base_entry_price - 1 >= 0.4; (4) liquidity filter: SMA(Volume, 20) > SMA(Volume, 100); (5) there is no active additional tranche.

Position size of the additional tranche: buy_shares = floor(0.5 * UnrealizedProfit_base / Close), where UnrealizedProfit_base = (Close - base_entry_price) * base_shares; if buy_shares > 0, buy buy_shares.

Target level (analog of an OTM strike): take-profit at +12% from the tranche entry price: TP = entry_price * 1.12.

Exit from the additional tranche: (1) TP hit: Close >= TP; (2) protective exit on a drawdown of the underlying asset: Close <= 0.8 * base_entry_price; (3) time stop: if 126 trading days have passed since entry and TP has not been reached — close the tranche.

Risk management: the cost of the additional tranche does not exceed 50% of the current unrealized profit on the base; only one additional tranche is active at a time; the base position is not closed by these rules.

Style: breakout/momentum based on 52-week high + RSI, position expansion is financed by profit instead of using options.

reformulated_task: Strategy replacement for CPS on 1 asset (AAPL): mean-reversion long on the underlying as a proxy for selling a cash-secured put. Indicators: EMA(20), RSI(14), ATR(14). Entry: (1) Close < EMA(20); (2) RSI(14) < 40; (3) volatility filter ATR(14)/Close < 0.04; (4) anti-change: the current Close is not lower than the previous Close by more than 1% (avoid entry after a sharp drop). Enter at market on the next bar when conditions are met, only one position at a time. Exit (profit): when Close ≥ Entry + 0.5*ATR(14) or Close ≥ EMA(20) (whichever occurs first) — imitation of “premium decay”. Exit (stop): when Close ≤ Entry − min(1*ATR(14), 0.02*Entry). Time stop: close the position after 5 trading days if targets/stop are not reached. Risk management: calculate position size so that risk to the stop is no more than 1% of capital.

reformulated_task: Strategy "Synthetic Wheel" for 1 asset in Backtrader (without options). Idea: simulate selling cash-secured puts via limit buys below the market during high volatility and simulate covered calls via limit sells above the market after the purchase. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), ATR(14), EMA(50), EMA(200), RSI(14). Filters: trade only if 1 <= Close <= 15; volatility is high when (upperBB - lowerBB)/midBB > 0.06 or ATR(14)/Close > 0.03; additional safety trend filter: EMA(50) >= EMA(200). Entry ("put selling" surrogate): if all filters are met and there is no open position/limit order, once every 20 bars place a limit buy at price BuyLim = min(lowerBB, Close*0.93). Order size: so that BuyLim * size <= 3% of the portfolio; up to 3 tranches are allowed (max. 9% of the portfolio in total). After the limit buy is filled (position opened) — manage as a "covered call": immediately place a limit sell SellLim = max(upperBB, position average price*1.07); if the limit is not filled within 20 bars, replace it with a new SellLim based on the current BB (simulation of a monthly roll). Averaging (adding tranches) on a drop: if Close <= average price - 1.5*ATR and RSI(14) < 35, add the next tranche using the same sizing rule until the limit of 3 tranches and 9% of the portfolio is reached. Protective exit (catastrophic): close the entire position if Close < EMA(200)*0.9 or Close <= average price - 4*ATR. Exits: 1) via the limit sell SellLim — take profit (analog of covered call assignment); 2) via the protective stop; 3) after each sell/buy cycle, reset new limit levels every 20 bars. Breakeven point = position average price, decreases with partial profit-taking (realized profit from closed portions reduces the effective cost basis). Keep records of all cycles: date/price of limit buy, limit sell, realized profit, number of tranches, and current average price.

reformulated_task: Daily long strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL), emulating the idea of a short put spread: enter a long position at the market open if two conditions are simultaneously met: (1) low volatility — the current BollingerBands(20, 2) bandwidth, calculated as (upper − lower)/middle, is in the lowest 20% of its 100-day range (proxy IV Rank ≤ 20); (2) the opening gap is no worse than −0.4%: (Open_t − Close_{t−1})/Close_{t−1} ≥ −0.004. Entry — once per day when conditions are met (if a position is already open, do not make new entries). Exit — the same day at the close price (hold for 1 bar). No stop-loss is used. Position size — fixed stake (the same number of shares on each entry, as a substitute for “2 contracts”). Do not open a position if at least one filter is not met.

reformulated_task:
Strategy name: Position Management Based on MACD Crossovers  
Idea: Open a long position when a "buy signal" appears from the MACD indicator and close it on the opposite "sell signal". The position state is controlled so as not to execute repeated entries or exits.  
Data/asset: 1 instrument (e.g., AAPL), available OHLCV fields.  
Indicators: MACD(12,26,9) and CrossOver.  
Entry conditions: if the MACD line crosses the signal line from below to above (CrossOver(macd, signal) > 0) and there is no position — open a long position.  
Exit conditions: if the MACD line crosses the signal line from above to below (CrossOver(macd, signal) < 0) and a position is open — close the position.  
Risk management and position sizing: fixed position size for the entire backtest period; stop-loss and take-profit are not used; exit only on the reverse MACD signal.  
Additional rules: repeated entries are ignored until the current position is closed; exit signals are ignored when there is no position.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Buying Spike → Momentum” for a single asset on OHLCV: use SMA(20) on price for trend, SMA(5) and SMA(20) on volume to detect anomalous volume, Stochastic(14,3,3) to assess closing strength, ATR(14) as a volatility filter. Entry (long on the next bar at market) if: 1) close > SMA(20) on price; 2) SMA(5) on volume ≥ 3 × SMA(20) on volume (short-term volume spike); 3) Stochastic %K > 80 and (CrossOver(%K, %D) up or close > close[-1]) as confirmation of buying pressure. Exit: 1) close < SMA(20) on price; or 2) the position is held ≥ 10 bars. Risk management: position size — fixed 2% of capital per trade; stop-loss 2% below the entry price. Additional filters: trade only if ATR(14)/close > 0.015 (1.5%) and 20-day liquidity is sufficient: SMA(20, volume) × SMA(20, close) ≥ 10,000,000.

Entry (long on the next bar at market) if: 1) close > SMA(20) on price; 2) SMA(5) on volume ≥ 3 × SMA(20) on volume (short-term volume spike); 3) Stochastic %K > 80 and (CrossOver(%K, %D) up or close > close[-1]) as confirmation of buying pressure. Additional filters: trade only if ATR(14)/close > 0.015 (1.5%) and 20-day liquidity is sufficient: SMA(20, volume) × SMA(20, close) ≥ 10,000,000.

reformulated_task: ORB Intraday Momentum on a single instrument (e.g., AAPL) using only OHLCV and standard Backtrader indicators. Logic: trade the breakout of the opening range (Opening Range, OR) of the first 15-minute candle with a direction and volatility filter, add additional positions on momentum, manage risk via ATR, and close all positions at the end of the day. Indicators: ATR(14) for buffers/positioning, SMA(20) and SMA(50) for the trend filter, MACD(12,26,9) or RSI(14) for momentum confirmation, BollingerBands(20,2) as a support/resistance proxy and targets. Rules: 1) Form the OR using the first 15-minute session candle: OR_high/OR_low and OR width OR_w. If a different timeframe is used, take the first N bars totaling ~15 minutes. Skip the day if ATR(14)/close < 0.008 (low volatility) or OR_w < 0.3*ATR(14). 2) After the OR window closes, place OCO stop-limit orders: Long-stop at OR_high + 0.1*ATR(14) (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR), Short-stop at OR_low - 0.1*ATR(14) (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR). Direction filter: for longs require SMA(20) > SMA(50) and (MACD > 0 or RSI > 55); for shorts SMA(20) < SMA(50) and (MACD < 0 or RSI < 45). 3) Add-on trades (instead of “credit spreads”): after entry on the breakout allow up to 3 additional entries in the same direction (total limit 4 trades per day). Add-on triggers: a) retest of the OR boundary (long — touch/proximity to OR_high ± 0.1*ATR; short — OR_low) while momentum remains (MACD in the entry direction or RSI>55/RSI<45); b) breakout of yesterday’s High (for long) or yesterday’s Low (for short) with confirmed momentum. Size of each add-on = 50% of the initial position. 4) Stop-loss: for long — OR_low − 0.1*ATR(14); for short — OR_high + 0.1*ATR(14). Additionally exit if two consecutive candles closed inside the OR range (loss of momentum). 5) Take-profit: for the initial ORB position the target = entry ± 0.85*OR_w in the trade direction. For add-ons the target = 0.6–0.75 of the distance to the corresponding Bollinger band at entry (long: entry + 0.7*(BB_upper − entry), short: entry − 0.7*(entry − BB_lower)). Alternative: trailing stop 1.5*ATR(14) from the extreme in favor of the position. 6) Time-exit: close all positions by end of session (EOD). 7) Risk management/position sizing: risk on the initial trade R = 0.5–1.0% of capital; size = R$ / (|Entry − Stop|). Each add-on — 50% of the initial. Daily trade limit = 4. All entries/exits via stop-limit (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR) to control slippage.
description: ORB Intraday Momentum on a single instrument (e.g., AAPL) using only OHLCV and standard Backtrader indicators. Logic: trade the breakout of the opening range (Opening Range, OR) of the first 15-minute candle with a direction and volatility filter, add additional positions on momentum, manage risk via ATR, and close all positions at the end of the day. Indicators: ATR(14) for buffers/positioning, SMA(20) and SMA(50) for the trend filter, MACD(12,26,9) or RSI(14) for momentum confirmation, BollingerBands(20,2) as a support/resistance proxy and targets. Rules: 1) Form the OR using the first 15-minute session candle: OR_high/OR_low and OR width OR_w. If a different timeframe is used, take the first N bars totaling ~15 minutes. Skip the day if ATR(14)/close < 0.008 (low volatility) or OR_w < 0.3*ATR(14). 2) After the OR window closes, place OCO stop-limit orders: Long-stop at OR_high + 0.1*ATR(14) (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR), Short-stop at OR_low - 0.1*ATR(14) (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR). Direction filter: for longs require SMA(20) > SMA(50) and (MACD > 0 or RSI > 55); for shorts SMA(20) < SMA(50) and (MACD < 0 or RSI < 45). 3) Add-on trades (instead of “credit spreads”): after entry on the breakout allow up to 3 additional entries in the same direction (total limit 4 trades per day). Add-on triggers: a) retest of the OR boundary (long — touch/proximity to OR_high ± 0.1*ATR; short — OR_low) while momentum remains (MACD in the entry direction or RSI>55/RSI<45); b) breakout of yesterday’s High (for long) or yesterday’s Low (for short) with confirmed momentum. Size of each add-on = 50% of the initial position. 4) Stop-loss: for long — OR_low − 0.1*ATR(14); for short — OR_high + 0.1*ATR(14). Additionally exit if two consecutive candles closed inside the OR range (loss of momentum). 5) Take-profit: for the initial ORB position the target = entry ± 0.85*OR_w in the trade direction. For add-ons the target = 0.6–0.75 of the distance to the corresponding Bollinger band at entry (long: entry + 0.7*(BB_upper − entry), short: entry − 0.7*(entry − BB_lower)). Alternative: trailing stop 1.5*ATR(14) from the extreme in favor of the position. 6) Time-exit: close all positions by end of session (EOD). 7) Risk management/position sizing: risk on the initial trade R = 0.5–1.0% of capital; size = R$ / (|Entry − Stop|). Each add-on — 50% of the initial. Daily trade limit = 4. All entries/exits via stop-limit (limit-offset = 0.05*ATR) to control slippage.

reformulated_task: Volatility trading strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL). On each bar of the selected timeframe (e.g., 1 min), the percentage price change over the last N bars is calculated (default N=5). Entry (long): if (Close / Close[-N] - 1) ≥ 3% and volatility is elevated (ATR(14)/Close ≥ 1%), open a long position with a fixed size equivalent to 100 USDT. Exit: take-profit +6% from the entry price or stop-loss -3% from the entry price (preferably via bracket orders). Configurable parameters: N, growth threshold pct_up, tp_pct, sl_pct, volatility threshold vol_thresh, position size position_usdt, flag for enabling the ATR filter. Switching between test/real modes is supported via the mode parameter.

reformulated_task: Long-term strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with a proxy CAGR based on price: compute a 3-year rolling CAGR on closes excluding dividends and enter long when CAGR_3Y >= 5% and price crosses SMA(200) from below to above; exit when price crosses SMA(200) from above to below or when CAGR_3Y < 0. Calculation: TotalReturn_3Y = Close / Close[-756]; CAGR_3Y = (TotalReturn_3Y)^(1/3) - 1. Use only OHLCV, SMA, and CrossOver. History < 756 days — no trades.

reformulated_task: Breakout strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on 5-minute candles without additional indicators: enter long when the current Close is above the maximum Close over the previous 10 bars; exit long when the current Close is below the minimum Close over the previous 10 bars. Position size — 1% of capital; stop-loss — 2% below the entry price. To minimize latency, use a direct 5-minute feed without resampling and execute the logic strictly on each new 5-minute bar, relying only on standard fields (open, high, low, close, volume).

reformulated_task:
A single-asset “synthetic hedge” strategy on AAPL, mimicking a market maker’s hedge via partial opposite leverage in the same asset. Idea: when the main signal appears, open a “base” position of fixed size and simultaneously reduce market risk with a partial opposite hedge, whose size dynamically depends on the ratio of the long-term and short-term volatilities of the same asset.

Indicators and parameters:
• SMA(50) on close (trend)
• ATR(14) (volatility)
• BollingerBands(50, dev=2) to estimate long-term σ
• BollingerBands(20, dev=2) to estimate short-term σ
• MACD(12,26,9) + CrossOver for filter/signal.

Estimate σ via Bollinger:
σ_long = (BB50.top − BB50.mid)/2,
σ_short = (BB20.top − BB20.mid)/2.

Hedge coefficient:
h = clip(σ_long/σ_short, 0, 1.5), if σ_short≈0, then h=0.

Position sizes:
• Main position — fixed notional 100000 c.u.: units_main = floor(100000 / close).
• Synthetic hedge — opposite fraction of the same asset: units_hedge = floor(units_main * h).
Actual net target position = units_net = units_main − units_hedge for long (and vice versa for short).

Entry:
• Long when CrossOver(close, SMA(50)) > 0 and MACD > Signal > 0 and ATR(14)/close ≥ 0.005.
• Short when CrossOver(close, SMA(50)) < 0 and MACD < Signal < 0 and ATR(14)/close ≥ 0.005.
On entry, target units_net (positive for long, negative for short).

Exit:
• Close the position (go to 0) on the opposite crossover of price and SMA(50).

Hedge update:
• On each bar, while the signal remains valid, recalculate h and pull the position toward the new units_net (dynamic volatility-based “delta hedging”).

Additional filters:
• Volatility filter: ATR(14)/close ≥ 0.5%
• Trend filter (replacement for correlation): for long, MACD above signal and >0; for short, MACD below signal and <0.

All calculations and trades are performed on a single asset and the available Backtrader indicators.

reformulated_task: Intraday momentum strategy on a single asset (OHLCV) as a proxy for ODTE calls: entry either at the open or on the “dip” within the first 30–60 minutes. Volatility filter: trade only when ATR(14) > SMA(20) of ATR(14). Direction filter: price above SMA(5) and/or MACD histogram > 0. Entry 1 (open): buy on the first candle of the day when the filters are met. Entry 2 (dip): if price dropped ≥0.5% from the day’s opening price, then a reversal confirmation occurred (Stochastic %K crosses %D upward from the <20 zone or CrossOver of price above EMA(9)), and the direction filters are met — buy. Exit: take profit on a rise of 2.5×ATR(14) from the entry price, stop loss 1×ATR(14), forced close at end of day. Position size: fixed (1 unit), no more than one position at a time.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Sentiment proxy with a weak-signal amplifier” for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on OHLCV with indicators SMA/RSI/MACD/Bollinger/ATR/CrossOver. Idea: replace news sentiment with proxy price/volume signals that often precede a move (a sharp momentum shift and/or an opening gap with volume), and amplify weak signals during periods of elevated volatility. Indicators and parameters: (1) Proxy “General Sentiment”: momentum increase/decrease via ΔRSI = RSI(14) − RSI(14)[3 bars ago]; confirmation via CrossOver(MACD(12,26,9).macd, MACD.signal). (2) Proxy “Policy Sentiment”: opening gap and volume — for long: Open(0) > High(-1) + 0.5*ATR(14) and Volume(0) > 1.5*SMA(Volume,20); for short: Open(0) < Low(-1) − 0.5*ATR(14) and Volume(0) > 1.5*SMA(Volume,20). (3) Volatility: ATR(14); high regime — if ATR(14) > 1.5*SMA(ATR(14),100). (4) “Weak signal detector”: under high ATR the ΔRSI threshold is relaxed, but mandatory MACD confirmation is added. Entry conditions: Long — (A) General Sentiment: ΔRSI ≥ +15 (normal/low volatility) or ΔRSI ≥ +10 (high volatility) AND (if high volatility) CrossOver(MACD.macd, MACD.signal) = 1, AND “price has not reacted yet”: over the last 3 bars there was no close above the upper Bollinger(20,2), and the current Close is not above the middle Bollinger(20,2) line + 0.5*band width. Or (B) Policy Sentiment: the gap and volume condition is met (entry without additional price-reaction filters). Short — symmetrically: ΔRSI ≤ −15 (normal/low volatility) or ΔRSI ≤ −10 (high volatility) and (high volatility) CrossOver(MACD.macd, MACD.signal) = −1, and “price has not reacted yet”: no close below the lower Bollinger(20,2) for 3 bars, Close not below the middle − 0.5*width; or Policy Sentiment with a downward gap and volume. Exits: take-profit +5% from the entry price; stop-loss −3% (mandatory); alternative exit on a “sentiment” reversal: for long — RSI(14) dropping by ≥10 points from the local maximum after entry, for short — RSI(14) rising by ≥10 from the local minimum. Risk management: position size — 1% of capital per trade; stop-loss 3% is placed immediately (bracket); in the test environment hold no more than 1 simultaneous position on the single asset. Additional filters: avoid entries if Bollinger(20,2) has been clearly broken in the direction of the intended trade within the last 3 bars (it is considered that “price has already reacted”); under high ATR entry by General Sentiment is allowed only with volume confirmation (Volume(0) > 1.3*SMA(Volume,20)).

reformulated_task:
Strategy: Single-asset periodic rebalancing (100% weight)

Idea: Manage one available asset by maintaining a constant target allocation of 100% in the asset and forcibly rebalancing the position every 12 weeks. Rebalancing is modeled as a full close and immediate re-open of the position to account for trading costs. Evaluate average performance across multiple random start dates, including commissions.

Data: One instrument with OHLCV. Working timeframe — weekly (resampling from daily data if needed).

Entry: On the selected start date, buy the asset with 100% of the portfolio.

Rebalancing: Every 12 weeks, fully close the position and immediately buy again with 100% of the current portfolio value (simulating rebalancing with transaction costs).

Exit: At the end of 52 weeks, close the position and lock in the result.

Commissions: 0.1% of the amount of each trade (buy and sell) at each rebalancing and at the final exit.

Testing: Select 50 random start dates with an available 52-week forward horizon; for each date, run a separate backtest. For each run, store metrics: total return, maximum drawdown, Sharpe ratio. As the final output, report the average values of the metrics across all 50 runs.

Indicators: Not required (only OHLCV and the rebalancing schedule are used).

reformulated_task: Intraday strategy “1% Playbook (simplified)” for a single asset on the 1–5 minute timeframe using only OHLCV and standard Backtrader indicators. Three independent entry signals: 1) ORB (Opening Range Breakout): in the first 5 minutes (09:30–09:35 ET) the high/low (ORH/ORL) is recorded. Long: after 09:35 the close crosses above ORH (CrossOver(close, ORH)). Short: after 09:35 the close crosses below ORL (CrossOver(ORL, close)). 2) EMA Reclaim (VWAP Reclaim replacement): EMA(20) is used, calculated from the current day’s bars. Long: in the current session the price was previously below EMA(20), then a cross above occurs (CrossOver(close, EMA(20))) and the bar closes above EMA(20). Short: in the current session the price was previously above EMA(20), then a cross below occurs (CrossOver(EMA(20), close)) and the bar closes below EMA(20). 3) Gap-and-Go: the current day’s open is compared to the previous day’s close (PClose). Long: with a bullish gap (Open > PClose) enter on confirmation — the close crosses above the PClose level after the open (CrossOver(close, PClose)). Short: with a bearish gap (Open < PClose) enter on confirmation — the close crosses below PClose (CrossOver(PClose, close)). Exits: for each trade, place a bracket order: stop-loss 1% from the entry price, take-profit 2% from the entry price. Risk management: stop trading for the current day if the cumulative daily loss is ≤ -0.5% of capital; stop trading after two losing trades in a day; auto-close all positions and cancel orders at 15:55 ET. All calculations (ORB, EMA) are performed within a single session; the signals are independent of each other.

reformulated_task: Momentum strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on the 1-minute timeframe with a volume and volatility filter. Indicators: SMA(20) and SMA(50) on closing price; RSI(14); SMA(20) on volume for average volume; ATR(20) for the volatility filter. Enter a long position (only during 9:30–16:00 ET) if simultaneously: (1) the price is within the $10–$200 range; (2) current volume > SMA(volume,20) × 1.2; (3) SMA(20) crosses SMA(50) from below upward; (4) at the moment of the crossover or within the next 5 bars RSI is in the 40–60 range; (5) volatility is elevated: ATR(20) > SMA(ATR(20), 1950) — the average over 5 trading days on minute data. Exit the position if any of the conditions occur: (1) stop-loss — price falls below the low of the last 10 bars; (2) take-profit +2% from the entry price; (3) SMA(20) crosses SMA(50) from above downward. Risk management and position sizing: no more than 1% of capital at risk per trade based on the initial stop (qty = floor(Equity×0.01 / (Entry−Stop))); if the distance to the stop > 2% of the entry price — the trade is skipped. Additionally: no pyramiding — only one open position at a time.

reformulated_task: Title: Short Volatility (Long DTE proxy) on a single asset with BollingerBands and ATR. Idea: instead of selling OTM strikes, we “collect premium” via mean-reversion within the channel; enter at extremes, exit on a half retracement to the mean, with a long time-stop. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), SMA(20) (middle band), ATR(14), SMA(100) on band width, SMA(20) on volume. Entry signals (only if there is no position): volatility filter — BW = (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid; trade when BW > SMA(BW, 100) and BW < 1.5 * SMA(BW, 100); liquidity/news filter — volume < 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20). Entry: Short when price crosses down through the upper band (CrossOver(close, BB.top) < 0); Long when price crosses up through the lower band (CrossOver(close, BB.bot) > 0). Execution at the next open (next open). Position management (place a bracket immediately): target (half retracement) — for Long: TP = entry + 0.5 * (SMA20 - entry); for Short: TP = entry - 0.5 * (entry - SMA20). Stop: SL = 1.5 * ATR(14) from the entry price; additional emergency exit on an adverse move of ±5% from entry. Time management: close the position at market when held for ≥ 45 bars; do not open new positions if there is already an open one (analogous to a ban with low DTE). Position sizing: risk per trade ≤ 1% of capital — size_risk = floor((equity * 0.01) / |entry - SL|); “premium” constraint — target profit ≤ 0.5% of capital — size_target = floor((equity * 0.005) / |TP - entry|); final size = min(size_risk, size_target). Additionally: do not trade under extreme volatility (if BW ≥ 1.5 * SMA(BW, 100)).

Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), SMA(20) (middle band), ATR(14), SMA(100) on band width, SMA(20) on volume. Entry signals (only if there is no position): volatility filter — BW = (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid; trade when BW > SMA(BW, 100) and BW < 1.5 * SMA(BW, 100); liquidity/news filter — volume < 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20). Entry: Short when price crosses down through the upper band (CrossOver(close, BB.top) < 0); Long when price crosses up through the lower band (CrossOver(close, BB.bot) > 0). Execution at the next open (next open). Position management (place a bracket immediately): target (half retracement) — for Long: TP = entry + 0.5 * (SMA20 - entry); for Short: TP = entry - 0.5 * (entry - SMA20). Stop: SL = 1.5 * ATR(14) from the entry price; additional emergency exit on an adverse move of ±5% from entry. Time management: close the position at market when held for ≥ 45 bars; do not open new positions if there is already an open one (analogous to a ban with low DTE). Position sizing: risk per trade ≤ 1% of capital — size_risk = floor((equity * 0.01) / |entry - SL|); “premium” constraint — target profit ≤ 0.5% of capital — size_target = floor((equity * 0.005) / |TP - entry|); final size = min(size_risk, size_target). Additionally: do not trade under extreme volatility (if BW ≥ 1.5 * SMA(BW, 100)).

reformulated_task:
Create a scalping strategy for Backtrader on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with a 5-minute timeframe that filters market regimes in the first 30 minutes using a proxy from available indicators and applies mean-reversion and trend rules.

Regime proxies:
- GammaProxy — the average width of Bollinger Bands (BBands 20, 2σ) over the first 6 bars of the day; High/Low is defined relative to the median of this metric over the previous 20 sessions (low width = High Gamma, high width = Low Gamma).
- SpotVolProxy — the size of the overnight gap |Open_t − Close_{t−1}|/Close_{t−1}; High/Low is defined relative to the median over 20 sessions.

Form 4 regimes: HH, HL, LH, LL.

Entry logic:
1) Mean reversion on RSI(14): long on an RSI cross up through 30, short on a cross down through 70. In High Gamma regimes (H*) it is allowed as standard; in Low Gamma (L*) — only if price simultaneously touches the outer BBands.
2) Trend via EMA: enter long on EMA(9) crossing up through EMA(21), enter short on crossing down. Enable trend signals primarily when SpotVol is High (*H); in LH allow only in the direction of the aggregate move of the first 6 bars (if the return of the first 30 minutes > 0, accept only longs, otherwise only shorts); in HL ignore trend entries.

Exits:
- For MR — take profit at the BBands midline (SMA20) or when RSI > 70 (long)/RSI < 30 (short);
- For trend — on the reverse EMA(9/21) crossover.

Stop-loss ATR(14): 1.0×ATR for MR and 1.5×ATR for trend; optional take-profit 1.0×ATR for MR and 2.0×ATR for trend.

Additional noise filters:
- In LL reduce trade frequency by requiring CrossOver(EMA9, EMA21) confirmation for MR entries;
- In HH allow MR on touches of the outer BBands, but with a closer take-profit (0.75×ATR).

reformulated_task:
Multi-Strategy ORB Income (OHLCV, 1 ticker)

Idea: Preserve the spirit of “options income” and ORB approaches without options, trading a single underlying via price/volume. Three subsystems: 1) range mean-reversion (iron condor analog), 2) ORB return to range under high volatility (iron butterfly 1DTE analog), 3) ORB directional breakout (call/put buying analog).

Instrument and data: one liquid ticker (e.g., AAPL), OHLCV. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), EMA(20), RSI(14, optional), CrossOver. Replace volatility with proxies: Bollinger Band width (BandWidth = (upper-lower)/middle) and ATR.

Global filters:
- No new entries if BandWidth is below the 10th percentile over 252 bars (too “dead” a market).
- No more than 1 active position per strategy, maximum 3 simultaneously.

Strategy 1 — Range mean-reversion (iron condor analog):
- Regime/filter: BandWidth in the lower 30% over 252 bars (compressed bands, “calm” market).
- Entries:
  - Short on touch/breach of the upper Bollinger band and RSI(14) > 60. 
  - Long on touch/breach of the lower band and RSI(14) < 40.
- Exits:
  - Take-profit: the middle band line (mavg) or +0.5R (whichever comes first), where R is the risk to the stop.
  - Stop: 1×ATR(14) beyond the corresponding band (above the upper for short, below the lower for long).
  - Timeout: close after 3-5 bars if neither TP nor SL has triggered (analog of “position lifespan”).

Strategy 2 — ORB return to center (iron butterfly 1DTE analog):
- Regime/filter: high volatility — BandWidth in the upper 70-90% over 252 bars.
- ORB: range of the first N minutes/bars of the session (e.g., first 30 minutes): OR_high/OR_low, midOR = (OR_high+OR_low)/2.
- Entries (fake breakout with reversion):
  - Short: price broke OR_high, then closed back inside the range (close < OR_high). 
  - Long: price broke OR_low, then closed back inside the range (close > OR_low).
- Exits:
  - Take-profit: midOR.
  - Stop: beyond the opening range boundary ± 0.5×ATR(14) in the direction of the position.
  - Time exit: end of session (EOD) — close the position (imitation of 1DTE/0DTE logic).

Strategy 3 — ORB directional breakout (call/put buying analog):
- ORB: the same OR_high/OR_low.
- Entries (confirmed breakout):
  - Long: two consecutive candles close above OR_high and EMA(20) is rising (EMA(20) > EMA(20)[-1]).
  - Short: two consecutive candles close below OR_low and EMA(20) is falling.
- Exits:
  - Stop: 1×ATR(14) from entry or beyond the opposite side of the OR (whichever is closer).
  - Take-profit: 2R or ATR trailing (max of EMA(20) trail and 1×ATR trail).
  - Time exit: EOD.

Position sizing and risk:
- Base capital: 150000.
- Risk per trade: 1% (1500) of capital; position size = risk / stop distance (in $), rounded to whole shares.
- Allocation by strategies: 40% (Str-1), 30% (Str-2), 30% (Str-3) as a limit on simultaneously deployed margin exposure; but risk per trade is still ≤1%.

Additionally:
- Volume filter (optional): do not enter if current volume < volume SMA(20).
- Logging: record signals, entries/exits, R-multiple, and BandWidth percentile.

Compatibility: one ticker, OHLCV only, SMA/EMA/RSI/MACD/BollingerBands/Stochastic/ATR/CrossOver only; no options data and no multi-ticker dependencies.

reformulated_task: Single-asset volatility targeting strategy with monthly rebalancing: use ATR(21) as the volatility estimate instead of Yang–Zhang and monthly scale the share of capital in the single asset inversely proportional to volatility. Indicators: ATR(21) to calculate base volatility and ATR(5) as a high short-term volatility filter. Entry: on the first bar of the month, compute ATR(21) and ATR(5); if ATR(5)/Close > 2% — target weight = 0 (cash until the next month), otherwise target weight w = min(0.4, 0.01 / (ATR(21)/Close)) and rebalance to this weight. Exit: at the beginning of each new month, fully rebalance; if the filter is active — close the position. Risk management: maximum 40% of capital in the asset, the rest in cash; use only OHLCV data and available indicators.

reformulated_task:
Downward reversal strategy without delta (proxy footprint) on a single asset using OHLCV and standard indicators. Core idea: look for a bearish candle with a long upper wick and a volume spike near the upper Bollinger Bands in a downtrend as a signal of failed buying at highs and a likely pullback downward.

Indicators and parameters:
- EMA(50) for trend filtering.
- Bollinger Bands(20, 2) for local extremes and range width.
- ATR(14) for volatility filtering and stop calculation.
- SMA(volume, 20) for assessing a volume spike.

Short entry conditions:
1) Trend: close < EMA(50).
2) Volatility: ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 50).
3) Local extreme: high >= upper Bollinger(20,2) band (a touch is allowed).
4) Candlestick pattern of buyer absorption:
   - Bearish candle (close < open).
   - Close in the lower third of the candle range: (close - low) / (high - low) <= 0.33.
   - Long upper wick as a sign of failed buying: (high - max(open, close)) >= 2 * |close - open| and (high - max(open, close)) >= 1.5 * (min(open, close) - low).
5) Volume: volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20).
6) Signal strengthening (analog of delta difference): the upper wick range is at least 50% of the entire candle: (high - max(open, close)) / (high - low) >= 0.5.

Stop-loss and take-profit:
- Stop-loss: above the high of the signal candle by 0.5 * ATR(14): SL = high_signal + 0.5 * ATR.
- Base take-profit: 1R (where R = the distance from entry to SL).
- Alternative profit-taking: partial take-profit at the Bollinger midline, the rest at 1R; or full exit on a test of the midline if risk management is conservative.

Alternative exit on an opposite signal:
- Bullish candle with a long lower wick near the lower BB band: close > open, (high - close) / (high - low) <= 0.33, (min(open, close) - low) >= 2 * |close - open|, low <= lower BB band, volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20).

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade 1–2% of capital.
- Position size = (allowed risk in $) / (distance to SL in $).
- Do not open new positions if the asset is already in a position.

Additional quality filters (optional):
- Avoid entry when RSI(14) < 35 (for shorts, RSI >= 50 is preferable to avoid shorting into oversold conditions).
- Block entry if the BB midline has been rising for 3+ bars in a row (signal against a strong pullback).

reformulated_task: Market-maker grid strategy for a single asset on OHLCV: on each bar, place 3 limit buy orders and 3 limit sell orders around the fair price defined by SMA(20). Price levels are calculated as deviations from SMA(20): for buys at levels -0.05%, -0.10%, -0.15%; for sells at levels +0.05%, +0.10%, +0.15%. When any order is filled, it is immediately replenished at the same level on the next bar (order TTL = 1 bar). Risk management: the total budget allocated to open limit orders (both sides combined) does not exceed 10% of capital; volume allocation across levels on each side is 20%/30%/50% of the amount allocated to that side; total volume per side is not less than 0.1 units of the asset. Dynamic liquidity adjustment: trade only when ATR(14)/Close <= 0.2% (a proxy for a “tight spread”); if the condition is violated, cancel all orders and wait for recovery. Slippage control: if the actual fill price of a limit order deviates from its limit price by more than 0.1%, cancel all remaining orders and skip the next trading cycle. Position exit occurs via paired execution of the opposite limit order; the goal is to earn from the price difference between levels.

reformulated_task: Volatility-trend strategy on a single asset (D1) using ATR(14), SMA(20), and RSI(14). The main idea: use ATR as a measure of volatility magnitude and determine direction via SMA and RSI, simulating “rising/falling” volatility through ATR expansion in the direction of the trend. Rules: Long entry — if Close > SMA(20), RSI(14) > 55, ATR(14) > EMA(ATR(14), 14) (a sign of volatility expansion), and Volume > SMA(Volume, 20). Short entry — if Close < SMA(20), RSI(14) < 45, ATR(14) > EMA(ATR(14), 14), and Volume > SMA(Volume, 20). Long exit — when Close < SMA(20) or RSI(14) crosses below the 50 level, or via stop-loss/take-profit. Short exit — when Close > SMA(20) or RSI(14) crosses above 50, or via stop-loss/take-profit. Risk management: for long SL = Entry - 1.5*ATR(14), TP = Entry + 2.0*ATR(14); for short SL = Entry + 1.5*ATR(14), TP = Entry - 2.0*ATR(14). Applied to the daily timeframe.

reformulated_task:
Daily strategy for XAUUSD (1 instrument, D1) based on price action: buy on a retest of broken resistance (former) in an uptrend, with a tight SL and four partial TPs.

Trend and channel conditions:
- Uptrend: Close > SMA(50) and SMA(50) today > SMA(50) 5 days ago.
- Within an ascending structure: the last two pivot-low (window 3 bars) are rising.

Level definition (former resistance → support):
- Look for pivot-high (window 3 bars) as local resistance.
- Confirmed breakout: daily close above pivot-high by at least 0.25*ATR(14). Level S = this pivot-high.

Confirmation of a retest of the demand zone:
- Within 1–10 days after the breakout, the day’s Low touches level S with tolerance ≤ 0.25*ATR(14).
- On the retest bar, a bullish price action signal forms: one of
  a) hammer: lower wick ≥ 1.5*body and Close > Open; or
  b) bullish engulfing: Close_t > High_{t-1} and Open_t ≤ Close_{t-1}; or
  c) long lower wick at the level: Low ≤ S and Close > Open.

Entry (Buy):
- At the open of the next day after the signal, only if Open ≤ S + 0.5*ATR(14). If the open is above S + 0.5*ATR(14) or there was no retest within 10 days, the entry is skipped (analog of a “breakout without pullback”).

Stop-loss (tight, does not move):
- SL = S − 0.5*ATR(14), not moved to breakeven.

Take-profits (partial exits):
- Define R = Entry − SL.
- TP1 = Entry + 2R (close 25% of the position)
- TP2 = Entry + 4R (close 25%)
- TP3 = Entry + 6R (close 25%)
- TP4 = Entry + 8R (close the remaining 25%)

Risk management:
- Position size is such that (Entry − SL) * quantity ≤ 1% of current equity. Only one position at a time.

Volatility filter (instead of news):
- Entry is allowed only if ATR(14)/Close ≤ 2.5% and (High − Low)/Close ≤ 3.5% on the signal day.

Implementation notes in Backtrader:
- Only SMA(50), ATR(14), and OHLC are used. Candlestick patterns are computed from prices; level S is via pivot-high; swing structure is via pivot-low. Partial take-profits are implemented with four limit orders for 25% of the volume and one common SL.

reformulated_task: Breakout strategy on a single asset (QQQ) with a Donchian proxy via Stochastic: use Stochastic %K with a period of 260 (no smoothing) as a replacement for the Donchian channel. Entry (Long): open a long position when %K crosses above level 85 from below (the first time the condition %K > 85 is met). Exit: close the position when %K drops below 1 (approaching a new 260-day low). Capital: $100,000, position size — 100% of available capital per trade. Data: QQQ, 2015-01-01 — 2017-12-31. Include commissions and slippage if available. Report: total and annual return, max drawdown, Sharpe, number of trades.
description: Breakout strategy on a single asset (QQQ) with a Donchian proxy via Stochastic: use Stochastic %K with a period of 260 (no smoothing) as a replacement for the Donchian channel. Entry (Long): open a long position when %K crosses above level 85 from below (the first time the condition %K > 85 is met). Exit: close the position when %K drops below 1 (approaching a new 260-day low). Capital: $100,000, position size — 100% of available capital per trade. Data: QQQ, 2015-01-01 — 2017-12-31. Include commissions and slippage if available. Report: total and annual return, max drawdown, Sharpe, number of trades.

reformulated_task:
Title: Spot Wheel (proxy) on a single asset via Bollinger+ATR  
Idea: Imitation of the “wheel” strategy without options: entry “like CSP” — buying the dip in a high-volatility uptrend and partial profit-taking at the middle (analog of a 50% premium decay), then holding the remainder with a target/time-out “like CC”.  
Indicators: SMA(200) for trend; Bollinger Bands(20, 2) and their width W = (upper-lower)/middle as an IV proxy; ATR(14) for stops; CrossOver for signals. Additional metric: percentile of Bollinger width over 500 bars (IV-like filter), average dollar volume over 20 bars.  
Filters:  
- Trend: close > SMA(200)  
- Volatility: percentile of W over 500 bars > 70  
- Price: 50 <= close <= 500  
- Liquidity: 20-bar average (volume * close) > 50,000,000  
Signal “entry like CSP (long)”:  
- Trigger condition: when close crosses from below to above the lower Bollinger band (CrossOver(close, bb.bot) == 1) AND all filters are met; enter with a limit ~ bb.bot (or at market on the signal bar).  
Position size and risk:  
- Maximum 15% of capital in the position.  
- Risk per trade: 1.5% of capital. Stop = min(entry*0.90, entry - 1.5*ATR). Size = min(0.15*equity/entry, risk_dollars / (entry - stop)).  
Management “CSP phase” (taking profit “like 50% premium”):  
- Upon reaching the Bollinger middle line (bb.mid), take profit on 50% of the position and move the stop on the remaining portion to breakeven (entry).  
Transition to “CC phase” (holding the remainder):  
- Take-profit on the remainder: the upper band bb.top or if price has risen >= 10% from entry (analog of the stop-loss for CC at +10% to the strike).  
- Time-out: close the remainder 35 bars after partial profit-taking (analog of 30–45 days to expiration) if the target has not been reached.  
Stops:  
- Initial stop for the whole position: as specified above.  
- After partial profit-taking: stop for the remainder at breakeven; optionally trailing stop = entry + 0.5*ATR, trail by ATR as price rises.  
Cycle repeat:  
- After a full exit, conditions are re-evaluated; the strategy repeats the “CSP entry” and subsequent “CC management” when filters are met.  
Parameters (default, adjustable): sma_len=200, bb_len=20, bb_dev=2, atr_len=14, volp_lookback=500, volp_thresh=70, risk_per_trade=0.015, max_alloc=0.15, cc_timeout=35, cc_overrun=0.10.

reformulated_task: Trend-following strategy for a single asset with a trend-strength filter via Bollinger Bands width and direction via MACD. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), SMA(50) of the band width, MACD(12, 26, 9), CrossOver. Activation filter (analog of ADX>25): band width (upper − lower) > SMA(50) of this width. Long entry: with the filter active, CrossOver(MACD line, MACD signal) upward. Short entry: with the filter active, CrossOver(MACD line, MACD signal) downward. Exit from long: reverse MACD cross (line below the signal). Exit from short: reverse MACD cross (line above the signal). If the filter is not active (width ≤ SMA of width), the strategy does not trade. Position size: 100% of available capital per trade.

reformulated_task: Intraday “credit spread proxy” strategy on a single asset (e.g., SPY or an available ticker) with a trend filter and small profit-taking. Rules: 1) Trade only after 90 minutes from the market open and close all positions 15–30 minutes before the close. 2) Determine the trend on intraday data: bullish if Close > SMA(20) and SMA(20) is above its value 30 bars ago; bearish if Close < SMA(20) and SMA(20) is below its value 30 bars ago; otherwise — sideways (do not trade). 3) In an uptrend, enter Long when touching/breaking the lower Bollinger Bands(20, 2) followed by the price returning inside the bands (CrossOver of price upward through the lower band). 4) In a downtrend, enter Short when touching/breaking the upper Bollinger Bands(20, 2) with the price returning inside the bands (CrossOver of price downward through the upper band). 5) Take-profit — small: min(0.25*ATR(14), distance to the middle Bollinger line), which imitates “collecting a small premium.” 6) Stop-loss — far: 1.25*ATR(14) from entry or the opposite Bollinger band (whichever is farther), so that the risk/reward ratio is unfavorable (about 5:1) and the probability of hitting the small take — high. 7) One asset and only one open position at a time. 8) Risk management: position size such that the potential loss at the stop does not exceed 1% of capital (initial capital $4000); if it is impossible to comply with the risk — skip the signal. 9) Filters: do not trade if since the day’s open a sideways market has formed (trend condition not met) or under extremely high volatility (e.g., ATR(14)/Close > 2%). Data: OHLCV. Backtrader indicators: SMA, BollingerBands, ATR, CrossOver.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Fixed date-based tranches on a single asset with performance evaluation via TWR/CAGR”. On one instrument (e.g., AAPL), a schedule of buys and sells with fixed amounts is executed on user-specified calendar dates (buy/sell at the opening price of the corresponding bar). Any number of tranches with arbitrary dates and amounts is allowed. Risk management: fixed size of each tranche, without stop-losses and take-profits. At the end, time-sensitive portfolio metrics are calculated and displayed: time-weighted return (TWR) and annual CAGR, taking the actual cash flow dates as a proxy for IRR. Additionally (optional, if no schedule is provided): generate entry/exit dates based on CrossOver SMA(20)/SMA(50) signals with closing on the reverse crossover. Only standard OHLCV fields and available Backtrader indicators are used.

reformulated_task: Develop a mean reversion strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) in Backtrader, using the z-score of the price deviation from the Bollinger Bands mean. Algorithm: 1) Data source: one ticker with OHLCV for 3–5 years. 2) Indicators: BollingerBands(period=lookback, devfactor=2), EMA(z, period=half_life). Compute the z-score as z = 2 * (Close - BB.mid) / (BB.top - BB.mid); when the bands are very narrow (almost zero width), ignore signals. 3) Entry: long when z < -entry_threshold; short when z > entry_threshold (if there is no position). 4) Exit: close the position on a return to the mean — when the smoothed EMA(z, half_life) crosses 0 (for long — from below to above, for short — from above to below). 5) Stop-loss: for long — close if z >= stoploss_threshold; for short — if z <= -stoploss_threshold, where stoploss_threshold = entry_threshold * stoploss_factor. 6) Position size: fixed (e.g., 100% of available capital); optionally — volatility scaling via ATR (optional). 7) Optimization: iterate over parameters lookback, half_life, entry_threshold, stoploss_factor; objective — maximize the Sharpe ratio based on backtest results.

reformulated_task:
NXXT Base Breakout Strategy (single asset, OHLCV, standard indicators)

Idea: Trade a breakout after a “constructive base”: price consolidates in a narrow range, forms a higher low, and breaks upward on increased volume with momentum confirmation from MACD/RSI.

Instruments and parameters:
- Ticker: NXXT (single OHLCV stream)
- SMA(200) — trend filter (bullish regime if Close > SMA200)
- BollingerBands(20, 2) — base/squeeze detection
- ATR(14) — volatility and stop-loss estimation
- MACD(12,26,9) — momentum (histogram)
- RSI(14) — trend confirmation
- Volume SMA(20) — average volume

Definitions:
- Base (consolidation):
  - Bollinger band width bb_width = (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid < 0.08
  - 20-bar range (HH20 - LL20) < 2.5 * ATR(14)
- Higher low: current Low(5) > Low(5) 5 bars ago (5-bar rolling minimum is rising)
- Base levels: base_high = maximum over 20 bars; base_low = minimum over 20 bars; base_height = base_high - base_low

Entry conditions (long):
1) Trend: Close > SMA(200)
2) Consolidation: bb_width < 0.08 and (HH20 - LL20) < 2.5*ATR(14)
3) “Higher low”: RollingLow(5) now > RollingLow(5) 5 bars ago
4) Momentum: MACD histogram crosses 0 from below OR rises for the last 3 bars; RSI(14) > 50
5) Volume: Volume today > 1.5 * SMA(Volume, 20)
6) Trigger: Upside breakout — enter via stop order when Price >= base_high + 0.1*ATR(14)

Stop-loss and take-profits:
- Initial SL: base_low - 1*ATR(14)
- TP1 (intermediate): Entry + base_height (close 50% of the position and move SL up to base_high)
- TP2 (main): Entry + 2*base_height (close the remainder)
- Fail-safe exit: if after entry Close < SMA(20) and MACD histogram < 0 — close the position early
- Time exit: close the position after 30 trading days if TP2 has not been reached

Risk and position sizing:
- Risk per trade: 0.5% of capital (calculated based on the Entry–SL distance)
- Exposure limit: no more than 2% of capital in the position
- Final position size = min(size based on 0.5% risk, size based on 2% of capital)
- Maximum open positions: 1

Implementation notes in Backtrader:
- Use standard indicators: SMA, BollingerBands, ATR, MACD, RSI; SMA(Volume, 20) as average volume
- Define bb_width and base_high/base_low via standard Bollinger band lines and Highest/Lowest over 20 bars (can use bt.ind.Highest/Lowest or custom logic)
- Entry — stop order above base_high; volume and momentum checks are performed on the breakout bar

reformulated_task: Strategy for a single asset on the daily timeframe (D1), using EMA(20) on close as a proxy for VWAP and a volume filter via SMA(volume,20). Long entry — when Close crosses EMA(20) from below upward and current volume > SMA(volume,20). Short entry — when Close crosses EMA(20) from above downward and volume > SMA(volume,20). Long exit — on the reverse cross of EMA(20) or stop-loss 1.5%/take-profit 3% from the entry price; short exit — on the reverse cross or the same stop/profit. Use the CrossOver indicator for signals. Position size — 100% of capital per trade, only one position at a time. Trade only after accumulating ≥20 daily bars for correct calculation of EMA and volume SMA.
description: Strategy for a single asset on the daily timeframe (D1), using EMA(20) on close as a proxy for VWAP and a volume filter via SMA(volume,20). Long exit — on the reverse cross of EMA(20) or stop-loss 1.5%/take-profit 3% from the entry price; short exit — on the reverse cross or the same stop/profit. Use the CrossOver indicator for signals. reformulated_task: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset with a dynamic volatility-based threshold. We use BollingerBands(30, 2) to estimate the Z-score of the price deviation from the mean: z = (close − mid) / std, where std = (upper − mid)/2. Volatility and its “forecast” are replaced with ATR(14) and EMA(ATR, 50): vr = ATR / EMA(ATR). Dynamic threshold: T = base_threshold * clip(vr, 0.7, 1.6), where base_threshold = 2.0. Entries: long position when z < −T (optional filter RSI(14) < 45 and volume > SMA(volume, 20)); short when z > +T (optional RSI(14) > 55 and volume > SMA(volume, 20)). Exit: close when z crosses through 0 (equivalent to the price crossing the Bollinger midline), stop-loss 1.5×ATR(14) from the entry price, time-stop 7 bars. Position sizing: risk per trade 1–2% of capital, quantity = risk / (1.5×ATR).

reformulated_task: Breakout strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) using the upper Bollinger Bands line as an approximation of the 75th percentile: calculate Bollinger Bands(period=10, devfactor=0.674) on closes; open a long position when the close crosses above the upper band (CrossOver(close, BB.top) > 0); exit the position when the close falls below the upper band (CrossOver(close, BB.top) < 0 or close < BB.top). Position size — 100% of capital, with no stop-losses or take-profits. The indicator starts working from the 10th bar.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Theta Proxy on a Single Asset”: mean reversion capturing the return to the mean during volatility compression. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), EMA(ATR, 14), RSI(14), CrossOver. Inputs (single asset, OHLCV): trade counter-trend at the Bollinger boundaries when there are signs of volatility compression. Filters: (1) ATR(14) < EMA(ATR,14) for the last 2 bars (or CrossOver(EMA(ATR), ATR) = 1 in the last 3 bars), (2) avoid “news” volatility: do not enter if BB width (top-bottom) > 1.5 × SMA(20) of the width or ATR(14)/Close > 4%, (3) do not open a new position if < 24 bars have passed since the last entry. Signals: • Long: Close ≤ lower BB band and RSI(14) < 35 with filters satisfied. • Short: Close ≥ upper BB band and RSI(14) > 65 with filters satisfied. Position sizing: risk per trade 1–2% of capital; size = floor((Equity × 0.015) / UnitRisk), where UnitRisk = max(ATR(14), 0.5 × |Close − SMA(20)|); pyramiding up to 3 units is allowed on further deviation by 0.5 × ATR while ATR is still declining and filters are satisfied. Exits: • Time-based: close the position on the 3rd bar after entry (or earlier upon reaching target/stop). • “Theta” (analog): target at 70% of the way to SMA(20) from the entry price: TP = Entry ± 0.7 × |Entry − SMA(20)| (sign toward SMA). • Loss-based: SL at 1.5 × UnitRisk from entry (analog of 150% premium). • Volatility-based: close the position if ATR(14) crosses above EMA(ATR,14) (CrossOver(ATR, EMA(ATR)) = 1) or if the price closes outside the BB band by > 0.5 × ATR (breakout sign). Exposure management: no more than 3 simultaneous positions (pyramiding in the same direction); new entries are prohibited for 24 bars after the last opening.

reformulated_task: Micro-momentum strategy on 1-minute OHLCV data (AAPL) with an imbalance proxy via volume and short-term trend. Indicators: EMA(5), EMA(10), RSI(7), SMA(20) on volume, ATR(14), CrossOver. Entries: Long — when EMA(5) crosses EMA(10) from below to above (CrossOver), RSI(7) > 55 and volume > SMA(20) on volume; Short — when EMA(5) crosses EMA(10) from above to below, RSI(7) < 45 and volume > SMA(20) on volume. Liquidity/volatility filters: trade only if ATR(14)/Close > 0.001 (0.10%) and volume > SMA(20) on volume. Exits: take-profit = +0.15*ATR(14) from the entry price; stop-loss = −0.10*ATR(14); time exit — close the position after 3 bars if TP/SL have not triggered. Risk management: position size = 0.1% of capital per trade; at most 1 open position at a time; assume fixed slippage of 0.05% per trade in the backtest settings; no overnight holds — close all positions 5 minutes before the session close. Backtest: 1-minute data for 6 months; compare with buy-and-hold; metrics: Sharpe, max drawdown, win rate, profit factor.
description: Micro-momentum strategy on 1-minute OHLCV data (AAPL) with an imbalance proxy via volume and short-term trend. Indicators: EMA(5), EMA(10), RSI(7), SMA(20) on volume, ATR(14), CrossOver. Entries: Long — when EMA(5) crosses EMA(10) from below to above (CrossOver), RSI(7) > 55 and volume > SMA(20) on volume; Short — when EMA(5) crosses EMA(10) from above to below, RSI(7) < 45 and volume > SMA(20) on volume. Liquidity/volatility filters: trade only if ATR(14)/Close > 0.001 (0.10%) and volume > SMA(20) on volume. Exits: take-profit = +0.15*ATR(14) from the entry price; stop-loss = −0.10*ATR(14); time exit — close the position after 3 bars if TP/SL have not triggered. Risk management: position size = 0.1% of capital per trade; at most 1 open position at a time; assume fixed slippage of 0.05% per trade in the backtest settings; no overnight holds — close all positions 5 minutes before the session close. Backtest: 1-minute data for 6 months; compare with buy-and-hold; metrics: Sharpe, max drawdown, win rate, profit factor.

reformulated_task: Low-volatility strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL): each month we enter a long position when current volatility is low relative to its own annual history. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2) and ATR(14) as a proxy for standard deviation, SMA(volume, 252) as a liquidity filter. Rules: 1) On the last trading day of the month, we calculate the relative Bollinger Band width width = (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid and its percentile over 252 days; if the current width is in the bottom 20% (≤ 20th percentile) and simultaneously ATR(14) < SMA(ATR(14), 252), and SMA(volume, 252) ≥ 1,000,000 (shares per day), then at the beginning of the next month we open a long position sized at 100% of capital. 2) At the end of each month we rebalance: if the conditions are no longer met — we close the position and remain in cash; if they are met — we keep/reopen the long for the next month. Data requirement: do not trade until there are at least 126 daily bars.

reformulated_task: Hedge overlay on a single asset (e.g., AAPL), simulating the purchase of put options via a partial short position. Indicator: RSI(14). Entry: open a short position when RSI(14) > 70. Exit: close the short position when RSI(14) < 50. Position size: 10% of the current portfolio value, calculated as floor(0.10 * value / close). Rules: single entry (do not open a new hedge until the current one is closed); after exit, a new entry is possible only after RSI rises above 70 again; stop-loss and take-profit are not used.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Single-Asset Volatility Rotation” for a single asset: maintain a base long position and perform partial rotation (±5% of the portfolio) at extremes. Indicators: Bollinger Bands(20, 2), ATR(14) and SMA(ATR, 30). Volatility filter: trade only if ATR(14) > 0.5 * SMA(ATR(14), 30). Entry (tactical sell of 5%): when the closing price touches/breaks the upper Bollinger band and the ATR filter is met, while the cooldown is observed. Record the “anchor high” = the high of the entry bar and the sell price. Exit (buy back 5%): 1) mean-reversion — if the price falls by 5% from the anchor high, buy back 5% and complete the cycle; 2) protection in case the trend continues — if the price rises by 3% from the sell price before −5% is reached, immediately buy back 5% (cancel the reduction). After completing the cycle — cooldown of 30 trading days. Trade size is fixed: each action is 5% of the portfolio value (or less if the current position is smaller).

reformulated_task: Dynamic single-asset strategy (e.g., AAPL) with CVaR proxy control: positioning is determined by momentum, and position size is capped by an estimate of tail risk via ATR. Expected return signal: bullish regime when EMA(20) > EMA(100) and MACD > 0; bearish — otherwise. Risk proxy: ES95 ≈ c * ATR(14) / Close, where c = 2.0. Risk threshold r = 0.02 (2% per period). Position sizing rule: if momentum is negative — w = 0; if positive — w = min(1, r / (c * ATR/Close)). Rebalancing at the beginning of each period (daily by default, optionally weekly/monthly). Exit — upon a change in the momentum regime or at the next rebalancing. Additionally: liquidity filter via volume SMA (e.g., trade only if SMA(20) on volume is above a specified minimum). Backtrader indicators used: EMA, MACD, ATR, CrossOver.

reformulated_task:
Hybrid strategy for a single asset (Backtrader): permanent core + Wheel-like overlay without options.

Indicators:
- SMA_50 and SMA_200 to determine the trend.
- ATR_14 to assess volatility and stops.
- CrossOver to confirm entry.

Portfolio logic (ETF-core replacement):
- Hold a permanent base core: long 50% of capital in the single asset (rebalance periodically, no stops; the core mimics the long-term “ETF” part).
- Active overlay (imitation of selling put/call): short-term trades on top of the core with fixed risk.

Overlay — “put proxy” (buying the dip in an uptrend):
- Trend condition: SMA_50 ≥ SMA_200.
- Accumulation zone: level L = SMA_50 − 1×ATR_14.
- Entry: CrossOver(Close, L) upward after touching/breaking L.
- Position size: 5% of capital per signal; maximum 5 active overlay longs (total ≤ 25% of capital), total exposure ≤ 75% (core 50% + overlay ≤ 25%).
- Take-profit (imitation of “+50% premium”): close the overlay upon reaching price Entry + 0.5×ATR_14 (fixed at entry) or upon price returning to SMA_50 + 0.5×ATR_14 (whichever comes first).
- Stop-loss (anti-catastrophe): close the overlay when Close ≤ min(Entry − 1.5×ATR_14, Entry × 0.90). Time-stop: close after 10 bars if TP/SL did not trigger.
- “Under-acquisition block” (analog of refusal of assignment below −10%): do not open new overlay longs if Close < 0.9 × SMA_50; wait for price to return to the corridor (≥ 0.9 × SMA_50) and a new CrossOver.

Overlay — profit-taking on a rally (“covered-call” proxy):
- If there are overlay longs and Close ≥ U = SMA_50 + 1×ATR_14, close 50–100% of the overlay (at discretion) to imitate “call expiration/selling with capped upside.” Do not open new long overlays while price is above SMA_50.

Risk management and constraints:
- Per overlay trade, risk and capital ≤ 5%.
- No averaging against the trend; only long overlays simultaneously in an uptrend. In a downtrend (SMA_50 < SMA_200), new overlay entries are forbidden; keep only the 50% core.
- Emergency mode: if SMA_50 < SMA_200 and Close < SMA_200 × 0.9, close all active overlay positions, leave the 50% core unchanged (resume the overlay only after SMA_50 ≥ SMA_200 is restored).

reformulated_task: Single-asset strategy 'RRG‑proxy Momentum' for Backtrader (e.g., AAPL). Idea: trade only when the asset shows leadership (a sustained up-trend) and momentum acceleration — a proxy for the RRG 'Leaders' quadrant. Indicators: SMA(50), SMA(200), MACD(12,26,9), RSI(14), ATR(14), CrossOver. Entry conditions (Long): 1) Leadership (RS‑proxy): close > SMA(200) and SMA(50) > SMA(200). 2) Momentum (Velocity‑proxy): MACD histogram > 0. 3) Acceleration trigger: an upward crossover of the MACD line and signal line (CrossOver(macd.macd, macd.signal) > 0) OR an upward crossover of RSI(14) above level 50. Exit conditions: 1) Momentum weakening: MACD histogram < 0 while maintaining close > SMA(200). 2) Trend reversal: close < SMA(200) OR SMA(50) < SMA(200). 3) Take-profit (for the short-term version): fixed +7% from the entry price OR when RSI > 70. Risk management and position sizing: stop-loss = max(2% of the entry price, 2*ATR(14)); position size = min(10% of capital, capital*1% risk / (stop per share)), where per-trade risk is 1% of capital by default; optional liquidity filter: volume > SMA(volume, 20). All thresholds are parameterized: stop_pct [0.02..0.05], take_pct [0.05..0.10] or disable via use_tp=False.

reformulated_task: Backtrader strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL): periodic rebalancing of exposure to a precomputed target weight. Input: a schedule of target weights w_t in the range [0, 1] (e.g., from external optimization), rebalancing period — a parameter (quarterly or yearly). Logic: on each rebalancing date, set the position in the single asset equal to w_t of the current portfolio value, using the current market price to compute the number of shares; if w_t = 0 — fully exit to cash; between rebalancings do not trade. Data: standard OHLCV; the strategy starts from the first available date after valid prices appear and the first rebalance date. Capital management: full reinvestment, reallocation on rebalance, commissions 0.1% per trade, slippage 0.05% of price. Indicators are not required (it is acceptable to use SMA/EMA only for technical initialization, if needed).

reformulated_task: Momentum strategy on a single asset with volume confirmation and dynamic EMA levels. Indicators: EMA(9/21/50/200) on close; BollingerBands(20, 2); MACD(12, 26, 9); ATR(14); Volume SMA(20) for Relative Volume (RVOL = volume / SMA(volume, 20)). Filters: 1) Price in the range 0.75–10.00; 2) RVOL >= 1.5; 3) Trend: close > EMA9 > EMA21 > EMA50 and close > EMA200. Entry (long), one of two scenarios: A) Breakout with confirmation: CrossOver(close, upper Bollinger band) > 0 and CrossOver(MACD.macd, MACD.signal) > 0 on the current bar or one of the previous 2 bars, with RVOL >= 1.5; B) Sniper-bounce: within the last 3 bars there was a pullback touch to EMA21 or the middle Bollinger band (low <= EMA21 or close <= middle band), then CrossOver(close, EMA9) > 0, and the distance to the upper Bollinger band <= 0.5*ATR (the goal is to enter before the breakout), with RVOL >= 1.5. Stop-loss: initial SL = min(EMA21 price, entry - max(0.02, ATR(14))). Alternative indicator-based exit: CrossOver(EMA21, close) > 0 (i.e., close crosses below EMA21) or CrossOver(MACD.signal, MACD.macd) > 0 (bearish cross). Take-profit: TP = entry + clamp(ATR(14), 0.10, 0.20). Trade management: when unrealized profit reaches >= 0.10, move SL to breakeven (or to EMA9 if it is higher), then a trailing exit on a close below EMA9. Risk management: do not open a new position if there is already an open one; calculate position size from risk R per trade (e.g., 1% of capital) via R / (entry - SL).

reformulated_task: Strategy name: Single-asset regime strategy based on SMA(200). Idea: trade one asset (e.g., AAPL) only in “bullish” regimes determined by its own price relative to SMA(200). Indicators: SMA(200) on close; optionally CrossOver(price, SMA(200)) to detect regime changes. Entry (buy): once a week (at Friday close) — if close > SMA(200) and there is no position, buy the asset with 100% of available cash. Exit (sell): once a week — if close < SMA(200) and a position is open, sell the entire amount and move to cash. Reinvestment: funds remain in cash until the next signal. Parameters: commission 0.1% per trade, starting capital $1,000,000. Logging: record entries/exits, order execution, portfolio value, and current position.

reformulated_task:
Strategy “Capitulation + short squeeze” on a single asset using only OHLCV and basic Backtrader indicators. Idea: look for strong oversold conditions with anomalous volume (capitulation), enter after a reversal signal, and exit on momentum/key moving-average levels.

Indicators (on one timeframe, e.g., daily or hourly):
- Bollinger Bands(periode=126, devfactor=2) on the close price as a proxy for “6-month VWAP ±SD”.
- RSI(14) to determine oversold conditions.
- EMA(200) as long-term trend/resistance.
- ATR(14) for stops and a volatility filter.
- SMA(volume, 20) to assess volume abnormality.
- CrossOver to detect breakouts/returns into the range.

Long entry conditions (main scenario — “capitulation + bounce”):
1) Oversold: close < lower Bollinger(126, 2) band AND RSI(14) < 30.
2) Anomalous volume: volume >= 1.8 × SMA(volume, 20).
3) Reversal confirmation: on the next candle CrossOver(close, lower BB band) upward OR a bullish candle with range (high - low) >= 1.2 × ATR(14) and a close above the open.

Alternative entry (after “breakout and hold”):
- CrossOver(close, max(BB midline, EMA(200))) upward AND volume >= 1.5 × SMA(volume, 20) AND RSI(14) > 50.

Stop-loss:
- For the “capitulation” entry: stop below the local low of the signal candle by 1.0 × ATR(14) (min(low of the last 3-5 bars) - 1×ATR).
- For the “breakout” entry: stop at max(BB midline, EMA(200)) or 1.0 × ATR(14) below the entry price — whichever is closer.

Exit from the position (scaled out):
- TP1: close 50% at the Bollinger midline (BB midline). Move the stop to breakeven.
- TP2: close 30% at the upper Bollinger band.
- TP3: close the remaining 20% upon reaching the upper BB band + 0.5 × ATR(14) or via an ATR trailing stop (stop = max(stop, close - 2 × ATR(14))).
- Alternative full exit: if close >= EMA(200) + 1.5 × ATR(14) (strong impulsive move), take profit on the entire position.

Conditions for a hedging short (symmetrical, optional):
- Overbought: close > upper BB(126, 2) band, RSI(14) > 70, volume >= 1.8 × SMA(volume, 20). Confirmation — CrossOver(upper BB band, close) downward or a bearish candle with range >= 1.2 × ATR(14).
- TP1: BB midline; TP2: lower BB band; trailing stop = close + 2 × ATR(14). Stop-loss above the high of the signal bar + 1 × ATR(14).

Additional filters/notes:
- Works on one asset and one timeframe (no multi-TF). “New 6-month low” is replaced with the condition of the price breaking below the lower BB(126, 2) band + a volume spike.
- You can add a volatility filter: do not enter if BB band width < the median over 126 bars (low volatility).

reformulated_task: Aggressive combined strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on OHLCV using only available Backtrader indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), EMA(50), ATR(14), CrossOver. Idea: combine short-term mean-reversion “scalps” around the mean and 2–5-day trend-following swing positions, with risk management via ATR and fixed risk per trade. Entries: 1) Scalp (mean reversion): after price moves outside the upper/lower Bollinger band, price closes back inside the bands; RSI in the 40–60 range; momentum confirmation — MACD.macd crosses its signal line toward the mean or the MACD histogram moves toward zero for two bars in a row. Long: CrossOver(close, bb.bot) after touching the lower band; short: CrossOver(bb.top, close) after touching the upper band. Exit: take-profit at bb.mid or at +1…1.5 ATR; stop: 1 ATR beyond the local extreme; time stop 3–5 bars. 2) Swing (trend-following): trend filter — price > EMA(50) and EMA(50) is rising (for longs; for shorts symmetrically). Long entry: MACD.macd > MACD.signal and > 0; pullback of price to bb.mid (distance to bb.mid ≤ 0.5 ATR) with RSI 40–60 or RSI exiting oversold (>40). Short — mirrored with MACD.macd < MACD.signal and < 0. Volatility filter (instead of macro events): skip new entries if ATR(14)/close > 0.03. Swing exit: take-profit 2–3 ATR or a touch of the opposite Bollinger band; alternatively — a reverse MACD cross or a time stop 5–10 bars. Risk/sizing: risk per trade 1–2% of capital; position size = risk_per_trade / stop distance by ATR. Account for broker commissions and slippage in Backtrader.
