reformulated_task: Strategy “short volatility proxy around pseudo‑earnings” on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) in Backtrader using only OHLCV and indicators SMA, EMA, RSI, BollingerBands, ATR, CrossOver. Idea: extract profit from volatility “crash” after a sharp price event. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2) with band width BBW = (upper - lower)/mid; EMA(BBW,100) as the baseline level; ATR(14); EMA(ATR,100); RSI(14); SMA(volume,20). Entry (simulation of the 21–5 days pre‑event window): maintain a counter bars_since_vol_spike after the first crossing of BBW > 1.5*EMA(BBW,100) and ATR > 1.2*EMA(ATR,100) with volume volume > 1.2*SMA(volume,20); entry is allowed if 5 <= bars_since_vol_spike <= 21. Direction: short if close > the upper Bollinger band and RSI > 60; long if close < the lower band and RSI < 40. Exit (simulation of the “day after earnings”): if during the position an “event” occurs — an opening gap abs(open - close[-1]) > 1.5*ATR and volume > 1.5*SMA(volume,20) and ATR > 1.3*EMA(ATR,100) — close at the next open. Alternative exit: close on reversion to the Bollinger middle band (mid), or on “volatility crash” BBW < EMA(BBW,100), or after 21 bars, whichever comes first. Risk management: stop‑loss 1.5*ATR from the entry price; position size such that risk per trade is 1–2% of equity (qty = risk_in_$ / (1.5*ATR)); optional ATR trailing. Liquidity filter: trade only if SMA(volume,20) is above a specified threshold. Commissions: account for a fixed per‑share commission per trade.

reformulated_task: Daily ORB-style strategy with two attempts and a retest via Bollinger Bands (only 1 instrument, D1 OHLCV). Indicators: EMA(20), EMA(50), BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14). Logic: 1) Trend filter: bullish regime if EMA(20) > EMA(50); bearish if EMA(20) < EMA(50). 2) First trade: enter 1 lot at market on the day’s open in the direction of the trend (long when EMA20>EMA50, short when EMA20<EMA50). Exit: take-profit +0.5% and stop-loss -0.3% from the entry price; within the daily bar, if TP and SL are reached simultaneously, assume the stop-loss triggers (conservatively). 3) Second trade (enabled only if the first closed by stop-loss): wait for a retest to the Bollinger midline and a bounce in the trend direction. For a long: during the next 3 trading days, the day’s low ≤ the BB middle band and the day’s close above the middle band; enter 1 lot on the next day’s open. For a short: the day’s high ≥ the BB middle band and the close below the middle; enter 1 lot on the next day’s open. 4) Second trade management: initial stop-loss at the prior day’s extreme (for a long — at the low, for a short — at the high). Take-profit is fixed at 0.6% from entry. Trailing-stop is active only for the second trade with distance trailamount = 1.0 * ATR(14) (calculated on the previous bar); if TP and the trail are hit simultaneously, assume the trailing triggers first (conservatively). 5) Position size: fixed 1 lot. 6) Frequency constraints: no more than one trade per day; maximum two attempts (the first + one re-entry) per trading episode within up to 3 days. 7) Timeframe: D1; rules about trading until 12:00 are replaced by daily proxies (retest/bounce relative to the Bollinger middle band).

reformulated_task: Trend-following strategy on a single asset with a “pseudo-forecast” of the trend based on an ensemble of indicators: entries/exits are determined by the total scoring of signals from MACD(12,26,9), SMA/EMA, RSI(14), Stochastic(14,3,3), and Bollinger Bands(20,2). Rules: compute the trend score (+1/-1 for each criterion) — (1) MACD histogram > 0 → +1, < 0 → -1; (2) SMA(20) > SMA(50) and the slopes of EMA(10) and SMA(20) are rising (EMA(10) > EMA(10)[1], SMA(20) > SMA(20)[1]) → +1; SMA(20) < SMA(50) and both slopes are falling → -1; (3) RSI > 55 → +1, RSI < 45 → -1; (4) Price above the middle Bollinger band and band width is increasing (upper-lower today > yesterday) → +1; below the middle and width is increasing → -1; (5) Stochastic %K and %D crossover: CrossOver(%K, %D) upward and %K > 20 → +1; downward and %K < 80 → -1. Sum the points: if score ≥ 2 and there is no position/short position — open/flip to long; if score ≤ -2 and there is no position/long position — open/flip to short. Exit — only on the opposite signal (no stops/take-profits). Position size — 100% of capital. Data — OHLCV only; indicators — standard Backtrader: SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, BollingerBands, Stochastic, ATR (ATR optional for a low-volatility filter).

reformulated_task: Synthetic covered calls for a staged exit from a large position without options. Data: one asset with OHLCV. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), ATR(14), EMA(ATR%,50), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), SMA(volume,20). Parameters: strike=150, annual sales limit=270000 USD, lot=100 shares. Logic: 1) At the beginning of each month, calculate the available monthly lots to sell: lots_month = min(floor((remaining_limit_usd)/(strike*100)), floor(position/100)). 2) During the month, sell no more than lots_month lots with market orders on the next bar when the sell trigger is met: (a) breakout of the target price zone: high >= 150 or close >= upper BB band; (b) volatility is elevated: ATR% = 100*ATR/close > EMA(ATR%,50); (c) liquidity: volume >= SMA(volume,20). 3) If no sales occurred by the end of the month and on the last bar of the month close >= 150, sell the remaining lots_month lots on the next bar (analogous to option exercise at expiration). 4) If a strong bullish impulse is observed on the signal (RSI(14) > 75 and the MACD histogram > 0 and rising), the sale can be skipped in this month (analogous to early option buyback), moving the lots to the next cycle. 5) Carry unused lots over to the next month, but the total sold volume in dollars for the calendar year does not exceed 270000 USD. 6) Sales stop when the limit is exhausted or if the available position < 100 shares.

reformulated_task:
Breakout momentum on a single asset with a proxy for “thin float” via relative volume and a volatility squeeze.

Indicators:
- Trend: SMA(200), EMA(20), EMA(50)
- Volatility/channel: BollingerBands(20, 2), BB width = (upper - lower) / middle
- Volume: SMA(volume, 20) for RVOL = volume / SMA(volume, 20)
- Momentum: RSI(14)
- Risk/stops: ATR(14)

Entry conditions (Long):
1) Uptrend: close > SMA(200) and EMA(20) > EMA(50)
2) Squeeze (supply shortage): current BB width < 0.8 * SMA(BB width, 100)
3) Volume spike: RVOL >= 5 (current volume >= 5 * SMA(volume, 20))
4) Breakout: CrossOver(close, upper band BollingerBands(20,2)) > 0
5) Momentum: RSI(14) > 55

Exit conditions:
- Take-profit: when close >= entry + 2 * ATR(14)
- Weakening/breakdown signal: close < EMA(20)
- Trailing stop: exit if close <= (max close since entry) - 2 * ATR(14)
- Stop-loss: initial_stop = entry - 1.5 * ATR(14)

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade: 2% of capital. Size = floor(0.02 * equity / (entry - initial_stop))
- Additional liquidity filter: trade if SMA(volume, 20) is not below SMA(volume, 50) * 0.7

Notes on mapping the original concepts:
- “Thin float” and news are replaced by a combination of strong RVOL and a preceding Bollinger Bands squeeze
- The rising trendline and support are replaced by EMA(20)/EMA(50) and monitoring the breakout above the upper BB band

reformulated_task: Impulse Strategy 12-1 for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on daily data: use SMA(252) of the closing price as a proxy for annual momentum. Indicators: SMA(252); ATR% = SMA(ATR(14), 252) / Close. Entry (long): if Close > SMA(252) and the volatility filter passes (ATR% ≤ 0.05). Exit: if Close < SMA(252) or the stop-loss/take-profit is triggered. Risk management: position fixed at 10% of capital; stop-loss 2% from the entry price; take-profit 5% from the entry price. If ATR% > 0.05, do not open new positions until volatility decreases.

reformulated_task: Universal portfolio for a single asset (AAPL) as an allocation between the asset and cash. Daily rebalancing based on the wealth-weighted average across all constant fractions w∈{0.0, 0.1, ..., 1.0} over a 252-day window. Steps: (1) Calculate daily returns r_t = close_t/close_{t-1} - 1 for the last 252 days. (2) For each fraction w, simulate wealth(w) = Π_t (1 + w·r_t). (3) Obtain the target weight for the next day w_next = Σ_w [w·wealth(w)] / Σ_w [wealth(w)]. (4) Set the position in AAPL to w_next of capital (the rest is cash), recalculate and rebalance daily. Stop-losses and take-profits are not used; risk management only through the fraction w_next; if there is insufficient data (<252 days), do not trade.

reformulated_task: Swing strategy on a single instrument (e.g., AAPL, daily bars) with a trend filter and RSI pullback entry without external news. Indicators: EMA(12), EMA(26), RSI(14), ATR(14). Long entry: (1) EMA(12) > EMA(26); (2) RSI drops below 40, but not below 30; (3) after RSI crosses up level 35 — enter when the close is above the high of the candle on which RSI first closed above 35. Short entry symmetrically: EMA(12) < EMA(26); RSI rises above 60, but below 70; after RSI crosses down 65 — enter when the close is below the low of the candle on which RSI first closed below 65. Long exit: take-profit when RSI > 70; stop 1.5% below entry; move stop to breakeven at +1% profit. Short exit: take-profit when RSI < 30; stop 1.5% above entry; move to breakeven at +1% profit. Risk management: risk no more than 1% of capital per trade, position size = risk/stop distance; do not open new positions during a drawdown > 5% from the equity peak. Filters: (a) trade only if the daily range (high - low) ≥ 0.5% of the open price; (b) instead of a news filter — skip new entries if the current True Range > 2 × ATR(14) or the absolute gap |open - prev_close|/prev_close > 1% (emulation of “news” spikes).
description: Swing strategy on a single instrument (e.g., AAPL, daily bars) with a trend filter and RSI pullback entry without external news. Indicators: EMA(12), EMA(26), RSI(14), ATR(14). Risk management: risk no more than 1% of capital per trade, position size = risk/stop distance; do not open new positions during a drawdown > 5% from the equity peak. reformulated_task: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) using Bollinger Bands and RSI: trade price deviations from the long-term mean as an analog of a Z-spread. Indicators: BollingerBands(period=200, devfactor=2) for signals, BollingerBands(period=200, devfactor=3) for stop-loss, RSI(14) as a filter, ATR(14) for risk calculation. Long entry: close below the lower band of BB(200, 2σ) and RSI(14) < 35. Short entry: close above the upper band of BB(200, 2σ) and RSI(14) > 65. Exit on reversion to the mean: close the position when crossing the middle line of BB(200, 2σ) (SMA200). “Anti-meltdown” stop-loss: close the position when touching the corresponding BB(200, 3σ) band against the position. Risk management and position sizing: target position size — up to 100,000 kroner; actual size is limited by a 2% capital risk — the position is calculated as min(target size/price, (0.02 * capital) / max(distance to stop by BB(3σ), ATR(14))).

reformulated_task: Weekly MACD Crossover Momentum on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) in Backtrader. Signals are generated on the weekly timeframe (resample from daily). Indicators: MACD(12,26,9) with CrossOver, SMA(50) as a trend filter, ATR(14) for a volatility filter, SMA(volume,50) for relative volume. Entry: buy at the weekly close when MACD crosses the signal line from below upwards (bullish CrossOver > 0), the closing price is above SMA(50), weekly volume > 1.3 × SMA(volume,50), and volatility is moderate (ATR(14)/Close < 0.04). Position size: fixed stake=1. Exit: close the position on the reverse MACD cross down, or on a stop-loss of −8% from the entry price, or on a take-profit of +20%, or on a time stop after 6 weeks — do not hold the position “forever.”

reformulated_task:
**Title:** Trading Strategy Based on Implied Volatility ETF  
**Description:** Write a trading strategy that trades an ETF based on the implied volatility (IV) of its components. The core idea of the strategy is to calculate the IV of the entire ETF based on the IV of the assets included in it and open positions when there are significant discrepancies between the current and the calculated IV.  

**Indicators:**  
- **Implied Volatility (IV)** of the ETF components (use historical options price data for each component).  
- **Calculated IV of the ETF** — the weighted average IV of the components (weights are each component’s share in the ETF).  
- **Deviation of the current ETF IV from the calculated IV** (e.g., in percent).  

**Entry Conditions:**  
1. **Buy (long position):**  
   - The current ETF IV is below the calculated IV by **15% or more** (the ETF is undervalued in terms of volatility).  
   - The calculated IV is stable (does not change by more than **5%** over the last **5 minutes**).  

2. **Sell (short position):**  
   - The current ETF IV is above the calculated IV by **15% or more** (the ETF is overvalued in terms of volatility).  
   - The calculated IV is stable (does not change by more than **5%** over the last **5 minutes**).  

**Exit Conditions:**  
- **Close long position:**  
  - The current ETF IV approaches the calculated IV (difference less than **5%**).  
  - Or **30 minutes** after entry if the discrepancies have not corrected.  
- **Close short position:**  
  - The current ETF IV approaches the calculated IV (difference less than **5%**).  
  - Or **30 minutes** after entry if the discrepancies have not corrected.  

**Risk Management and Position Sizing:**  
- **Position size:** **10%** of the portfolio per trade.  
- **Stop-loss:** **2%** of the entry price (to protect against sharp moves).  
- **Take-profit:** **1%** of the entry price (lock in profit on small fluctuations).  

**Additional Rules:**  
- Trade only during periods of high liquidity (ETF trading volume above the daily average).  
- Exclude trades if market volatility (VIX) is above **30%** (high uncertainty).  
- Use a **moving average** of the calculated IV with a period of **10** to smooth short-term noise.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion on a single asset using Bollinger Bands (20, 1.5σ). Long entry: when the price crosses the lower Bollinger Band from top to bottom (divergence signal), with the following filters: ATR(14) is not in the bottom 20th percentile over the last 252 bars and the price is not above SMA(200). Short entry: when the price crosses the upper band from bottom to top, with the following filters: ATR(14) is not in the bottom 20th percentile over the last 252 bars and the price is not below SMA(200). Exit: when the middle Bollinger line (SMA(20)) is crossed in any direction or a time stop after 5 bars (whichever comes first). Risk management: fixed position size of $1000; close the position if the floating loss on the trade exceeds 2% of capital. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 1.5), SMA(200), ATR(14), CrossOver.

reformulated_task: Pseudo-protective put on a single asset (without options) using ATR. Assume the investor has an underlying long position; its purchase price base_price is passed as a parameter, and the size of the core position main_shares is also a parameter (for limit calculations). Activate the hedge when Close >= 3 * base_price (profit on the underlying position >= 200%) and volatility is low: ATR(14)/Close <= 0.04 (a proxy for “cheap premiums”). Open a short overlay on the same asset (modeled as a separate account/strategy that does not touch the underlying long) with size: qty = floor(0.02 * (main_shares * Close) / ATR(14)), which limits the maximum hedge risk to 2% of the value of the core position with a stop at 1*ATR. Hedge stop: price_stop = entry + 1*ATR(14); take-profit: close the hedge if the price drops by 15% from the entry level (Close <= entry * 0.85). The hedge “expiration” term is 90 bars (daily); upon expiry, close it. Review/roll: on each new breach of the price threshold base_price * (1 + 0.10 * k), where k = 1,2,... and the threshold has not been reached before, close the current hedge and open a new one under the same rules; for rolls, the “expiration” term is 180 bars. Opening/rolling is allowed only if ATR(14)/Close <= 0.04. Thus the ATM strike is replaced by a linkage to the current price (protection level and take-profit from entry), the premium is replaced by a capped stop risk up to 2% of the value of the underlying position, and expiration is replaced by a 90/180-bar timer. Data/indicators used: one asset (OHLCV), ATR(14).

reformulated_task:
**Assignment:**

Write a trading strategy for QBTS (D-Wave Quantum) stock based on a price range breakout confirmed by volume.

**Core idea:**
The strategy looks for a strong upward price breakout from the previous price range with a simultaneous sharp increase in volume. The goal is to catch the start of a new upward move.

**Indicators:**
1.  **Range:** Defined as the highest price over the last 20 trading sessions.
2.  **Volume:** Current trading volume.
3.  **Average volume:** Simple moving average (SMA) of volume over the last 20 sessions.

**Conditions for entering a long position (buy):**
Open a long position if all of the following conditions are met simultaneously:
1.  The opening price of the current candle exceeds the highest price over the last 20 sessions (range breakout).
2.  The current trading volume exceeds the average volume (20-period SMA) by more than 115% (i.e., volume > 2.15 * SMA volume).

**Conditions for exiting the position (sell):**
Close the long position when the price reaches the target level around $28.00. If the target level is not reached, the position must remain open until the end of the trading day.

**Risk management and position sizing:**
The initial request does not include risk management information. For backtesting, use a standard approach: fixed position size (e.g., 100% of the portfolio per trade).

**Additional rules:**
*   The strategy is intended for the daily timeframe (1D).
*   The entry signal is generated only once per day, based on the opening data of the first candle.
*   To determine the target level ($28.00), use a fixed value rather than a dynamic calculation.

reformulated_task: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset (e.g., TSLA) around fixed levels 445.00–450.00 with entries based on “false breakout” patterns and a return to the middle of the range. Volatility filter: trade only if ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 50). Call-selling analog (short): enter SHORT 2 units on a false breakout of resistance — if High > 450.00 and Close < 450.00, with overbought confirmation (RSI(14) > 60) and a touch of / being at the upper BollingerBands(20,2) band; stop-loss via a stop order at 450.25, take-profit upon reaching the 447.5 zone (or if Close <= 446.0, or a touch of the Bollinger middle line — whichever comes first). Put-selling analog (long): enter LONG 2 units on a bounce from support — if Low < 445.00 and Close > 445.00, with oversold confirmation (RSI(14) < 40) and a touch of / being at the lower Bollinger band; stop-loss at 444.75, take-profit upon reaching the 449.0–450.0 zone (e.g., High >= 449.5 or Close >= 449.0). Trade management: only one position at a time, no more than one new trade per day; close all positions by the end of the session (for daily bars — forced exit on the next close if neither TP nor SL has triggered). Additional: after a move into profit by 0.75×ATR(14), move up a trailing stop by 0.5×ATR(14).

reformulated_task:
Single-asset multi-strategy DMA bot (Backtrader, 1 data feed)

Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), EMA(10), EMA(30), ATR(14), CrossOver.

Subsystem logic:
1) Mean Reversion (BB):
   - Long: close < lower BB(20,2) band
   - Short: close > upper BB(20,2) band
   - Exit: cross of the BB middle band in the direction of the position (long — price above the middle, short — below)
2) Moving Average Crossover (EMA):
   - Long: CrossOver(EMA10, EMA30) > 0
   - Short: CrossOver(EMA10, EMA30) < 0
   - Exit: opposite EMA cross
3) Volatility Breakout (ATR):
   - Long: close > (prev. High + 1.5 * ATR14)
   - Short: close < (prev. Low - 1.5 * ATR14)
   - Exit: take-profit 2 * ATR14 or stop-loss 1 * ATR14 from the entry price

Signal aggregator (majority vote):
- Each subsystem forms a signal {-1, 0, +1}. Final signal = sign of the sum of the three signals.
- Entry: Long if sum >= +2, Short if sum <= -2; otherwise — skip/wait. If equal and there is an open position — hold until an exit triggers.

Unified exit/reversal rules:
- Close/reverse the position when the final signal changes to the opposite.
- Additionally account for the specific exits of the subsystem that generated the entry (BB middle, opposite EMA cross, ATR SL/TP for breakout). The first to trigger in time applies.

Risk management and position sizing:
- Price limits: trades are allowed only if min_price <= close <= max_price.
- Notional limit: position value <= nominal_cap.
- Position size: min( floor(risk_pct * equity / (ATR14)), floor(nominal_cap / close) ), at least 1 lot.
- Order frequency limit: cooldown_bars — minimum N bars between sending new orders (emulates QPS throttle).
- Commissions/slippage: fixed Backtrader broker parameters (single broker, no venue routing).

PnL and position accounting:
- Track total realized/unrealized PnL on a single account (built-in Backtrader metrics), without venue-level breakdown.

Default parameters: bb_period=20, bb_dev=2, ema_fast=10, ema_slow=30, atr_period=14, breakout_k=1.5, tp_k=2.0, sl_k=1.0, risk_pct=0.01, nominal_cap — to be specified, min_price/max_price — to be specified, cooldown_bars=5.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL), exploiting a close-to-close imbalance via a daily proxy. Long entry on the next bar after a day when: 1) close < lower band of BollingerBands(20, 2), 2) volume > 1.5 × SMA(volume, 20), 3) RSI(14) < 30, 4) liquidity filter: SMA(volume, 50) > 1,000,000. Exit: on a price cross above the middle Bollinger line (CrossOver(close, BB.mid)) or after 3 bars (whichever comes first). Risk management: no more than 100 shares per trade; daily loss limit of 0.5% of capital (once reached — new entries are forbidden until end of day); a new position is not opened if the current one is already open.

reformulated_task: A modular scalping strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) in Backtrader using only EMA/SMA/RSI/MACD/BollingerBands/ATR/CrossOver. It consists of two switchable modules and a set of filters. Module A (pseudo-“Alligator” on EMA): the trend is considered bullish when EMA(5) > EMA(8) > EMA(13) and the spreads (EMA5-EMA8, EMA8-EMA13) are increasing; Long entry — after a pullback of price to EMA(5) (low <= EMA5) and a breakout above the high of the pullback bar, with RSI(14) < 70 and MACD(12,26,9) histogram > 0; Short — mirrored (EMA5 < EMA8 < EMA13, pullback to EMA5, RSI > 30, MACD histogram < 0, entry on a breakout below the low). Module B (EMA Reversal/Flag): Long trend — EMA(9) > EMA(21) and an increasing EMA9-EMA21 spread over the last 3 bars; a “flag”/consolidation forms — the current BollingerBands(20,2) width (upper-lower) is below its SMA(20); price touches EMA(9) or EMA(21); Long entry — on a breakout above the high of the touch bar with RSI(14) in the 40–65 range and MACD histogram > 0; Short — mirrored (EMA9 < EMA21, BB width below SMA(20), EMA touch, RSI 35–60, MACD histogram < 0, entry on a breakout below the low). Filters (optionally enabled): use_rsi_filter, use_macd_filter, use_atr_vol_filter (e.g., ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 50) for sufficient volatility), use_bbands_squeeze (BB width < SMA(width, 20)). Position management: initial stop = 1.5*ATR(14) from the entry price; trailing stop — the maximum of EMA(21) and 1*ATR(14) from the local extremum; take-profit at 2R or exit on the opposite signal/close below EMA(13) for Long (above EMA(13) for Short), or a time stop after 10 bars. Position size — by fixed risk R per trade based on ATR. All components (modules A/B and filters) must have switches for optimization.

reformulated_task: Strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with monthly position sizing adjustment using a utility function with a penalty for negative skewness. Once a month (on the first trading day), compute over a 252-day window: expected daily return μ as the mean of log returns; volatility σ as ATR(14)/Close; a negative skewness proxy SkewPenalty = max(0, pLower - pUpper), where pLower and pUpper are the fractions of days over 252 when Close is below the lower and above the upper Bollinger(20, 2) band respectively. Utility function: U = μ − α·σ − β·SkewPenalty (α=2.0, β=1.0). Rules: if U ≤ 0 — no position; if 0 < U ≤ T — long position at 50% of capital; if U > T — long position at 100% of capital. Threshold T is the median U over the last 252 days (if history is shorter — T=0). Hold the position until the next monthly rebalance. Additional filters: enter only if Close > SMA(50) and RSI(14) > 45; avoid entry under low liquidity (SMA(volume,20) > SMA(volume,50)).

Utility function: U = μ − α·σ − β·SkewPenalty (α=2.0, β=1.0). Rules: if U ≤ 0 — no position; if 0 < U ≤ T — long position at 50% of capital; if U > T — long position at 100% of capital. Threshold T is the median U over the last 252 days (if history is shorter — T=0). Hold the position until the next monthly rebalance. Additional filters: enter only if Close > SMA(50) and RSI(14) > 45; avoid entry under low liquidity (SMA(volume,20) > SMA(volume,50)).

reformulated_task: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset with volatility-targeted position sizing. Idea: replace a cointegration spread with the deviation of the price of a single asset from its long-term mean and trade the return to the mean, minimizing risk via ATR. Indicators: BollingerBands(period=100, devfactor=2) for ±2σ thresholds and mid (SMA(100)); BollingerBands(period=100, devfactor=3) for the stop-loss at ±3σ; ATR(20) for positioning; optionally RSI(14) as a filter. Entry signals: long position when close < lower band of BB(100,2) (analogous to Z < -2); short position when close > upper band of BB(100,2) (analogous to Z > +2). Exit: close on return to the mean — when the price crosses mid BB(100,2) (SMA(100)) or when |close - mid| ≤ 0.5σ (practically: |close - mid| ≤ 0.25 * (top - mid) at dev=2). Stop-loss: close the position if the price reaches the corresponding band of BB(100,3) (analogous to |Z| ≥ 3). Position size: volatility targeting based on ATR(20): shares = min(available_cash/close, floor(capital / (ATR(20) * risk_factor))), where risk_factor is a constant (e.g., 100) to stabilize risk; for shorts use the same calculation. Additional filter (optional to reduce trend risk): do not open trades if SMA(20) diverges significantly from SMA(100), e.g., |SMA(20) - SMA(100)| > ATR(20); or require RSI(14) < 40 for longs and > 60 for shorts. Implementation in Backtrader: use the BollingerBands, ATR, RSI indicators and CrossOver to check for crossovers.
description: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset with volatility-targeted position sizing. Idea: replace a cointegration spread with the deviation of the price of a single asset from its long-term mean and trade the return to the mean, minimizing risk via ATR. Indicators: BollingerBands(period=100, devfactor=2) for ±2σ thresholds and mid (SMA(100)); BollingerBands(period=100, devfactor=3) for the stop-loss at ±3σ; ATR(20) for positioning; optionally RSI(14) as a filter. Entry signals: long position when close < lower band of BB(100,2) (analogous to Z < -2); short position when close > upper band of BB(100,2) (analogous to Z > +2). Exit: close on return to the mean — when the price crosses mid BB(100,2) (SMA(100)) or when |close - mid| ≤ 0.5σ (practically: |close - mid| ≤ 0.25 * (top - mid) at dev=2). Stop-loss: close the position if the price reaches the corresponding band of BB(100,3) (analogous to |Z| ≥ 3). Position size: volatility targeting based on ATR(20): shares = min(available_cash/close, floor(capital / (ATR(20) * risk_factor))), where risk_factor is a constant (e.g., 100) to stabilize risk; for shorts use the same calculation. Additional filter (optional to reduce trend risk): do not open trades if SMA(20) diverges significantly from SMA(100), e.g., |SMA(20) - SMA(100)| > ATR(20); or require RSI(14) < 40 for longs and > 60 for shorts. Implementation in Backtrader: use the BollingerBands, ATR, RSI indicators and CrossOver to check for crossovers.

reformulated_task: Strategy “risk on/risk off” for a single asset based on SMA(200): trade the only available asset (e.g., AAPL). Buy 100% of capital when its close crosses its SMA(200) from below to above (CrossOver). Fully exit to cash when the close crosses the SMA(200) from above to below. The SMA(200) and CrossOver indicators are used on the close data of the current asset.
description: Strategy “risk on/risk off” for a single asset based on SMA(200): trade the only available asset (e.g., AAPL). Buy 100% of capital when its close crosses its SMA(200) from below to above (CrossOver). Fully exit to cash when the close crosses the SMA(200) from above to below. The SMA(200) and CrossOver indicators are used on the close data of the current asset.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) based on 21-day returns: once a week (on Monday), calculate ROC21 = close/close[-21] - 1 and build BollingerBands(ROC21, period=252, dev=2). If ROC21 is below the lower band — open a long position; if above the upper band — a short position. Determine position size using ATR(14) with fixed risk (e.g., 1% of capital with a stop at 2*ATR from entry). Close the position strictly after 5 trading days. Trade only when history is ≥ 273 days.

If ROC21 is below the lower band — open a long position; if above the upper band — a short position. Determine position size using ATR(14) with fixed risk (e.g., 1% of capital with a stop at 2*ATR from entry). Close the position strictly after 5 trading days. Trade only when history is ≥ 273 days.

reformulated_task: Breakout strategy on a single asset (D1) with a proxy trend line via Bollinger Bands: buy on a breakout and close above the upper Bollinger Bands(20, 2) band, if the last 3 candles closed on/below the upper band and SMA(20) is rising (SMA(20) > SMA(20)[1]). Stop-loss: close the position on a close below the upper Bollinger band (false breakout confirmation). Take-profit: lock in +2% from the entry price. Position size is calculated so that the risk to the stop (entry − the value of the upper band at the time of entry) does not exceed 1% of capital.

Position size is calculated so that the risk to the stop (entry − the value of the upper band at the time of entry) does not exceed 1% of capital.

reformulated_task: Single-asset mean-variance strategy for AAPL: once a month, recalculate the target position weight using the formula w = clip( μ̂ / (λ · σ̂^2), [wmin, wmax] ), where μ̂ is SMA(252) of log returns, σ̂ is ATR(63)/Close (volatility in fractions), λ = 2.5. At rebalancing, place order_target_percent(w) and hold until the next date. Volatility filter: if σ̂ < 0.005, do not enter (w=0). Optional: if shorting is unavailable, wmin=0; otherwise wmin=-1, wmax=1.

reformulated_task:
ORB (Opening Range Breakout) on 1 asset with EMA/RSI/Bollinger confirmation and a Fib retracement, without VWAP.

Data/timing:
- One instrument (e.g., AAPL), OHLCV, intraday bars (minutes).
- Morning range window: the first X minutes after the main session open (default X=60; parameter).
- Additional session logic: “London” = morning (until 12:00), “New York” = after 13:30 until end of day (intraday windows).

Indicators:
- EMA(21) for trend.
- RSI(14) for momentum (threshold 50/55/45).
- Bollinger Bands(20, 2) — the middle band as the base “mean,” band width as a volatility proxy.
- ATR(14) — to assess volatility and for an optional trailing stop.

Morning range (ORB) definition:
- During the window [open; open+X] collect High and Low. ORH = maximum, ORL = minimum. Range R = ORH - ORL.
- Fib levels based on R (from ORL to ORH): 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%.

Trend/momentum filters (at entry):
- Long: close > EMA(21) and close > Bollinger middle band, RSI(14) > 50.
- Short: close < EMA(21) and close < Bollinger middle band, RSI(14) < 50.

Entry signals:
- Entry after the ORB window completes.
- Long: 1) price breaks ORH for the first time; 2) then a pullback to one of the Fib zones (38.2/50/61.8% R) occurs and the bar closes with a bounce upward; 3) trend/momentum confirmation (see above); 4) trigger — CrossOver(close, EMA(21)) upward on the bounce bar or a break of the bounce bar high.
- Short: mirrored (break ORL, retracement to the Fib zone from above downward, close < EMA(21) and below the Bollinger middle band, RSI < 50; trigger — CrossOver(EMA(21), close) or a break of the bounce bar low).
- Do not trade if there was no ORB breakout before 12:00.
- No more than 1 signal per direction per day (max. 2 trades: one “morning,” one “day” on a new setup).

Position management and exit:
- Stop-loss: on the opposite side of the ORB (for long — ORL, for short — ORH).
- Base take-profit: 1.0 × R from the entry price.
- Time-out: close the position 30 minutes before market close if neither TP nor SL has triggered.
- Optional trailing: when profit > 1.0 × R, enable an ATR(14) trailing stop with multiplier 1.0–1.5 to hold the move.

Holding-time adaptation by signal quality (without VWAP):
- Calculate a “quality score” Q at entry:
  - +1 if RSI(14) ≥ 55 (long) / ≤ 45 (short);
  - +1 if Bollinger width (Upper-Lower) is increasing vs the previous bar (volatility expanding);
  - +1 if the EMA(21) slope is aligned with the trade direction (EMA today > yesterday for long; < for short).
- If Q ≥ 2: set TP = 1.5 × R (more “patient” holding). Otherwise TP = 1.0 × R.

Session logic (simplified “London→New York”):
- If the morning trade (in “London”) closed at a loss, then if there is a valid setup in the second half of the day (“New York”) the strategy becomes more patient:
  - ignore boosting TP to 1.5 × R based on Q — use a minimum of 1.5 × R even if Q < 2;
  - allow holding until the time-out with ATR trailing enabled after reaching 1.0 × R.

Risk and position sizing:
- Risk no more than X% of capital per trade (default X=1%).
- Position size = (X% * Equity) / (|Entry - SL|). Round to a whole number of shares.

Other:
- Ignore signals on days when R is too small (e.g., R < k × ATR(14), k=0.5) to avoid false breakouts.
- Do not average down losing positions; re-entries are allowed only on a new valid setup after a pullback forms again.

reformulated_task:
Strategy: Post-Disaster Capitulation Bounce (OHLCV version for Backtrader)

Idea: catch the bounce after a capitulation drop amid panic selling and signs of reversal, using only price and volume.

Indicators and parameters:
- SMA(20), SMA(50)
- RSI(14)
- ATR(14)
- Average volume: VolSMA(20) = SMA of volume over 20 days
- Capitulation window: 5 days; active window after capitulation: 10 days

Definitions:
- “Capitulation drop”: over the last 5 trading days the price fell ≥ 15% (Close_now ≤ Close_5_days_ago × 0.85).
- “Capitulation day” (cap day): on one of the last 5 days there was a down day (Close < Open) with volume > 7 × VolSMA(20). Store cap_low = Low(cap day) and cap_date.
- “Pre-drop maximum” H: the maximum High over 20 bars prior to and including cap day (excluding days after cap day).
- Drop range: [L, H], where L = cap_low.

Entry conditions (Long) — all must be met on the entry bar:
1) Capitulation confirmed: drop ≥ 15% over 5 days and a cap day detected (see above).
2) Enter only within 10 trading days after cap day (current date ≤ cap_date + 10 bars).
3) Reversal signal:
   - Positive day: Close > Open
   - RSI(14) crosses up level 30 (CrossOver(RSI, 30) > 0) as a proxy for panic ending
   - Volume has eased to normal: Volume ≥ 0.8 × VolSMA(20)
   - Volatility is sufficient: (High - Low) / Open ≥ 3%
4) Trend filter: Close > SMA(20)
5) No open long position.

Exit (Take Profit):
- Stepwise by recovery levels of the [L, H] range:
  - TP1 = L + 0.382 × (H - L)
  - TP2 = L + 0.500 × (H - L)
  - TP3 = L + 0.618 × (H - L)
  - TP4 = H (100% recovery)
- If partial exit is unavailable, close the entire position at TP2 (50% retracement) or upon touching SMA(50) — whichever occurs first.

Exit (Stop Loss):
- Hard stop: Stop = L - 1 × ATR(14) (fixed at entry and not trailed).

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade = 2% of equity. Position size = floor((0.02 × Equity) / (EntryPrice - Stop)). If the calculated size < 1, the signal is skipped.

Additional rules:
- New signals are ignored after the 10-day window from cap day expires.
- If within the window a new cap day appears (even more extreme by volume), redefine L, H, and the window anew.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion on a single asset with a “pseudo-spread” (price − SMA(50)). Indicators: SMA(200) — trend filter, SMA(50) — base for deviation, ATR(14) and SMA(ATR(14), 30) — volatility filter. Long entry: if price > SMA(200), price ≤ SMA(50) × 0.95, and SMA(ATR(14), 30) / close ≤ 0.05; additionally liquidity: SMA(20) of volume × close above a specified threshold. The entry is executed with a limit order no worse than 0.5% from the price of the signal candle. Long exit: when price crosses SMA(50) from below upward (CrossOver), or stop-loss if the initial deviation d0 = SMA(50)_entry − entry_price increased by 10% and d ≥ 1.1 × d0. Symmetric short scenario: if price < SMA(200) and price ≥ SMA(50) × 1.05, volatility is within normal range; exit when price crosses SMA(50) from above downward, or stop when the deviation (close − SMA(50)) increases to 1.1 × d0. Position size: risk per trade as a fixed fraction of capital with ATR positioning — qty = floor((risk_pct × equity) / (k × ATR(14))).

reformulated_task: Liquidity Shock (proxy) strategy on a single asset with OHLCV: use a proxy spread as the normalized candle range s = (high - low) / close and its SMA(20) as the “normal” level. Entries are formed on a sharp contraction/expansion of this range against the background of abnormally high volume and low volatility. Conditions: 1) Long position — s < 0.8 * SMA(s, 20), SMA(volume, 10) > 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20), ATR(10) < SMA(ATR(10), 50), and |close - close[-1]| / close[-1] ≤ 0.001. 2) Short position — s > 1.2 * SMA(s, 20), SMA(volume, 10) > 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20), ATR(10) < SMA(ATR(10), 50), and |close - close[-1]| / close[-1] ≤ 0.001. Exit: close the position when s returns to SMA(s, 20) (crossing via CrossOver) or by take-profit 0.5% / stop-loss 0.3%. Risk management: the size of each order is no more than 1% of capital; up to 3 additions are allowed (no more than 3 simultaneously open lots for one asset), a stop-loss of 0.3% from the entry price is mandatory.

reformulated_task: Momentum Strategy in Ichimoku Style on SMA: Use SMA(9) as an analogue of Tenkan, SMA(26) as Kijun, and SMA(52) as the long-term support of the cloud. Enter long when SMA(9) crosses SMA(26) from below to above at the bar close, with the closing price above both averages (SMA(26) and SMA(52)) and SMA(26) > SMA(52) (an analogue of a “bullish cloud”). Enter short when SMA(9) crosses SMA(26) from above to below at the close, with the price below both averages and SMA(26) < SMA(52) (an analogue of a “bearish cloud”). Exit long on the reverse cross (SMA(9) crosses SMA(26) from above to below) or when the price closes below the lower of SMA(26) and SMA(52). Exit short on the reverse cross (SMA(9) crosses SMA(26) from below to above) or when the price closes above the higher of SMA(26) and SMA(52). Position size: 100% of available capital per trade. Stop-loss and take-profit are not used. All signals are confirmed at the bar close. Indicators: SMA(9), SMA(26), SMA(52), CrossOver.

reformulated_task: Momentum strategy on a single asset (daily timeframe) with one-day holding: we use MACD(12,26,9) and CrossOver as the signal source. Entry: if on day t the MACD line crosses the signal line from below to above (CrossOver > 0), then on day t+1 we open a long position at the opening price (open). Exit: we close the position on the same day t+1 at the closing price (close). Initial capital $100; position size = 100% of current capital; after each trade, capital is updated as Capital_{new} = Capital_{old} * (1 + (close_{t+1} - open_{t+1}) / open_{t+1}). If there is no signal, no trade is opened; no short selling. Track the capital dynamics after each trade.
description: Momentum strategy on a single asset (daily timeframe) with one-day holding: we use MACD(12,26,9) and CrossOver as the signal source. Exit: we close the position on the same day t+1 at the closing price (close). Initial capital $100; position size = 100% of current capital; after each trade, capital is updated as Capital_{new} = Capital_{old} * (1 + (close_{t+1} - open_{t+1}) / open_{t+1}). If there is no signal, no trade is opened; no short selling. Track the capital dynamics after each trade.

reformulated_task: Breakout Support/Resistance without ZigZag: levels = range extremes. Define resistance as the maximum high over the last 60 candles, support as the minimum low over the last 60 candles. Level confirmation: over the last 100 candles there were at least 3 touches (for resistance — high was within 0.2% of the level, for support — low within 0.2%). Long entry: the current close closes above the resistance level by at least 0.5% and the current candle volume > SMA(volume, 20) by 30%+. Short entry: the current close closes below the support level by at least 0.5% and the current candle volume > SMA(volume, 20) by 30%+. Stop-loss: for long — 1% below the broken resistance level, for short — 1% above the broken support level. Take-profit: 2% from the entry price (risk/reward ratio 1:2). Alternative exit: if within 5 candles after entry the take-profit is not reached and price returns back beyond the broken level (for long — a close below the resistance level, for short — a close above the support level), close the position. Ignore signals if the breakout is less than 0.5% from the level. Risk management: position size such that the potential loss at the stop does not exceed 1% of capital (size = floor(Equity*0.01 / (|entry - stop|))). Do not open a new position if there is already an open one. Indicators used: SMA(volume, 20); if necessary, ATR(14) may be added only for volatility monitoring, without changing the entry/exit rules.

Alternative exit: if within 5 candles after entry the take-profit is not reached and price returns back beyond the broken level (for long — a close below the resistance level, for short — a close above the support level), close the position. Ignore signals if the breakout is less than 0.5% from the level. Risk management: position size such that the potential loss at the stop does not exceed 1% of capital (size = floor(Equity*0.01 / (|entry - stop|))). Do not open a new position if there is already an open one. Indicators used: SMA(volume, 20); if necessary, ATR(14) may be added only for volatility monitoring, without changing the entry/exit rules.

reformulated_task:
Strategy: Technical breakout on a single asset (e.g., XOM) on the daily chart without an external driver

Idea: Look for a confirmed bullish breakout from consolidation on a single asset via a break above the upper Bollinger band after a squeeze, with the price simultaneously positioned above key moving averages and confirmation of momentum strength via rising volatility and volume.

Indicators and parameters:
- SMA(200) on close
- EMA(8) and EMA(20) on close
- Bollinger Bands(20, 2)
- ATR(14)
- SMA(20) on volume (SMA on data.volume)
- (Optional for exit) MACD(12, 26, 9)

Entry conditions (BUY) — all simultaneously, at the daily close:
1) Breakout from consolidation: close closes above the upper Bollinger(20,2) band AND the day before there was a “squeeze” — BB band width yesterday (BB_top - BB_bot) was below its SMA(20) of the width.
2) Trend filter: close > SMA(200) AND EMA(8) > EMA(20) AND close > EMA(8).
3) Confirmation of move strength:
   - ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 14)
   - Day’s volume > 1.2 × SMA(volume, 20)

Exit conditions (SELL):
1) Take Profit: lock in profit upon reaching the target level = entry price + 3 × ATR(14) at the time of entry.
2) Stop Loss: initial stop = entry price − 1.5 × ATR(14) at the time of entry.
3) Trailing/alternative weakness-based exit: a close below EMA(20) OR (optional) a bearish MACD crossover (the MACD line crosses the signal line from above to below) — whichever triggers first.

Risk management:
- Position size is chosen so that the risk to the initial stop-loss is no more than 1–2% of capital per trade.
- New entries only when there is no open position.

Rules and frequency:
- Timeframe: Daily.
- All decisions are made based on the closing prices of the respective day.
- All entry conditions must be met simultaneously.

reformulated_task: Strategy “Panic/Euphoria” for a single asset on OHLCV: trade only in a long-term uptrend (Close > SMA(200)). Entry (buy) during market panic: Close below the lower Bollinger band (20, 2) and RSI(14) ≤ 30, with elevated volatility: ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 50) or Bollinger width (upper-lower) > SMA(width, 50). Exit (sell) during euphoria/calm: Close above the upper Bollinger band (20, 2) and RSI(14) ≥ 65, and volatility is decreasing: ATR(14) < SMA(ATR(14), 50) or width < SMA(width, 50). Outside extreme zones — cash. No stop-losses; position size is fixed (e.g., 100% of capital).

Exit (sell) during euphoria/calm: Close above the upper Bollinger band (20, 2) and RSI(14) ≥ 65, and volatility is decreasing: ATR(14) < SMA(ATR(14), 50) or width < SMA(width, 50). reformulated_task: Dynamic single-asset (OHLCV) strategy based on the longest sequence of non-decreasing bars. Instead of strikes, use time-ordered bars. Sequence-indicator: the length of the current continuous series of bars where both Close[t] >= Close[t-1] and SMA(10)[t] >= SMA(10)[t-1] are simultaneously satisfied. Entry (buy): when the series length reaches at least 2 bars and becomes a record over the last N bars (e.g., N=100), open a long position on the next bar. Exit (sell): on the first violation of the series condition (Close[t] < Close[t-1] or SMA(10)[t] < SMA(10)[t-1]), close the position on the next bar. Risk management: one position, no stops/takes; minimum sequence length for trading is 2 bars; uniqueness is ensured by trading only one active series and the “record length” filter within the N window. Indicators: SMA(10) (optionally, a trend filter can be added: price > SMA(50)).

reformulated_task: Strategy “Bounce off Support in an Uptrend” for a single asset (OHLCV) in Backtrader. Indicators: EMA(20) for trend and support, EMA(50) as a long-term filter, RSI(14) for the neutral zone, SMA(20) on volume, ATR(14) for the stop buffer. Filters: (1) Return over 20 bars ≥ 15%; (2) Long-term trend up: EMA(50) > EMA(50)[-1]. Entry (long) when all conditions are met: (a) Price above EMA(20) and EMA(20) is rising; (b) There was a support test: low of the current or previous candle ≤ EMA(20), and the current candle closed above EMA(20) and above the previous candle’s high (confirmed bounce); (c) RSI(14) in the 40–60 range; (d) Current candle volume > 1.2 × SMA(volume, 20). Targets/exit: take-profit at the nearest resistance — the maximum over the last 20 bars (HH20); stop-loss — below the support level: min(local swing-low over 5 bars, low of the EMA20 test candle) − 0.5×ATR(14); early exit on a close below EMA(20). Entry is allowed only if the expected profit/risk ratio ≥ 2: (HH20 − entry price) ≥ 2 × (entry price − stop). Risk per trade 1–2% of capital with position sizing calculated from the distance to the stop.

reformulated_task: Strategy "Managed Income with Capital Protection (ATR targets instead of covered calls)" for a single asset in Backtrader. Indicators: SMA(50), SMA(200), ATR(14). Entry: if there is no position, the closing price is above SMA(50) and SMA(200), and ATR(14)/Close ≤ 0.015 (1.5% of price) — buy. Position size: no more than 5% of current equity (size = floor(0.05 * equity / Close)). Take-profit (strike analog): lock in profit upon reaching the target TP = min(Entry * 1.05, Entry + 2.5 * ATR(14) at the time of entry). Stop/protection: close the position if Close falls below SMA(50). Portfolio risk management: if the equity drawdown is ≥ 10% from the starting level — close all positions and stop opening new ones. Re-entries are possible when the trend and volatility conditions are met again.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader module for a SINGLE asset (input: ticker), working only with OHLCV and the indicators SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, BollingerBands, Stochastic, ATR, CrossOver. The module calculates 30 classic approaches and for each returns an unambiguous signal (Buy/Sell/Hold) and the levels: entry (by default — next bar at market), stop-loss, and take-profit. General level rules: unless otherwise specified, SL = 1.5*ATR(14) from the entry price; TP = 2*ATR(14) from the entry price; for mean-reversion to averages/bands, TP to the target line (middle band/moving average) is allowed. Default indicator parameters: SMA(10/20/50/200), EMA(10/12/20/26/50), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), Bollinger(20,2), Stochastic(14,3,3), ATR(14). List of 30 approaches:
1) MACD signal: Buy on MACD crossing above the signal line, Sell — below.
2) MACD zero line: Buy on MACD crossing above 0, Sell — below 0.
3) MACD histogram: Buy when the histogram crosses above 0 and rises for ≥2 bars; Sell — below 0 and falling for ≥2 bars.
4) RSI zones: Buy when RSI(14) exits the <30 zone upward; Sell when exiting >70 downward.
5) RSI 50 level: Buy when RSI crosses above 50; Sell — below 50.
6) RSI pullback in trend: In an up-trend (Close>SMA200) Buy when RSI dropped <40 and then back >40; in a down-trend (Close<SMA200) Sell on >60 and back <60.
7) SMA 50/200 (golden/death cross): Buy on SMA50↑SMA200; Sell — SMA50↓SMA200.
8) EMA 12/26 crossover: Buy on EMA12↑EMA26; Sell — EMA12↓EMA26.
9) SMA 20/50 crossover: Buy on SMA20↑SMA50; Sell — SMA20↓SMA50.
10) Triple alignment: Buy if SMA20>SMA50>SMA200 and Close>SMA20; Sell — SMA20<SMA50<SMA200 and Close<SMA20.
11) SMA200 regime: Buy on Close↑SMA200; Sell — Close↓SMA200.
12) Bollinger mean-reversion (re-entry): Buy on touch/close below the lower band and the subsequent close above it (TP=middle band); Sell — mirrored (TP=middle band).
13) Bollinger breakout: Buy on a close above the upper band; Sell — below the lower.
14) Bollinger squeeze breakout: If band width (Upper−Lower) is at a 20-bar minimum and the close is above the upper — Buy; if below the lower — Sell.
15) Middle band + RSI: Buy on Close↑Bollinger middle band and RSI>50; Sell — Close↓middle and RSI<50.
16) Stochastic zones: Buy when %K crosses %D from below to above in the <20 zone; Sell — from above to below in the >80 zone.
17) Stochastic + trend: Buy on %K↑%D and Close>SMA200; Sell — %K↓%D and Close<SMA200.
18) Stochastic 50 level: Buy when %K crosses 50 upward; Sell — downward.
19) Stochastic pop: Buy when %K rises 3 bars in a row and breaks above 20; Sell — falls 3 bars and breaks below 80.
20) Volatility trend: Buy on Close↑SMA20 and ATR(14)>SMA(ATR(14),14); Sell — Close↓SMA20 and ATR>average.
21) ATR momentum to SMA50: Buy when Close−SMA50 > 1*ATR(14); Sell — SMA50−Close > 1*ATR(14).
22) RSI+MACD confluence: Buy if RSI>50 and MACD>signal; Sell — RSI<50 and MACD<signal.
23) EMA20 pullback: In an up-trend (EMA20>EMA50) Buy on a close below/on EMA20 and the next close above EMA20 (TP=previous swing high or by default); in a down-trend — Sell mirrored.
24) SMA20 ± ATR channel: Buy when Close < SMA20−1*ATR and returns above SMA20−ATR; Sell — when Close > SMA20+1*ATR and returns below SMA20+ATR (TP=middle SMA20).
25) 3-bar breakout + RSI: Buy when Close>max(High[-1..-3]) and RSI>55; Sell — Close<min(Low[-1..-3]) and RSI<45.
26) RSI divergence: Bullish — price makes a lower low, RSI — a higher low (over 20 bars) → Buy; Bearish — price a higher high, RSI — a lower high → Sell.
27) MACD+BB confirmation: Buy on a close above the upper band and MACD>0; Sell — below the lower band and MACD<0.
28) EMA12/26 pullback continuation: In an up-trend (EMA12>EMA26) Buy on a pullback with Close between EMA12 and EMA26 and a close above EMA12; Sell — mirrored.
29) Bollinger range mean-reversion: If band width < SMA(width,20) and price touches the lower band with re-entry inside — Buy (TP=middle band); touch the upper band and re-entry — Sell.
30) Scuttle trend SMA20: Buy if SMA20 rises 3 bars in a row and Close>SMA20; Sell — SMA20 falls 3 bars and Close<SMA20.
“Hold” outputs are formed when there are no conditions for “Buy”/“Sell”. The module returns for each approach: the signal, entry price (by default — next bar at market), SL and TP according to the rules above (or those specified for a particular approach).

reformulated_task: Zonal strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) without options. On each bar, zones are automatically determined: support — the minimum over the last 20 bars, resistance — the maximum over the last 20 bars; the width of each zone = 2% of the current price (zone — level ±1% from Close). Trend filter: SMA(50) with slope (for long: SMA(50) > SMA(50) 5 bars ago; for short: SMA(50) < SMA(50) 5 bars ago). Volatility: ATR(14)/Close > 0.015. Long entry: the bar touches/enters the support zone (High ≥ lower boundary and Low ≤ upper boundary of the support zone) and closes above the upper boundary of the zone; SMA(50) slope up; the ATR filter is met and win rate ≥ 60% over the last 100 trades; open “units” < 3. Short entry: the bar touches/enters the resistance zone and closes below the lower boundary of the zone; SMA(50) slope down; the same filters and position limit are met. Exit: take-profit = +1×ATR(14) from the entry price (toward profit), stop-loss = −2.5×ATR(14); additional exit — when the bar closes outside the opposite zone (for long — above the upper boundary of the resistance zone, for short — below the lower boundary of the support zone). Risk management: entry size — for 1% of capital (qty = floor(0.01*Equity/Close)); no more than 3 open “units” per instrument at the same time.

reformulated_task: Bullish “volatility selling” on a single asset with a 60/30/10/0-bar timebox: enter long on a touch/break of the lower Bollinger Bands(20,2) in an uptrend (EMA(50) > SMA(200)) and RSI(14) < 35. Volatility filter (VIX/IV analog): band width (BB.top − BB.bot) / BB.mid must be in a moderate range of 2–8%; outside this range, entries are prohibited. Position management (credit put spread analog): risk per trade 1–2% of capital; stop = 1.5×ATR(14) below entry price; take-profit = +0.5R (50% of the calculated risk), soft stop-loss at −0.8R and hard at −1.0R; forced time-based exit at DTE=60/30/10 bars and “0 DTE” (timebox expiration day). Volatility exit: close if Bollinger Bands width has increased by ≥20% from the entry value. Additional rules: do not open new trades during a portfolio drawdown ≥10% and if price is below SMA(200).
description: Bullish “volatility selling” on a single asset with a 60/30/10/0-bar timebox: enter long on a touch/break of the lower Bollinger Bands(20,2) in an uptrend (EMA(50) > SMA(200)) and RSI(14) < 35. Volatility filter (VIX/IV analog): band width (BB.top − BB.bot) / BB.mid must be in a moderate range of 2–8%; outside this range, entries are prohibited. Position management (credit put spread analog): risk per trade 1–2% of capital; stop = 1.5×ATR(14) below entry price; take-profit = +0.5R (50% of the calculated risk), soft stop-loss at −0.8R and hard at −1.0R; forced time-based exit at DTE=60/30/10 bars and “0 DTE” (timebox expiration day). reformulated_task: Strategy “Quasi-Credit Spreads for Small Accounts” on a single asset with OHLCV. Idea: imitate selling far OTM credit spreads via countertrend mean-reversion trading off Bollinger Bands in calm volatility regimes, with early exit and strict risk management. Data: one ticker (e.g., AAPL), fields open/high/low/close/volume. Indicators: SMA(50), BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), RSI(14). “Event” filters (proxy): low volatility and no abnormal volume — entries are allowed only if band width bb_width = (upper−lower)/middle < SMA(bb_width, 100) and current volume ≤ 1.5×SMA(volume, 20), and also there is no big gap: |open − close[-1]| < 1.5×ATR(14). Direction: trend by SMA(50). Long entry (bull-put analog): if close ≤ lower Bollinger Band, trend is up (close > SMA(50)), RSI(14) < 45, and the volatility/volume filters are met — open a long position. Short entry (bear-call analog): if close ≥ upper band, trend is down (close < SMA(50)), RSI(14) > 55, and the filters are met — open a short position. Profit target: close upon reaching 70% of the distance to SMA(20) from the entry price (target = entry − sign*(0.7*(entry − SMA20_entry))) or upon price crossing SMA(20) — whichever comes first. Stop-loss: 1.2×ATR(14) from the entry price; immediate exit if the price closed beyond the corresponding band by another 0.5×ATR (analog of “early assignment risk”). Time in position: close after 6 bars if TP/SL have not triggered. Risk and position sizing: risk per trade 1–2% of capital; size = floor((equity*risk_pct)/(1.2×ATR(14))). Hold no more than one position at a time.
description: Strategy “Quasi-Credit Spreads for Small Accounts” on a single asset with OHLCV. Idea: imitate selling far OTM credit spreads via countertrend mean-reversion trading off Bollinger Bands in calm volatility regimes, with early exit and strict risk management. Data: one ticker (e.g., AAPL), fields open/high/low/close/volume. Indicators: SMA(50), BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), RSI(14). “Event” filters (proxy): low volatility and no abnormal volume — entries are allowed only if band width bb_width = (upper−lower)/middle < SMA(bb_width, 100) and current volume ≤ 1.5×SMA(volume, 20), and also there is no big gap: |open − close[-1]| < 1.5×ATR(14). Direction: trend by SMA(50). Long entry (bull-put analog): if close ≤ lower Bollinger Band, trend is up (close > SMA(50)), RSI(14) < 45, and the volatility/volume filters are met — open a long position. Short entry (bear-call analog): if close ≥ upper band, trend is down (close < SMA(50)), RSI(14) > 55, and the filters are met — open a short position. Profit target: close upon reaching 70% of the distance to SMA(20) from the entry price (target = entry − sign*(0.7*(entry − SMA20_entry))) or upon price crossing SMA(20) — whichever comes first. Stop-loss: 1.2×ATR(14) from the entry price; immediate exit if the price closed beyond the corresponding band by another 0.5×ATR (analog of “early assignment risk”). Time in position: close after 6 bars if TP/SL have not triggered. Risk and position sizing: risk per trade 1–2% of capital; size = floor((equity*risk_pct)/(1.2×ATR(14))). Hold no more than one position at a time.

reformulated_task: Trend-following strategy for a single asset with entries on MACD crossing the zero line and ATR-based risk management. Indicators: MACD(12,26,9) and ATR(14). Entries: Long — when the MACD line crosses 0 from below to above; Short — when the MACD line crosses 0 from above to below. Exits: immediately after entry, SL and TP are placed; SL = 1 * ATR from the entry price (for Long: Entry - ATR; for Short: Entry + ATR), TP = 3 * ATR from the entry price (fixed R:R = 3:1). Position size is calculated based on risking 1–2% of capital per trade using the ATR stop. Backtest: one asset, 3-year period, standard OHLCV data.

reformulated_task:
Market making (mean reversion) on a single asset with a microstructure proxy via OHLCV: simultaneously quote passive limit orders around the “mean price” and profit from a narrow range. Data: OHLCV only. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), ATR(14), SMA of volume(20).

Activation conditions (narrow and liquid market):
- Bollinger Band width: (BB_up − BB_low) / BB_mid ≤ 0.003 (0.3%).
- Relative volatility: ATR(14) / Close ≤ 0.004.
- Volume ≥ SMA(Volume, 20).

Quoting (when there is no position and the total risk of active orders < 5% of capital):
- “Mean price” = Bollinger middle line (BB_mid).
- Place two limit orders simultaneously:
  • Buy Limit at price: BB_mid − 0.15 × ATR(14)
  • Sell Limit at price: BB_mid + 0.15 × ATR(14)
- Size of each order = floor(1% of capital / current price).

Execution and exit:
- If the Buy is filled: immediately place an opposing Take-Profit Sell Limit: price = entry price + 0.30 × ATR(14); cancel the original opposing quote (sell).
- If the Sell is filled: immediately place an opposing Take-Profit Buy Limit: price = entry price − 0.30 × ATR(14); cancel the original opposing quote (buy).
- Time-out: if the opposing order is not filled within 1 bar, cancel it and close the position at market on the next bar.
- Stop protection: if price moves against the position beyond the corresponding Bollinger Band, close the position at market.

Time constraints:
- On minute data, do not trade during the first and last 30 minutes of the session (bar time filter). For daily data, this rule is ignored.

Risk management:
- Maximum size of a single order — 1% of capital.
- Do not place new orders if the total value of all open limit orders and positions > 5% of capital.

reformulated_task: Quant-Value strategy for 1 asset (e.g., AAPL) without fundamental data: use price/volume proxies for “cheapness” and a trend filter. Entry: (1) trend — Close > SMA(200); (2) “golden cross” — SMA(50) crossing above SMA(200) within the last 5 days (CrossOver); (3) value proxy — BollingerBands(20,2): Close below the middle band and closer to the lower band (PercentB ≤ 0.2 = (Close−BBbot)/(BBtop−BBbot) ≤ 0.2); (4) RSI(14) in the 40–60 range (avoid overbought/oversold extremes); (5) liquidity — SMA(Volume,20) > 100000 and > SMA(Volume,50); (6) stability — ATR(14)/Close ≤ 0.04. Exit: (1) take-profit +20% from the entry price; (2) stop-loss −10% from the entry price; (3) “death cross” — SMA(50) crosses SMA(200) from above to below; (4) additional exit on overheating: RSI ≥ 70 or a touch of the upper Bollinger band; (5) exit on a risk spike: ATR(14)/Close > 0.06. Risk management: position size based on risking 1% of capital with initial stop distances = max(10% of price, 2×ATR); cash allocation ≈ min(25% of capital, risk-based size). Backtest: daily data for 10+ years, one ticker, comparison of metrics in the strategy report (return/drawdown/Sharpe based on strategy returns without an external benchmark).

reformulated_task: Long-term breakout strategy on a single asset (OHLCV) with trend and volatility filters. Indicators: SMA(200), BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), SMA of band width (100), SMA ATR(100). Long entry: (1) close > SMA(200); (2) consolidation: Bollinger band width < SMA(width, 100) * 0.8 over the last 10 bars; (3) breakout: close crossing up through the upper band (CrossOver). Short entry symmetrically: close < SMA(200), the same consolidation, breakout of the lower band. Volatility filter: 0.7 <= ATR(14) / SMA(ATR, 100) <= 1.3. Risk management: risk per trade 1% of capital, position size = (1% equity) / stop distance. Initial stop: Long — entry - 3*ATR, Short — entry + 3*ATR. Take-profit: 3R. Trailing stop (chandelier style): Long — max(close since entry) - 3*ATR; Short — min(close since entry) + 3*ATR. Move to breakeven upon reaching 1.5R. Minimum holding period 60 trading days (except stop/take triggers). Cooldown for re-entry 20 days after exit.

reformulated_task:
Daily strategy Weekly-ORB (ORB proxy on D1):
- Timeframe: D1, 1 asset.
- Weekly Opening Range: use the High and Low of the first trading day of the week (usually Monday; if it’s a holiday — the first day of the week). Level 0.5 = (High+Low)/2.
- Trend filter (VWAP replacement): price < EMA(20), price < SMA(50), EMA(20) < SMA(50), EMA(20) slope down (EMA(20) today < EMA(20) yesterday).
- Short scenario: 1) In the current week, an initial breakout down occurs (daily close below Monday’s Low). 2) Then wait for a price return to the 0.5 level of Monday’s range within a maximum of the next 2 trading days of the same week. 3) On a touch/cross of the 0.5 level from above to below and with the trend filter satisfied — enter a short position.
- Exit: stop-loss at Monday’s High; take-profit upon reaching/breaking Monday’s Low.
- Risk management: position size so that the stop risk is 1–2% of capital.
- Signal skip: if there was no return to 0.5 within 2 days after the breakout or the week ended, do not open the trade.

reformulated_task:
**Trading Strategy Writing Assignment:**

Write a daily trading strategy based on the "Opening Range of the Day" method (ORB - Opening Range Breakout) for the 15-minute timeframe.

**Core strategy idea:**
Determine the price range of the first 15 minutes of the trading day and trade the breakout of its boundaries. Position entry is executed on a pullback to key levels confirmed by momentum indicators.

**Indicators and parameters:**
1. **ORB (Opening Range):** Defined as the difference between the highest (ORH - Opening Range High) and the lowest (ORL - Opening Range Low) price of the first 15 minutes of the trading day.
2. **Fibonacci Retracement:** 38.2% and 61.8% levels of the ORB range to determine entry points on a pullback.
3. **VWAP (Volume Weighted Average Price):** Standard calculation without additional parameters.
4. **EMA (Exponential Moving Average):** Two EMAs with periods 9 and 21 to determine trend direction.

**Conditions for entering a position:**
1. **Long position (buy):**
   - Price breaks above the ORH level.
   - A pullback occurs to the 38.2% or 61.8% level of the ORB range.
   - Both EMAs (9 and 21) are sloping upward.
   - Price is above VWAP.

2. **Short position (sell):**
   - Price breaks below the ORL level.
   - A pullback occurs to the 38.2% or 61.8% level of the ORB range.
   - Both EMAs (9 and 21) are sloping downward.
   - Price is below VWAP.

**Conditions for exiting a position:**
1. **Take profit:** Close the position upon reaching the opposite boundary of the ORB range (for a long position - upon reaching ORL, for a short position - upon reaching ORH).
2. **Stop loss:** Close the position if the price returns inside the ORB range (for a long position - a break of ORL, for a short position - a break of ORH).

**Risk management:**
- Position size is calculated based on a standard risk per trade of 1-2% of trading capital.
- The stop loss is placed on the opposite side of the ORB range.

**Additional rules:**
- The strategy is activated only after the completion of the first 15 minutes of the trading day.
- Trading is conducted only within one trading day. All open positions are closed at the end of the trading session.

reformulated_task: Strategy for a single asset (OHLCV): Deep-Dip → Leader Pyramiding. Data: one ticker, indicators SMA(200), EMA(20), EMA(10), RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), BollingerBands(20,2), ATR(14), CrossOver. Phase 1 (Dip Buying): crash filter — closing price ≤ 30% of the maximum close over 252 bars (H252, calculated from the data history), i.e., a drop ≥ 70%. Entry: CrossOver(close, EMA(20)) upward AND CrossOver(RSI(14), 30) upward; alternatively — a touch of the lower Bollinger band and a close above it with RSI < 35. Phase 2 (Leader/Momentum): activated when close > SMA(200) and MACD > 0. Pyramiding: add to the position on pullbacks to EMA(20) or the lower Bollinger band with RSI 45–60 and MACD > 0; maximum 3 adds and only with positive unrealized profit. Exits: profit taking — partial at +50% from the average entry price, remainder at +100% or on a touch of the upper Bollinger with RSI > 75; trend exit — CrossOver(MACD, signal) downward or two consecutive closes below EMA(20). Stops: initial stop = entry − 1.5*ATR(14); in the growth phase trailing stop = maximum price since entry − 3*ATR(14) (the maximum is tracked manually). Risk management: risk on the initial entry 1% of capital, size = 1%/ (1.5*ATR); max 3 averaging up; no new entries for 30 bars after exit.

reformulated_task: Strategy: "Bull Trend Bounce with Volatility Filter" for a single asset (OHLCV) in Backtrader. Indicators: SMA(20), RSI(14), BollingerBands(20,2). Entry (long, limit order): 1) Trend — Close > SMA(20). 2) Bounce — RSI(14) crosses level 40 from below upwards (after RSI was < 40). 3) Near-OTM proxy — moderate overbought relative to the baseline: (Close - SMA(20)) / SMA(20) in the range [5%, 10%]. 4) Volatility filter — relative Bollinger Bands width below 2%: (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid < 0.02. Order: Buy Limit at price Close * (1 - 0.005). Exit: Take Profit +2% from the entry price; Stop Loss -1.5% from the entry price; forced close after 5 bars. Risk management: position size — no more than 2% of capital (qty = int(0.02 * equity / Close)); simultaneously no more than 3 open entries (pyramiding up to 3 units). Additionally: for intraday data, signals are active only between 10:00 and 16:00 local time.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with walk-forward calibration using Bollinger Bands. Data: daily OHLCV. Rolling window regime: 12 months in-sample, then 1 month gap (no trades), then 6 months out-of-sample trading; then the window shifts. At the end of each in-sample, compute and fix for the entire next trading period: theta = SMA(252) of the closing price, sigma = the standard deviation from Bollinger Bands(252). Signals: 1) Long entry — if Close < theta − k*sigma (k=2) and RSI(14) < 40; 2) Short entry — if Close > theta + k*sigma and RSI(14) > 60. Exit (take profit): when Close returns to theta (crossing of price and the fixed theta; use CrossOver). Stop-loss: if Close moves further to m*sigma from theta (m=3) in the direction opposite to the reversion; an additional protective stop — if price has moved 2*ATR(14) against the position. Position sizing: risk 1% of capital per trade; quantity = (1% of capital) / max(distance to stop, 1.5*ATR(14)). Additionally: do not open a new position if there is already an active one; optional trend filter — skip shorts if EMA(50) < EMA(200) is violated, and longs if EMA(50) < EMA(200).

reformulated_task: Intraday short strategy on a single asset (e.g., SPY/AAPL), emulating a 0DTE PUT as a hedge against a morning sell-off. Only one spot instrument with OHLCV is traded. Indicators: SMA(20), SMA(50), EMA(9), RSI(14), ATR(14), BollingerBands(20,2), CrossOver. Entry (short) is allowed only from 9:30 to 11:30 ET, if: (1) the current price has fallen from the session open price by 0.5% or more (close/session_open - 1 <= -0.005), (2) downtrend: close < SMA(50) and RSI(14) < 45, (3) volatility is not low: ATR(14)/close >= 0.005. Entry trigger: a bearish crossover of EMA(9) below SMA(20) (CrossOver). Risk management: fixed risk per trade $100–$500, stop-loss = entry + 1.5*ATR(14), take-profit = entry - 2*ATR(14); position size = floor(risk_dollars / (1.5*ATR(14))). Exit: forced closing of all positions 15 minutes before the close (15:45 ET) or by stop/target. Additional filters: do not trade during an extreme opening gap |open - prev_close|/prev_close > 0.015 or during low volatility ATR(14)/close < 0.004.

reformulated_task: Breakout on a single asset (OHLCV-only): trade one instrument (e.g., AAPL). Filter entry days by volume, volatility, and recent returns, and enter on a range breakout via Bollinger Bands. Rules: 1) Trading days: Monday, Wednesday, Friday only. 2) Pre-entry filters: a) Volume: current volume > SMA(volume, 30) and SMA(volume, 30) > SMA(volume, 90); b) 7-day return: (Close/Close[-7] - 1) in the range [-10%, +20%]; c) Volatility: ATR(14)/Close > 1%. 3) Entry signal (long): CrossOver(Close, UpperBand) on BollingerBands(period=N=7, devfactor=2). 4) Exit: take-profit +5% or stop-loss -2% from the entry price (bracket orders), alternatively close the position on CrossOver(LowerBand, Close) — a close below the lower Bollinger(7) band. 5) Risk management and position sizing: risk per trade = 1% of initial capital; position size = floor((initial_capital*0.01)/(EntryPrice*0.02)); if the size is 0 — do not enter; reinvestment is disabled — use initial_capital for all trades. 6) Commissions and costs: account for them via commission in the Backtrader broker. 7) Testing: tune parameters on 2020–2022, walk-forward validation with 2023+. Additional: one asset, no averaging, new entries only when no position is open.

Additional: one asset, no averaging, new entries only when no position is open.

reformulated_task:
Trend-following strategy for 1 asset in Backtrader, avoiding flat markets: ATR consolidation filter + EMA(50) and MACD.

Indicators:
- ATR(14)
- SMA(ATR14, 50) and SMA(ATR14, 200) to assess relative volatility
- EMA(50) for trend direction
- MACD(12, 26, 9) to confirm momentum

Flat-market filter (mandatory for any entries):
- The market is considered NOT in a flat market if SMA(ATR14, 50) >= 0.75 * SMA(ATR14, 200)

Long entry conditions:
- Close price > EMA(50)
- EMA(50) has a positive slope: EMA(50) > EMA(50)[-1]
- MACD.macd > MACD.signal (histogram > 0)

Short entry conditions:
- Close price < EMA(50)
- EMA(50) has a negative slope: EMA(50) < EMA(50)[-1]
- MACD.macd < MACD.signal (histogram < 0)

Exit conditions:
- Stop-loss (initialization):
  - Long: stop = min(low of the entry bar, entry close) - 1.5 * ATR(14)
  - Short: stop = max(high of the entry bar, entry close) + 1.5 * ATR(14)
- Trailing stop (updated every bar):
  - Long: stop = max(prev_stop, close - 1.5 * ATR(14))
  - Short: stop = min(prev_stop, close + 1.5 * ATR(14))
- Take-profit: fixed target of 2 * ATR(14) from the entry price
- Exit on trend weakening:
  - Long: MACD.macd crosses below MACD.signal or EMA(50) stops rising (EMA(50) <= EMA(50)[-1])
  - Short: MACD.macd crosses above MACD.signal or EMA(50) stops falling (EMA(50) >= EMA(50)[-1])

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade 1% of capital
- Position size = floor((equity * 0.01) / stop_distance), where stop_distance is the distance from the entry price to the initial stop-loss

Data and timeframe:
- One asset, standard O/H/L/C/V fields
- Timeframe from 1 hour and above

reformulated_task:
Strategy “Fair Value Arbitrage” for a single asset (mean reversion):
- Idea: emulate a “spread to fair value” as the deviation of price from a moving fair price and profit from its convergence.
- Fair Value: EMA(50). Spread = (Close - EMA50) / EMA50.
- Volatility filter: annual volatility via ATR ≈ (ATR(14)/Close)*sqrt(252). Do not open positions if this estimate > 80% per annum. Additionally, you may require that the Bollinger Bands(20, 2) width relative to the middle be < 30% (Bandwidth = (Upper-Lower)/Middle < 0.3).
- Entry:
  • Long: Spread <= -1% and RSI(14) < 45 while satisfying the volatility filter.
  • Short: Spread >= +1% and RSI(14) > 55 while satisfying the volatility filter.
- Exit:
  • Basic: close the position when the spread converges to zero (price crossing EMA50: CrossOver(Close, EMA50)).
  • Stop-loss: -5% from the entry price.
  • Take-profit: +2% from the entry price.
- Position size: fixed fraction of capital (e.g., 2% per trade) or a fixed amount (e.g., $100,000), with optional scaling by the strength of the deviation: multiplier = min(1.5, 1 + |Spread|/0.02). Only OHLCV data and the EMA, RSI, ATR, BollingerBands, CrossOver indicators are used.

reformulated_task: Regime Trading Strategy (mean-reversion ↔ trend) on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) using Bollinger Bands and EMA. Idea: replace the Hurst exponent with a proxy — the Bollinger Bands width relative to its 200-bar average; narrow width ⇒ the market tends toward mean reversion, width expansion ⇒ the market is trending. Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), EMA(50), EMA(200), SMA(200) of the bands width. Calculation: width = (upper − lower). Regime: mean-reversion if width ≤ SMA(200, width); trend if width > SMA(200, width). Entries: mean-reversion long — in mean-reversion regime and close ≤ lower band; mean-reversion short — in mean-reversion regime and close ≥ upper band. Exits for mean-reversion: close on price crossing the middle line of the bands. Entries for trend: long — in trend regime, EMA(50) > EMA(200) and price crossing above the upper band; short — in trend regime, EMA(50) < EMA(200) and price crossing below the lower band. Exits for trend: close on the opposite crossing of the middle line. Risk management: fixed position size of 100% of capital, no stop-loss and no take-profit. Recalculate indicators on each new bar; width comparison window — 200 bars.

reformulated_task: Synthetic Straddle Open-Gap Volatility on a single asset (e.g., AAPL): trade opening volatility without options. Day filters: (1) volatility — the BollingerBands(20,2) width (Upper-Lower) must be above its EMA(50) by at least 20% (BandWidth / EMA(BandWidth,50) > 1.2); (2) gap — |Open - Close[-1]| / Close[-1] >= 1%. Entry at the open: place two OCO stop orders around the opening price — Buy Stop at Open + 0.8*ATR(14) and Sell Stop at Open - 0.8*ATR(14). One triggers — the other is canceled (straddle simulation). Initial stop: 1.5*ATR(14) from the entry price. Trailing “give-back 40%”: for longs, record the maximum since entry; exit if price <= Entry + 0.6*(PeakHigh - Entry). For shorts, record the minimum; exit if price >= Entry - 0.6*(Entry - PeakLow). If by the end of the session the position is not closed — close at market (daily horizon, equivalent to 0DTE). Position size: risk 2% of capital per trade, size = floor(0.02*Equity / (1.5*ATR(14))).

reformulated_task:
Swing-momentum strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) on OHLCV with technical filters and an ATR trailing stop:

1) Trend filter: trade only if close > SMA(200).

2) Long entry: when EMA(20) crosses EMA(50) from below to above OR RSI(14) crosses the 50 level from below to above; (optional) volume confirmation: volume > SMA(volume, 20).

3) Exit: take-profit +18% from the average entry price; ATR(14)-based trailing stop: initial stop = entry_price - max(8% of price, 2*ATR), then trail at the level max(initial stop, highest_close_since_entry - 3*ATR); or exit on trend reversal (EMA(20) crosses EMA(50) from above to below OR RSI(14) crosses 50 from above to below).

4) Position sizing and capital: keep 20% of capital in reserve; initial entry — up to 60% of capital; one-time add-on (averaging) up to +20% of capital on a 10–15% pullback from the post-entry high, if the trend conditions (close > SMA(200) and EMA20 > EMA50) remain in place; maximum position size — 80% of capital.

reformulated_task: Volatility-balanced mono-asset strategy (Long/Short) on D1: trade one available asset in both directions with ATR-based position sizing. Indicators: ATR(20) for volatility assessment and stop/take, SMA(20) for trend confirmation, BollingerBands(20, 2) for controlling abnormal deviation, CrossOver for signals. Entries: Long when Close crosses above SMA(20) (CrossOver > 0), Short when it crosses below SMA(20) (CrossOver < 0). Position size is calculated so that the risk at a 2*ATR stop does not exceed 1% of capital: size = min((Capital * 0.01) / (2*ATR), Capital / Close), optionally multiplied by a risk coefficient (e.g., 0.5); minimum 0.01 lot. Position management: Stop-loss = 2*ATR, Take-profit = 4*ATR, daily recalibration of size based on the current ATR. Alternative exit: close the position if |Close - SMA(20)| > 2*Std (price moved outside the Bollinger(20,2) bands). Maximum 1 simultaneous position.
