reformulated_task:
Long-term comparison of three proxy option-income strategies (Covered Calls, Cash-Secured Puts, Wheel) versus buy&hold on a single available asset using only OHLCV and standard Backtrader indicators. Data: one asset (e.g., AAPL). Frequency: decisions are made once per week (on Monday). Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), SMA(50), RSI(14), ATR(14).

Strategy 1 (Covered Call Proxy):
- Idea: hold the asset and “lock in” profit upon reaching the upper band, mimicking assignment on a covered call, then move to cash and wait for re-entry.
- Entry: on Mondays, if there is no position and Close > SMA(50) and RSI(14) > 50 — buy 1 position.
- Exit (analog of “call assigned”): if Close >= upper BB band or RSI >= 70 — sell the entire position.
- Protective exit: if Close <= middle BB band − 1*ATR(14) — sell.
- Cooldown before re-entry: 1 week.

Strategy 2 (Cash-Secured Put Proxy):
- Idea: “get assigned” only on drawdowns (oversold) and exit to the middle / by time, mimicking weekly-to-monthly puts.
- Entry (analog of “assignment”): on Mondays, if there is no position and (Close <= lower BB band or RSI <= 35) — buy 1 position.
- Take profit/“expiration”: exit when Close >= middle BB band or by timeout of 20 trading days (whichever comes first).
- Stop: if Close <= lower BB band − 1*ATR(14) — sell.
- Otherwise, the strategy stays in cash.

Strategy 3 (Wheel Proxy):
- Idea: CSP → CC cycle. First, wait for “assignment” on oversold conditions, then “sell covered calls,” taking profit near the upper band, and return to waiting.
- CSP stage: entry as in Strategy 2.
- After entry, the position is switched to CC mode: exit as in Strategy 1 (upper band or RSI >= 70, or stop at middle − 1*ATR).
- After exit — return to the CSP stage.

Strategy 4 (Benchmark Buy & Hold):
- Entry: on the first bar buy 1 position.
- Hold with no actions until the end of the test.

Metrics for comparison: CAGR, maximum drawdown, Sharpe/Sortino (if feasible), percent time in position, number of trades. Test period: 10+ years. Implementation: 4 separate Strategy classes in Backtrader, common data feed, trading only on Mondays (dt.weekday()==0), indicators strictly: BollingerBands, SMA, RSI, ATR.

reformulated_task: Intraday mean reversion on a single asset with a volatility filter via ATR/Bollinger and position size adjustment by time of day. A minute timeframe (1–5 minutes) is assumed. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), SMA(20), RSI(14), ATR(14), CrossOver. Entry (first 30 minutes after the open): open a long when price crosses the lower Bollinger band from below to above (CrossOver(close, bb.bot) > 0) and RSI(14) < 45, under moderate volatility: band width (bb.top - bb.bot)/bb.mid <= 0.5% and/or ATR(14)/close <= 0.8%. Position size: base = 100% of capital; morning entry target = 150% (if ATR% > 1.0%, reduce to 125%); actual size is limited by available cash/broker leverage. Take-profit: at +0.3% from the average entry price, take profit on 50% of the position. Reduction toward the end of the day: 30–60 minutes before the close, reduce the position to 50% of the base size (keep half overnight). Stop-loss: close the position if price has fallen by 2% from the day’s maximum formed after entry (trailing from the intraday high since entry). No-trade days: skip if at the open the Bollinger(20) width > 1.5% or ATR(14)/close of the previous day > 1.5%.
description: Intraday mean reversion on a single asset with a volatility filter via ATR/Bollinger and position size adjustment by time of day. A minute timeframe (1–5 minutes) is assumed. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), SMA(20), RSI(14), ATR(14), CrossOver. Entry (first 30 minutes after the open): open a long when price crosses the lower Bollinger band from below to above (CrossOver(close, bb.bot) > 0) and RSI(14) < 45, under moderate volatility: band width (bb.top - bb.bot)/bb.mid <= 0.5% and/or ATR(14)/close <= 0.8%. Position size: base = 100% of capital; morning entry target = 150% (if ATR% > 1.0%, reduce to 125%); actual size is limited by available cash/broker leverage. Take-profit: at +0.3% from the average entry price, take profit on 50% of the position. Reduction toward the end of the day: 30–60 minutes before the close, reduce the position to 50% of the base size (keep half overnight). Stop-loss: close the position if price has fallen by 2% from the day’s maximum formed after entry (trailing from the intraday high since entry). No-trade days: skip if at the open the Bollinger(20) width > 1.5% or ATR(14)/close of the previous day > 1.5%.

reformulated_task: Strategy: “Bearish Weekly Short Strategy with ATR Target” (emulation of a bear credit call spread without options). Instrument: one liquid asset (e.g., SPY or AAPL) with OHLCV. Indicators: ATR(14). Entry: open a short position with a market order every 7 trading days (if there is no open position). Position size is calculated from a fixed risk per trade: risk_per_trade (e.g., 1% of capital), size = risk_per_trade / (k * ATR), where k=2. Exit: set a bracket — take-profit at 0.5 * k * ATR below the entry price (i.e., 50% “premium” relative to risk) and stop-loss at k * ATR above the entry price. Time-based forced exit: if the position did not close by TP/SL, forcibly exit 20 trading days after entry (emulation of closing at 15–20 DTE). Assumptions: trading is conducted with a single position without layering new entries until the current one is closed; ATR is used as a volatility proxy for adaptive TP/SL levels.

reformulated_task:
Strategy "MSTR Squeeze Momentum (OHLCV proxy)" for D1. The goal is to catch momentum out of consolidation as a proxy for a market catalyst, using only price and volume.

Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), RSI(14), ATR(14), SMA on volume (SMA_vol20, SMA_vol3), CrossOver(close, bb.mid).

Entry conditions (LONG), all at the same time:
1) Volatility squeeze: Bollinger band width W = (bb.top - bb.bot) < 0.75 * SMA(W, 20) and ATR(14) < SMA(ATR(14), 20).
2) Price in a narrow range: close is between bb.mid - 0.25*W and bb.mid + 0.25*W (middle of the consolidation).
3) Oversold: RSI(14) < 35.
4) Abnormal volume: SMA(volume, 3) > 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20).
5) Momentum trigger: CrossOver(close, bb.mid) > 0 (the close crosses the middle band from below to above) on the entry day.

Exit from the position (any):
1) Take-profit: High >= entry_price + 2 * ATR(14).
2) Stop-loss: Low <= stop_level, where stop_level = min(bb.bot on the entry bar, entry_price - 1.5 * ATR(14)).
3) Close on acceleration: if Close > bb.top and RSI(14) > 50, close the position at the daily close.

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade no more than 2% of capital: size = floor(0.02 * equity / (entry_price - stop_level)).
- One trade per one squeeze cycle: after exit, re-entry is possible only after an expansion phase (W > SMA(W, 20)) and a new squeeze (condition in item 1 is met again).

Constraints:
- One instrument (e.g., MSTR) with OHLCV data and the specified indicators.
- Timeframe D1.
description:
Strategy "MSTR Squeeze Momentum (OHLCV proxy)" for D1. The goal is to catch momentum out of consolidation as a proxy for a market catalyst, using only price and volume.

Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), RSI(14), ATR(14), SMA on volume (SMA_vol20, SMA_vol3), CrossOver(close, bb.mid).

Entry conditions (LONG), all at the same time:
1) Volatility squeeze: Bollinger band width W = (bb.top - bb.bot) < 0.75 * SMA(W, 20) and ATR(14) < SMA(ATR(14), 20).
2) Price in a narrow range: close is between bb.mid - 0.25*W and bb.mid + 0.25*W (middle of the consolidation).
3) Oversold: RSI(14) < 35.
4) Abnormal volume: SMA(volume, 3) > 1.5 * SMA(volume, 20).
5) Momentum trigger: CrossOver(close, bb.mid) > 0 (the close crosses the middle band from below to above) on the entry day.

Exit from the position (any):
1) Take-profit: High >= entry_price + 2 * ATR(14).
2) Stop-loss: Low <= stop_level, where stop_level = min(bb.bot on the entry bar, entry_price - 1.5 * ATR(14)).
3) Close on acceleration: if Close > bb.top and RSI(14) > 50, close the position at the daily close.

Risk management and position sizing:
- Risk per trade no more than 2% of capital: size = floor(0.02 * equity / (entry_price - stop_level)).
- One trade per one squeeze cycle: after exit, re-entry is possible only after an expansion phase (W > SMA(W, 20)) and a new squeeze (condition in item 1 is met again).

Constraints:
- One instrument (e.g., MSTR) with OHLCV data and the specified indicators.
- Timeframe D1.

reformulated_task:
Hybrid Yield-and-Core Strategy on a Single Asset (analog of Bull Put Spreads + PMCC without options)

Idea: split capital 70/30. Use 70% for a short-term yield module on pullbacks in conditions of elevated volatility and a bullish trend with a quick exit by time/target (analog of credit put spreads). Keep 30% in a long-term trend core with periodic partial profit-taking on overbought conditions and adding on pullbacks (analog of PMCC/covered call yield without options).

Indicators and parameters:
- Trend: SMA(200), EMA(50), MACD(12,26,9)
- Volatility and deviation: BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14)
- Momentum/overbought: RSI(14)

Module 1 (70% of capital) — Yield pullback in high volatility:
Entry:
- Trend filter: close > SMA(200)
- High volatility: Bollinger Bands width (upper − lower) above its SMA(50) of width (proxy for high IV)
- Pullback: close touches or is below the lower Bollinger band, RSI < 40
- Open long on the next bar
Position sizing and risk:
- Stop: entry − 1×ATR(14)
- Take-profit: +0.7×ATR (target 70–80% of R) or a return to the middle Bollinger line, whichever comes first
- Max hold: 10 bars (approximation of closing 7–14 days before expiration)
- Risk per trade: ≤ 1% of capital via ATR-based position sizing
Exit:
- TP at target or touch of the middle band
- Stop at 1×ATR
- Time exit after 10 bars
Module constraints:
- In total, no more than 70% of capital is engaged in active positions of this module

Module 2 (30% of capital) — Trend core with periodic “yield”:
Entry and scaling the core:
- Bullish filter: EMA(50) > SMA(200) and MACD > 0
- Base entry: on EMA(50) crossing above SMA(200) or on a pullback to the middle Bollinger band while maintaining the bullish filter conditions
- Add: when price drops 10% from the 20-day high and touches the lower Bollinger band with RSI 35–45 (not exceeding the 30% capital limit)
Management and “yield” (analog of covered calls):
- When touching the upper Bollinger band and RSI > 65, sell 10–20% of the core (partial profit-taking). Buy back upon a return to the middle Bollinger band or RSI < 45
Exit from the core:
- Trailing stop: maximum since entry minus 3×ATR(14) (updated on new highs)
- Liquidate the core when EMA(50) < SMA(200) and MACD < 0 for 5 consecutive bars

Risk and capital management:
- Risk on any single trade (module 1) ≤ 1% of capital (ATR-based position sizing)
- Share of capital in the strategy ≤ 90% (keep ≥ 10% in cash)
- For module 1 in total ≤ 70% of capital, for module 2 ≤ 30%
- Do not average down losing positions in module 1; adding is allowed only for the core per the 10% pullback rule and not above the 30% limit

Timeframe: daily bars of a single asset with OHLCV

reformulated_task: Distributed Sell Strategy for a Single Asset: buy 400 shares of the selected instrument (e.g., AAPL) on the first available bar, then each time the market value of the position reaches a multiple doubling of the initial investment amount, sell 50% of the current number of shares. Calculations: initial_investment = 400 * entry_price (close on the entry bar); current value position_value = position_in_shares * current close. Sell thresholds: threshold_n = initial_investment * 2^n (n = 1, 2, 3, ...). Upon reaching threshold_n, execute a one-time sale of floor(position * 0.5), then adjust the cost basis cost_basis := cost_basis * 0.5 and move to the next threshold threshold_{n+1}. Sales are tied only to the thresholds of the total market value of the position relative to initial_investment; there are no additional filters. Corporate actions (spin-offs, splits, dividends, taxes) are not taken into account; only the cost basis for a single asset and its proportional adjustment after partial sales are tracked. Assumption: trades are executed in an integer number of shares (rounding down on sale).

reformulated_task: Momentum strategy on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with entry on a breakout above the upper Bollinger Bands and position management via a 1% trailing stop. Rules: 1) Enter a long position when the closing price crosses above the upper Bollinger Bands (period=20, deviation=2) and RSI(14) > 55. 2) On the entry day set trailing_stop_loss = entry_price * (1 - 1%/100). 3) While the position is open, on each bar update highest_price = max(previous_highest_price, high) and recalculate trailing_stop_loss = max(previous_trailing_stop_loss, highest_price * (1 - 1%/100)); the stop level may only rise. 4) Exit the position (exit_signal = 1) on the day when close <= the current trailing_stop_loss; after exiting, trailing calculations stop until the next entry. 5) Position size: 1 share/contract. 6) On days without a position trailing_stop_loss = NULL; exit_signal = 0 on all days except the exit day.

reformulated_task: Stop-and-Reverse strategy based on an ATR trailing stop (D1, 1 asset). ATR(14) and multiplier k=2.5 are used to build an adaptive SAR_ATR line, which is located below the price in an uptrend and above the price in a downtrend. Calculation: up_stop = max(prev_up_stop, close - k*ATR), down_stop = min(prev_down_stop, close + k*ATR); SAR_ATR = up_stop if close > prev_SAR_ATR, otherwise SAR_ATR = down_stop. Entries: Long when CrossOver(close, SAR_ATR) > 0 (the close crosses SAR_ATR from below to above); Short when CrossOver(close, SAR_ATR) < 0 (the close crosses from above to below). Exits/reversal: close and reverse the position on the opposite crossover. Risk management: stop-loss at the SAR_ATR level at the time of entry. Take-profit is not used.

reformulated_task: Proxy Strategy “Rolling Covered Calls” on a Single Spot Asset with OHLCV: hold a core long position and systematically “collect premium” via partial profit-taking and buybacks. Indicators: BollingerBands(20,2), RSI(14), ATR(14), SMA(volume,20). Entry: initialization — buy the core position (e.g., the entire capital or a fixed quantity, a multiple of 100). “Call sale” (analog): when close crosses above the upper Bollinger band and RSI > 60, and volume > SMA(volume,20) — sell a tranche of 20% of the current position (min. 100 shares if available). Record a “virtual strike” = the upper band and “term” = 7 bars. Rolling: if on the 7th bar the price remains above the upper band — do not buy back the tranche, roll the “strike” to the current upper band and extend the term by another 7 bars; if RSI > 60 you may add one more 20% tranche (no more than 100% total, no more than 1 tranche per day). Expiration without exercise (buyback): if before expiration the price drops below the middle Bollinger band or RSI < 45 — buy back active tranches (partially or all), restoring the core position. Forced “assignment”: if after the sale the price moves above (upper band + 1*ATR) — sell the remaining position; re-enter the core long when the price returns below the middle band and RSI < 50. Goal — regular income through cycles “sell into overbought — buy back on mean reversion”; risk — missing upside when fully sold out.

reformulated_task: Swing trend strategy on 1 asset with an SMA filter and volume confirmation: entry on a pullback to SMA(50) in the direction of the SMA(200) trend, confirmed by increased volume; exit by swing levels and risk management. Indicators: SMA(50), SMA(200), SMA volume(20), ATR(14), CrossOver. Entry conditions: (1) Trend: long — close > SMA(200); short — close < SMA(200). (2) Pullback and re-entry: long — first the price was below SMA(50) in the last 10–20 bars, then an upward CrossOver(close, SMA(50)) occurs; short — symmetrically (first above SMA(50), then a downward crossover). (3) Volume confirmation: current volume >= 1.5 × SMA(volume, 20). (4) Volatility filter: ATR(14) > SMA(ATR(14), 50) to select periods of increased activity. Exits and risk management: (1) Stop-loss: for long — below the recent significant low (the low of the last 20 bars, excluding the current one); for short — above the high of the last 20 bars. Mandatory constraint: stop distance ≤ 5% of the entry price, otherwise the signal is skipped. (2) Take-profit: at the nearest swing level (for long — the previous high over 20–30 bars, for short — the previous low over 20–30 bars). (3) Optional trailing: when profit = 1.5R is reached, move the stop to break-even. (4) Sizing: risk 1–2% of capital per trade; position size = (allowed risk in $) / (distance to stop).

reformulated_task: Scalping momentum on a single instrument (e.g., SPY) with a proxy spread and a trailing stop on 1-minute bars. Entry: buy when the price crosses upward through SMA(20) with a narrow “proxy spread” and moderate volatility. “Proxy spread” = max(High-Low of the current bar, ATR(1)) and must be ≤ 0.1% of the price (0.001 * Close), which approximately corresponds to $0.40 at a price of ~$400. The additional “near-ATM” filter is replaced by proximity to the mean: |Close - SMA(20)| / Close ≤ 0.5%. The volatility filter VIX < 20 is replaced by Bollinger Bands width: BB_width = (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid ≤ 1.5%. Time filter: trades only from 09:45 to 11:00 ET. Position size is fixed (e.g., 100 shares as an analog of 1 options contract with a multiplier of 100). Limit: maximum 5 trades per day. Exit: the trailing stop is activated after the price rises by 0.2% from entry; after activation, the stop follows the maximum with an offset of 0.05% (trailing_stop = max_since_entry * (1 - 0.0005)). Upon triggering the exit, if the “proxy spread” > 0.1% of the price, a “slippage penalty” is applied as a fixed add-on to costs: 0.05% of the price (0.0005 * Close) per share. Backtest metrics: total profit/loss, number of trades, average “proxy spread” per trade, net profit after accounting for slippage penalties.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion strategy on a single asset with a “model fair price” and a synthetic strike vector: we estimate the fair price via EMA(20), and volatility via Bollinger Bands(20, 2σ) and ATR(14). As a “strike vector,” we use levels EMA(20) ± 1σ and ± 2σ (from Bollinger), where ±2σ are undervaluation/overvaluation zones, and ±0.5σ is the zone of return to the fair price. Entries: Long when price crosses the lower BB(20,2) band from below to above (the market is “below fair” by ~2σ), Short when price crosses the upper band from above to below (the market is “above fair” by ~2σ). Filters: do not enter if ATR(14) > 1.5 * SMA(ATR(14), 60) or volume < SMA(volume,20); avoid entries on gap days (|open - close[-1]| > 1.5 * ATR(14)). Exits: close when price returns inside BB(20,0.5) (difference < ~0.5σ) or after N=20 bars (an analogue of “time to expiration”). Risk management: initial stop = EMA(20) − 2*ATR(14) for Long (and EMA(20) + 2*ATR(14) for Short); position size is chosen so that risk to the stop ≤ 5% of capital; if portfolio drawdown ≥ 10%, close all positions and stop trading.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion on a single asset via the Z-score of the price deviation from the long-term average: use BollingerBands(period=252, devfactor=2) as a proxy for the “expected” price. Z = (Close - SMA(252)) / Std(252), where Std(252) = (TopBand - Mid) / 2. Enter Long when Z <= -1.5, enter Short when Z >= +1.5, if there is no open opposite position. Exit upon reversion to the mean: Z crosses 0 (CrossOver Close and SMA(252)) or by timer after 63 bars (≈ 90 calendar days). Risk management: stop-loss 1.5% from the entry price; position size = (Capital * 0.01) / (Entry price * 0.015). Frequency — daily. Skip signals under abnormally low volatility (Std ≈ 0).

reformulated_task:
**Trading Strategy Assignment:**

**Strategy name/description:** Breakout Retest Strategy (Breakout Retest)

**Main idea:** Trading a breakout from a consolidation level with subsequent confirmation of strength through a retest of the moving average. The strategy focuses on stocks demonstrating leadership in their sectors and having strong institutional support.

**Indicators:**
*   **Moving Average (Exponential Moving Average - EMA):** Period 10. Used to determine the support level after the breakout.
*   **Breakout Level:** The highest price over the last 50 trading days prior to the current day. This level serves as the entry trigger.

**Conditions for entering a long position (when to buy):**
1.  **Breakout:** The day’s closing price must exceed the highest price over the last 50 days (breakout level).
2.  **Retest:** Within the next 5 trading days after the breakout, price must pull back to the 10-day EMA, but must not close below it.
3.  **Demand confirmation:** On the retest day (when price touches or closes near the 10-day EMA), trading volume must be above the average daily volume over the last 50 days.

**Conditions for exiting the position (when to close):**
1.  **Stop-loss:** Close the position if price closes below the 10-day EMA.
2.  **Take-profit:** Close the position upon reaching a take-profit of 10% from the entry price.

**Risk management and position sizing:**
*   Position size should be 2% of total trading capital per trade.
*   The stop-loss should be set at the level of the 10-day EMA at the time of entry into the position.

**Additional rules or filters:**
*   **Sector filter:** The strategy should be applied only to technology sector stocks, especially in the cybersecurity and artificial intelligence sub-industries.
*   **Volatility filter:** Stocks must have average volatility (ATR over 14 days) above the market average to ensure sufficient movement potential.

reformulated_task: Day trading of a single underlying asset based on the crossover of SMA(10) and SMA(20) with intraday close (analogous to Theta minimization). Indicators: SMA(10), SMA(20), ATR(14), CrossOver. Long entry: when SMA(10) crosses SMA(20) from below upward and the closing price is within 0.5*ATR of SMA(20). Short entry: when SMA(10) crosses SMA(20) from above downward and the price is within 0.5*ATR of SMA(20). Exit: opposite crossover or a stop-loss of 1% from the entry price; all positions are forcibly closed 5 minutes before the session close. Risk management: position size such that the stop-loss risk of 1% does not exceed 1% of capital (qty = floor(Equity*0.01 / (Entry*0.01))). Additional rules: trade only during the regular session (e.g., 9:30–16:00 ET); do not open new positions within 30 minutes of the close; avoid the first 5 minutes after the open.

reformulated_task: Mean-reversion strategy on a single asset with a proxy for a “probability distribution” via Bollinger Bands: “fair price” = SMA(20) (middle band), probability corridor = Bollinger Bands(20). Entries: Long when the closing price is below the lower BB(20, 2σ) band and simultaneously RSI(14) < 35 and Stochastic %K < 20; Short when the price is above the upper BB(20, 2σ) band and RSI(14) > 65 and Stochastic %K > 80. Target/exit: close the position upon touching or crossing the middle band (SMA(20)); alternative exit on deterioration of conditions — if EMA(5) crosses EMA(10) against the position (CrossOver) or RSI returns to ~50, and the price does not reach SMA(20) within a reasonable time. Stop-loss: protective stop at BB(20, 3σ) against the position or by ATR(14)*1.5 (take the greater of the two). Position size: risk per trade 2% of capital; quantity = 2% of capital / stop distance, with scaling by deviation strength: multiplier = clip(deviation beyond 1.5σ to 2.5σ, 0.5–1.5). Filters: trade only if band width (BBWidth = (Upper-Lower)/SMA(20)) is in the 3%–20% range and SMA(volume, 20) > SMA(volume, 50); skip bars with extreme noise (High-Low > 3*ATR(14)). Signal updates — once per bar (e.g., daily timeframe).

reformulated_task: Time-Series Momentum on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with periodic rebalancing. Parameters: momentum window N days (default 90), rebalancing frequency R days (default 21), target capital allocation w (default 20%). Momentum M = Close / Close[-N] - 1. On the rebalancing date: if M > 0 — open a long position sized min(w, 10%) of capital; if M < 0 — open a short position sized min(w, 10%); if |M| is close to 0 — stay out of the position. Between rebalancings: close an open position when the sign of M changes (zero crossing) and wait for the next rebalancing. At each rebalancing, all positions are closed and the signal is re-evaluated. Liquidity filter: trade only if SMA(21) of dollar volume (Close*Volume) ≥ 1,000,000 and there is at least N days of history. Backtrader indicators used: SMA(21) for average dollar volume; CrossOver(close, close(-N)) to track changes in the sign of momentum; the momentum itself is calculated as (close / close(-N) - 1) from standard fields.

reformulated_task: Spot version of the “Dividend Pitchfork Strategist” for a single asset (e.g., AAPL) with OHLCV and indicators BollingerBands(20,2), RSI(14), ATR(14), SMA(50). Idea: mimic selling puts by buying drawdowns at the lower band and taking “premium” on a return to the mean; in a strong drawdown, convert the position into a long-term “core” holding. Entry (put-selling analog): open a long position when close ≤ the lower BB(20,2) band and 25 ≤ RSI(14) ≤ 45; the position is opened only if after the trade the share of invested capital will not exceed 50% (keep ≥50% cash). Position size: calculate so that the total value of all open positions ≤ 50% of capital; protective stop at entry − 1.5×ATR(14). Exit / “premium” taking: close upon reaching the BB midline (SMA20) or upon a price increase of 1×ATR(14), whichever comes first. DTE=30 analog: maximum holding of 30 bars; if the position has been active for 25–27 bars and the target has not been reached, close. “Roll”: if on bar 25–27 the price is still at the lower band (close ≤ lower BB), close the position and immediately reopen a new one (reset the timer to 30 bars). “Execution/assignment”: if after entry the price closes below the lower BB by another 1×ATR(14) or RSI(14) < 25, convert the position into a “core” (long-term holding without a time stop) provided that the total share of capital in positions does not exceed 50%; exit the “core” when close > SMA(50) or RSI(14) > 60. Additional: do not make new entries if after opening them cash will drop below 50% of capital; the strategy uses only one asset and the listed indicators.

reformulated_task: Strategy for a single asset (e.g., AAPL): trade only on those days when the daily RSI(14) at the close of the previous day was below 30. On such days, on the 5-minute timeframe, open a long position when the price crosses above its SMA(20). Exit the position when the price crosses below SMA(20) or forcibly 15 minutes before the session close. Risk management: stop-loss 2% from the entry price, take-profit 4%, position size 5% of capital. Maximum one position (for the single asset only).

reformulated_task:
Comparison of two stock strategies on a single asset (e.g., AAPL) that mimic Cash Secured Puts and Covered Calls using only OHLCV and ATR/Bollinger/SMA/EMA/RSI indicators.

General settings:
- Indicators: ATR(14), BollingerBands(20,2), SMA(50), EMA(20), RSI(14), Volume SMA(20).
- Pseudo “strike/premium”: k = clip(ATR(14)/Close, 0.01, 0.05); “expiration” = 20 bars.
- Liquidity filter: Volume > SMA(Volume, 20).

Strategy 1 (CSP-proxy: buy-the-dip mean reversion):
- Entry: when no position, place a limit buy L = Close*(1 - k), activate only if Close < lower Bollinger band and RSI(14) < 35, and the liquidity filter is met. The trade is considered filled if Low <= L.
- Exit:
  - Take-profit: max(Entry + 0.5*k*Entry, middle Bollinger line reached — close at market on the first event).
  - Stop-loss: Entry - 1.5*ATR(14).
  - Time-out: forced close 20 bars after entry.
- Position size: 20% of capital.

Strategy 2 (CC-proxy: trend + capped upside):
- Entry: buy at market on the next bar when EMA(20) crosses SMA(50) from below to above (CrossOver) and Close > EMA(20), with the liquidity filter met.
- Exit:
  - Take-profit (imitation of a “sold call”): on touch of the upper Bollinger band or on a rise of k*Entry (whichever comes first).
  - Early exit / 50% protection: upon reaching 0.5*k*Entry, trail the stop to EMA(20) or close when Close < EMA(20).
  - Stop-loss: Close < EMA(20) - 1.0*ATR(14) or reverse CrossOver (SMA(50) crosses EMA(20) from above to below).
  - Time-out: close after 20 bars if no TP/SL.
- Position size: 100% of capital.

Testing: run the strategies separately on the same data, comparing return, drawdown, WinRate, and the average win/loss ratio.

reformulated_task:
Strategy "Sniper Pullback" (1 asset, recent-high proxy via Bollinger Bands):

Idea: Buy a sharp pullback after a recent local maximum on a single instrument.

Indicators: BollingerBands(20, 2), ATR(14), SMA(volume, 20) [optionally RSI(14) as an oversold filter].

Logic:
1) Reference high H_ref: within the last 6 bars, there must be a bar with a close above the upper Bollinger Bands(20,2). The close of that bar is H_ref.
2) Long entry condition:
   - The current Close has dropped from H_ref by 9%–13% inclusive: (H_ref - Close)/H_ref ∈ [0.09, 0.13]
   - Signal bar volume > 1.5 × SMA(volume, 20)
   - [Optional] RSI(14) < 45
3) Exit:
   - Take-profit: +5% from the entry price
   - Stop-loss: -3% from the entry price
4) Position size: fixed — 2% of capital per trade.
5) Reference point update: each time a new close above the upper BB(20,2) appears, H_ref is updated to that new close (both while waiting and during an active position — for monitoring only, without averaging).

Data: one asset with fields open, high, low, close, volume.

reformulated_task: Buy & Hold strategy on a single asset (preferably an ETF like SPY): on the first bar, buy the only available instrument with 100% of capital and hold the position until the end of the backtest (target horizon 20 years). Additional exit rule (optional): close the position if the portfolio drawdown from its maximum reaches 30%; after exiting, do not re-enter. No indicators are used. Dividends are ignored (no data). Include commissions: $0.01 per share, minimum $1 per trade.

reformulated_task: Scalping strategy on a single asset (default SNAP) on 1-minute OHLCV data. Instead of order book imbalance, use a proxy for buyer/seller “pressure”: Stochastic(5,3,3) for the close position within the range and CrossOver(%K, %D) as the trigger, RSI(3) for micro-momentum, and SMA(volume,20) as a liquidity filter. Long entry: CrossOver(%K, %D) up, %K > 80, RSI(3) > 55, current volume > 1.2 × SMA(volume,20), and ATR(14) ≥ 0.03 (adjustable threshold). Short entry: mirrored — CrossOver down, %K < 20, RSI(3) < 45, volume > 1.2 × SMA(volume,20), ATR(14) ≥ 0.03. Exit: take-profit +0.01$ from the entry price; stop-loss −0.02$; alternative exit on signal reversal (for long: %K crosses %D down or falls below 50; for short: %K crosses %D up or rises above 50) or by timeout after 3 bars. Risk management: position size from 100 to 500 shares (default 100, but no more than 500), the strategy accounts only for its own positions; optionally trade only when account balance > 25,000$ (PDT rule). Additional filters: trade only during the main session; skip the first 3 minutes; the asset must be highly liquid (SMA(volume,20) above an adjustable threshold). The symbol is set via a parameter, default 'SNAP'.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday mean-reversion strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV bars. Indicators: ATR(14) and a 20-period moving average (parameterizable as SMA or EMA). Define the reference level as the current trading day’s opening price (first 5-minute bar open). Entry rules (one position at a time): go SHORT when Close > reference + 2 * ATR(14); go LONG when Close < reference - 2 * ATR(14). Exits: for LONG, exit when Close crosses above the 20-MA or when a stop-loss triggers at 2% below the entry price; for SHORT, exit when Close crosses below the 20-MA or when a stop-loss triggers at 2% above the entry price. Use Backtrader cross detection and attach stop orders or manage stops intrabar using close-based checks. Optionally restrict trading to regular session hours and reset the reference at the start of each day.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader backtest for a 15-minute AAPL data feed (assume data is already available via a CSV or PandasData feed). Implement a custom Supertrend indicator using ATR(period=10) with standard Supertrend bands: basic_upper = HL2 + 3.0*ATR, basic_lower = HL2 - 3.0*ATR, then compute final_upper/final_lower per standard rules and derive a supertrend line that switches between the bands. Trading logic: if the Supertrend direction flips from bearish to bullish (supertrend goes from above close to below close), close any short and enter long; if it flips from bullish to bearish (supertrend goes from below close to above close), close any long and enter short. Use market orders on the next bar; allow only one position at a time (always-in-the-market reversal).

reformulated_task: Build a 5-minute intraday Donchian breakout strategy for AAPL in Backtrader that can go long and short. Indicators: DonchianChannel(period=20), ADX(period=14), ATR(period=14). Entry: go long when close[0] > DonchianChannel.top[0] AND ADX[0] > 25; go short when close[0] < DonchianChannel.bottom[0] AND ADX[0] > 25. Exits: (1) exit/close long if close[0] < DonchianChannel.bottom[0]; exit/close short if close[0] > DonchianChannel.top[0]. (2) Protective stop: on entry, set an initial stop at entry_price - stop_dist for long / entry_price + stop_dist for short, where stop_dist = max(2 * ATR[0], 0.02 * entry_price). Position sizing: risk 1% of current account equity per trade based on stop_dist (size = floor((equity * 0.01) / stop_dist)). Ensure only one position at a time and issue bracket/stop orders accordingly.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday backtest strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data. Detect a Tweezer Bottom after a decline: (1) Define decline as Close[0] < SMA(Close, 20) and the last 5 closes have a negative slope (Close[0] < Close[-5]). (2) Tweezer Bottom setup uses the prior two completed candles (bars -2 and -1): their lows are equal within a tolerance of 0.1% of price (abs(Low[-2]-Low[-1]) <= 0.001 * Close[-1]); bar -2 is bearish (Close[-2] < Open[-2]) and bar -1 is bullish (Close[-1] > Open[-1]). (3) Confirmation: require the next candle (bar 0) to close above the high of bar -1; enter long at the close of bar 0. (4) Place an initial stop-loss at 2% below the common low (min(Low[-2], Low[-1]) * 0.98) using a stop order, and update nothing thereafter. (5) Exit if stop-loss is hit OR if a bearish engulfing pattern appears while in position: bar 0 is bearish (Close[0] < Open[0]) and its body engulfs the prior body (Open[0] >= Close[-1] and Close[0] <= Open[-1]). Ensure only one position at a time and ignore new signals while in a trade.

(3) Confirmation: require the next candle (bar 0) to close above the high of bar -1; enter long at the close of bar 0. Ensure only one position at a time and ignore new signals while in a trade.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday long-only strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data. Define a bullish engulfing pattern as: bar[-1] is bearish (close[-1] < open[-1]); bar[0] is bullish (close[0] > open[0]); and the real body of bar[0] fully engulfs bar[-1]'s real body (open[0] <= close[-1] and close[0] >= open[-1]). Add a filter that requires at least 3 consecutive bearish candles immediately preceding bar[-1]. When the engulfing bar (bar[0]) closes, submit a market buy to execute on the next bar open. Set an initial protective stop-loss at 1% below the pattern low, where pattern_low = min(low[0], low[-1]) and stop_price = pattern_low * 0.99, using a stop order linked to the position. Exit only via the stop-loss or by closing any open position at the end of the trading session (e.g., on the last 15-minute bar of the day).
description: Create a Backtrader intraday long-only strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data. Define a bullish engulfing pattern as: bar[-1] is bearish (close[-1] < open[-1]); bar[0] is bullish (close[0] > open[0]); and the real body of bar[0] fully engulfs bar[-1]'s real body (open[0] <= close[-1] and close[0] >= open[-1]). Add a filter that requires at least 3 consecutive bearish candles immediately preceding bar[-1]. When the engulfing bar (bar[0]) closes, submit a market buy to execute on the next bar open. Set an initial protective stop-loss at 1% below the pattern low, where pattern_low = min(low[0], low[-1]) and stop_price = pattern_low * 0.99, using a stop order linked to the position. reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Indicators: MACD (fast=12, slow=26, signal=9) and ATR(14). Add regime detection via a 2-state Gaussian HMM (using hmmlearn) trained on a rolling window of the last 390 bars of log returns (close-to-close). Refit the HMM once per day at the session open using only past data (no lookahead), then for each bar infer the current hidden state. Define state 1 as “bull” and state 0 as “bear” by comparing each state’s mean return from the fitted model. Entry rules: go long when MACD crosses above its signal AND on the same bar the inferred HMM state transitions into the bull state; go short when MACD crosses below its signal AND on the same bar the inferred HMM state transitions into the bear state. Exit rules: close long when an opposite confirmation occurs (MACD cross down AND HMM transitions to bear); close short when MACD cross up AND HMM transitions to bull. Risk management: apply an ATR-based stop-loss using stop distance = ATR(14) * k where k = base_k * (slow_period / fast_period) with base_k=1.0 (so k=26/12). Use stop orders updated each bar while in position. Allow both long and short, position size fixed (e.g., 1 share) or percent-of-equity sizing, and no time-of-day/seasonality filters.

reformulated_task: Create an intraday Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV bars that can trade both long and short. Indicators: ADX (bt.ind.ADX) with configurable period AdxPeriod (default 14) and a filter threshold AdxThreshold (default 25). Momentum oscillator: use Momentum (bt.ind.Momentum) with configurable period MomentumPeriod (default 5), and define the signal flip using a CrossOver of the momentum line vs a zero line (bullish flip when Momentum crosses above 0, bearish flip when it crosses below 0). Entry rules: only take signals when ADX > AdxThreshold; if bullish flip and not in a position, enter long; if bearish flip and not in a position, enter short. Risk management: compute ATR (bt.ind.ATR) with a reasonable configurable period (e.g., AtrPeriod default 14) and set the initial stop distance to ATRMultiplier (default 2.0) * ATR from entry price (below entry for long, above entry for short), updating/holding the stop as specified (e.g., fixed at entry or optionally trailing if desired). Ensure all key inputs (AdxPeriod, AdxThreshold, MomentumPeriod, ATRMultiplier, and AtrPeriod) are strategy parameters.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday trend-following strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV historical data (e.g., a CSV feed). Implement a custom Hurst Exponent indicator (lookback=100) and use it as a trend filter: only allow trades when Hurst > 0.55 (trend/persistence). Use Parabolic SAR with af=0.02 and afmax=0.2 for signals. Entry rules: (1) Go long when Close crosses above PSAR AND Hurst > 0.55; (2) Go short when Close crosses below PSAR AND Hurst > 0.55. Exit rules: close/reverse on opposite PSAR cross. Additionally, maintain a protective stop updated each bar: compute ATR(14); for longs set stop_price = max(current_psar, entry_price - 2.0 * ATR); for shorts set stop_price = min(current_psar, entry_price + 2.0 * ATR). Use stop orders (or manual close when price breaches stop) and update them as PSAR/ATR change. Trade both directions. Use a 'medium risk' position sizing rule such as risking 1% of current equity per trade based on the distance between entry price and stop_price, with a cap on maximum position size if needed.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday backtest strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV historical data, trading both long and short with one position at a time (no pyramiding). Indicators: SMA(20) on close, ATR(14). Entries: if flat, enter long at the next bar open when the prior bar close crosses above SMA(20); enter short when the prior bar close crosses below SMA(20). Exits: close the long when close crosses below SMA(20) OR when a ratcheting trailing stop is hit, where trailing stop distance = 3 * ATR(14). For long: trail_stop = max(previous_trail_stop, highest_close_since_entry - 3*ATR); for short: trail_stop = min(previous_trail_stop, lowest_close_since_entry + 3*ATR). Execute stop exits as stop orders updated each bar (or equivalent logic in next()). Ensure the trailing stop only moves in the trade’s favor. Use default sizer (e.g., fixed size) and include commission/slippage placeholders.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader intraday volume-climax reversal strategy for AAPL on 15-minute OHLCV data. Indicators: SMA on close (trend filter) with period=50, SMA on volume with period=20, ATR period=14.

Definitions:
1) Prior trend direction: Uptrend if close > SMA50 and SMA50 is rising (SMA50[0] > SMA50[-1]); downtrend if close < SMA50 and SMA50 is falling (SMA50[0] < SMA50[-1]).
2) Strong directional move into potential climax: In an uptrend, the last N=6 bars cumulative return > +R=1.5*ATR(14) (or close[0] - close[-6] > 1.5*ATR). In a downtrend, close[-6] - close[0] > 1.5*ATR.
3) Blow-off (climax) bar: current bar volume > 2.0 * SMA(volume,20) AND bar range (high-low) > 1.2 * ATR(14) AND bar closes in the trend direction (uptrend: close near high, e.g., (high-close)/(high-low) < 0.25; downtrend: (close-low)/(high-low) < 0.25).
4) Pullback setup after a blow-off bar: After a blow-off bar is detected, wait up to M=8 bars for a pullback against the prior trend of at least retrace=0.5 * ATR(14) from the blow-off close (uptrend: price drops by >=0.5*ATR; downtrend: price rises by >=0.5*ATR).

Entries (countertrend):
- If prior trend was uptrend and a blow-off bar occurred, enter SHORT on the first bar that closes below the low of the prior bar after the pullback condition is met.
- If prior trend was downtrend and a blow-off bar occurred, enter LONG on the first bar that closes above the high of the prior bar after the pullback condition is met.

Risk management:
- Fixed stop-loss at 2% from entry price (short: entry*1.02; long: entry*0.98). No position sizing constraints beyond standard Backtrader order sizing.

Exits:
- Exit immediately on stop-loss.
- Otherwise, for a SHORT taken after an uptrend climax: exit if price makes a new high relative to the blow-off bar high (close > blowoff_high) AND volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume,20) (trend resumption with elevated volume).
- For a LONG taken after a downtrend climax: exit if price makes a new low relative to the blow-off bar low (close < blowoff_low) AND volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume,20).

Implementation notes: Use Backtrader built-in indicators (SMA, ATR, Highest/Lowest if helpful) and store state variables for detected blow-off bar (timestamp/index, high/low/close) and whether pullback has occurred within M bars.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 5-minute bars that trades long and short using a clearly defined volatility-trend signal and Bollinger-style bands on that signal:

1) Compute ATR = ATR(period=10).
2) Compute a trend baseline MA = EMA(close, period=20).
3) Define the volatility-trend indicator (signal) as: signal = close - MA.
4) Compute signal_mean = SMA(signal, period=20) and signal_std = StdDev(signal, period=20).
5) Define upper_band = signal_mean + 2*signal_std and lower_band = signal_mean - 2*signal_std.

Entry (breakout + expansion):
- Go long when signal crosses above upper_band AND abs(signal - signal[-1]) > 1.0*signal_std (expansion filter).
- Go short when signal crosses below lower_band AND abs(signal - signal[-1]) > 1.0*signal_std.

Risk management (always place stop):
- For long entries, place an initial stop-loss at entry_price - 3*ATR.
- For short entries, place an initial stop-loss at entry_price + 3*ATR.

Exit:
- Exit long when signal crosses below signal_mean.
- Exit short when signal crosses above signal_mean.

Use default settings ATR period=10 and ATR multiplier=3; allow parameters to be configurable. Implement using Backtrader built-in indicators (ATR, EMA, SMA, StdDev, CrossOver) and bracket/stop orders.

reformulated_task: Backtest an intraday strategy on AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV candles in Backtrader. Trend confirmation (upswing filter): require EMA(20) > EMA(50) and EMA(20) slope positive (EMA20[0] > EMA20[-1]) to confirm an upswing context. Resistance filter: define resistance as the highest high of the prior 20 bars (excluding current bar). Three Black Crows detection (custom logic): after an upswing, detect three consecutive bearish candles where (1) each candle closes below its open, (2) each candle opens within the real body of the prior candle (between prior open and prior close), and (3) closes are progressively lower (close1 > close2 > close3). Entry: on the close of the 3rd bearish candle, if close is within 0.5% of the defined resistance (i.e., close >= 0.995 * resistance), then enter short; if currently long, close the long instead of opening a new long. Risk management: set stop-loss at pattern_high * 1.02 where pattern_high is the maximum high across the three-candle pattern; place a stop order (or implement manual stop) and also exit the short if any bar closes above pattern_high. Additional exit: exit the short if a bullish reversal occurs defined as RSI(14) crossing above 50 OR a bullish engulfing candle forms. Use a single position at a time (no pyramiding).

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 15-minute OHLCV data to trade a Double Top reversal.

Definitions:
1) Swing High (peak): bar i is a peak if High[i] is the maximum over a symmetric window of N=2 bars on each side (High[i] > High[i-1..i-2] and High[i] >= High[i+1..i+2]).
2) Double Top: identify two consecutive swing highs P1 and P2 such that:
   - P2 occurs at least 5 bars after P1,
   - abs(High[P2] - High[P1]) / max(High[P1], High[P2]) <= 0.02.
3) Bearish confirmation candle: the bar immediately after P2 (index P2+1) must have Close < Open.

Trading rules:
- Entry: enter short at the close of the confirmation candle (on bar P2+1 close).
- Stop-loss: initial stop at 1% above the higher of High[P1] and High[P2].
- Exit: close the short if stop-loss is hit; otherwise exit at end of the trading day (last bar of the session) to keep it intraday.

Constraints:
- Only one open position at a time.
- Use Backtrader data feed; no external APIs. Assume data is already loaded as a 15-minute timeframe feed for AAPL.

- Exit: close the short if stop-loss is hit; otherwise exit at end of the trading day (last bar of the session) to keep it intraday.

Constraints:
- Only one open position at a time.
- Use Backtrader data feed; no external APIs. Assume data is already loaded as a 15-minute timeframe feed for AAPL.

reformulated_task: Backtest an intraday reversal strategy on AAPL 15-minute OHLCV data in Backtrader using RSI(14) failure swing logic. Long setup: (1) record a first RSI trough where RSI < 30 (rsi_low1), (2) after RSI rises above 30 at least once, look for a second RSI trough where RSI < 30 but rsi_low2 > rsi_low1 (higher low), (3) enter long on the next bar where RSI crosses above 30. Short setup: (1) record a first RSI peak where RSI > 70 (rsi_high1), (2) after RSI falls below 70 at least once, look for a second RSI peak where RSI > 70 but rsi_high2 < rsi_high1 (lower high), (3) enter short on the next bar where RSI crosses below 70. Risk management: use a fixed 2% stop-loss from entry price (long stop = entry*0.98, short stop = entry*1.02). Exits: close longs when RSI crosses above 70; close shorts when RSI crosses below 30 (in addition to stop-loss). Only one position at a time; ignore new signals while in a trade.

reformulated_task: Intraday strategy for AAPL on 5-minute historical data that trades both directions using a MACD crossover signal confirmed by a volume spike. Use MACD (fast=12, slow=26, signal=9) and only take trades when the MACD line crosses the signal line in the trade direction while the current bar's volume is at least twice the 20-bar average volume. Go long on bullish crosses with the volume spike and short on bearish crosses. Manage risk with ATR-based stops (period 14) placed at approximately 2× ATR from entry. Exit or reverse position when the opposite MACD/volume signal occurs. This is a trend-following, medium-risk approach.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday breakout strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Compute an intraday VWAP that resets at the start of each regular trading session (e.g., 09:30 US/Eastern). Entry rules: (1) Long: if the previous N bars (parameter, default N=3) all closed below VWAP and the current bar closes above VWAP, submit a market buy on the next bar open. (2) Short: if the previous N bars all closed above VWAP and the current bar closes below VWAP, submit a market sell on the next bar open. Exit rules (symmetric): close the position when price closes back across VWAP in the opposite direction (long exits on close below VWAP; short exits on close above VWAP) or when a protective stop-loss is hit. Define stop-loss as a fixed percentage from entry price (parameter, default 0.5%) implemented via stop orders (or checked intra-bar using high/low). Ensure only one position at a time and no new entries until the current position is closed. Include parameters for N and stop-loss percentage.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV bars. Define the opening range as the high/low of the first 4 bars of each trading day (first 60 minutes). Entry: go long when close breaks above the opening-range high and relative volume is high; go short when close breaks below the opening-range low and relative volume is high. Define relative volume as (current bar volume) / SMA(volume, 20) and require it to be > 1.5. Use a trailing stop-loss implemented via Backtrader StopTrail orders with trail amount = 1.5 * ATR(14). Exit: close the position if RSI(14) crosses below 50 for longs (above 50 for shorts) (momentum stall) OR if an opposite opening-range breakout signal occurs. Only take one position at a time and ignore signals outside regular market hours represented in the data.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 2-hour or 4-hour bars. Trades may only be opened when the bar timestamp time is between 07:00 and 17:00 in the data feed's timezone (assume datetimes are already in the desired 'chart time'; no external timezone conversion). Define candle body = abs(close - open). Compute SMA(20) of candle body and SMA(20) of volume. Long entry: close > open AND body >= 1.5 * SMA(body,20) AND volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume,20) AND no open position. Short entry (if enabled): close < open AND body >= 1.5 * SMA(body,20) AND volume >= 1.5 * SMA(volume,20) AND no open position. Exit rule: close any open long on a valid short signal; close any open short on a valid long signal. No other exits (no manual exits, no TP/SL).

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data. Compute a 20-period SMA on the close as a trend filter: only take longs when close > SMA and only take shorts when close < SMA. Define swing levels using a rolling 5-bar lookback (no future bars): on each bar, set swing_high = highest(high[-5:-1]) and swing_low = lowest(low[-5:-1]) (i.e., the highest/lowest of the prior 5 completed bars, excluding the current bar). Entry: if not in position, enter long when the close crosses above swing_high; enter short when the close crosses below swing_low. Exit: while long, exit when the close crosses below the current rolling swing_low (updated each bar); while short, exit when the close crosses above the current rolling swing_high. Use market orders (execute on next bar) and do not place separate stop-loss or take-profit orders. Allow both long and short trading.

reformulated_task: Intraday strategy for AAPL on 5-minute historical bars that fades volatility extremes. Calculate a 14-period Average True Range (ATR), its 20-period SMA, and its 20-period standard deviation. Enter long when ATR is below (ATR_SMA - 2 * ATR_StdDev) AND the close is below the 20-bar SMA. Enter short when ATR is above (ATR_SMA + 2 * ATR_StdDev) AND the close is above the 20-bar SMA. Exit long when ATR crosses above its SMA. Exit short when ATR crosses below its SMA. Apply a percent-based stop-loss to all positions. A medium-risk mean reversion setup.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on a 15-minute data feed implementing monthly seasonality using only price history (no external data). On the first bar of each new calendar month, compute the historical average return for that same calendar month (e.g., for January use prior Januarys only) using only completed months strictly before the current month to avoid lookahead. Define each monthly return as (last close of the month / first open of the month) - 1. If the historical average for the current month is > 0, target a long position; if < 0, target a short position; if == 0 or insufficient history (e.g., fewer than N prior occurrences), stay flat. Enter (or reverse) at the first bar of the month to match the new seasonal bias. Hold the position until the month ends unless (a) the next month starts and the seasonal bias flips, in which case exit/reverse on that first bar, or (b) a 2% stop-loss from the entry price is hit intramonth (for longs: price <= entry*0.98; for shorts: price >= entry*1.02), in which case exit immediately and remain flat until the next month’s signal. Ensure no lookahead and handle month transitions on intraday bars.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for 15-minute AAPL data. Indicators: DonchianChannel(period=20) using high/low bands; ATR(period=14) for volatility. Add a seasonal filter as either (A) an additional data line named `seasonal` provided in the data feed (range -1 to +1), or (B) a simple custom indicator that outputs a deterministic seasonal score (e.g., sine function of day-of-year) if no external line is provided. Entry rules (confirmed on bar close): go long when `close[0] > donchian.dch[0]` AND `seasonal[0] > 0.5`; go short when `close[0] < donchian.dcl[0]` AND `seasonal[0] < -0.5`. Exits: reverse/close on the next opposite confirmed signal. Risk management: maintain an ATR-based trailing stop with distance = atr_mult * ATR; for long positions, trail stop at max(previous_stop, close - atr_mult*ATR); for short positions, trail stop at min(previous_stop, close + atr_mult*ATR). Use stop orders or manual close when the stop is hit on bar close. Include parameters: donchian_period=20, atr_period=14, atr_mult (e.g., 3.0), seasonal_long_thresh=0.5, seasonal_short_thresh=-0.5.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL (daily bars from a local CSV or any Backtrader-compatible feed). Implement a custom indicator 'TDP' that outputs a 0-100 oscillator: for each bar, compute the percentile rank of the current close within the last N bars (e.g., N=100) using exponential time-decay weights w_k = exp(-decay*k) for k=0..N-1 (k=0 is most recent), then scale to 0..100. Smooth TDP with an EMA(22) to produce 'tdp_ema'. Trading rules: Long entry when tdp_ema crosses up above 30 (oversold) OR crosses up above 15 (extreme oversold). Short entry when tdp_ema crosses down below 70 (overbought) OR crosses down below 85 (extreme overbought). Optional divergence filter: bullish divergence for long if close makes a lower low over the last D bars (e.g., D=20) while tdp_ema makes a higher low over the same window; bearish divergence for short if close makes a higher high while tdp_ema makes a lower high. Exits: for long, close when tdp_ema crosses down below 50 OR crosses up above 70 (opposite threshold); for short, close when tdp_ema crosses up above 50 OR crosses down below 30. Add a stop loss per trade (choose one and implement): fixed percent stop (e.g., 5% from entry) or ATR-based stop (e.g., 2 * ATR(14)); apply to both long and short. Include parameters for N, decay, EMA period, thresholds, divergence window D, and stop settings.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV bars. Indicators: Bollinger Bands on close (period=20, devfactor=2). Define Bollinger Bandwidth as (BB.top - BB.bot) / BB.mid. Compute BW_SMA = SMA(Bandwidth, period=20) and BW_STD = StdDev(Bandwidth, period=20). Define BW_Z = (Bandwidth - BW_SMA) / BW_STD.

Entry logic (define 'reversal' precisely):
- Long entry when BW_Z was > +2 on the prior bar and now crosses down to <= +2 AND Bandwidth is decreasing (Bandwidth[0] < Bandwidth[-1]).
- Short entry when BW_Z was < -2 on the prior bar and now crosses up to >= -2 AND Bandwidth is increasing (Bandwidth[0] > Bandwidth[-1]).

Exit logic:
- Close any open position when BW_Z crosses 0 (mean reversion to average volatility) OR when abs(BW_Z) < 0.5.

Risk management:
- Use ATR(14) with stop distance = 2 * ATR from entry price (for long: entry - 2*ATR; for short: entry + 2*ATR). Allow both long and short trades; only one position at a time.

reformulated_task: Design a Backtrader backtestable short-term mean-reversion strategy on 5-minute AAPL OHLCV data using an intraday session VWAP (reset cumulative PV and V at each new trading day). Compute VWAP slope as the 20-bar difference: slope = VWAP - VWAP(-20). Compute the 'typical range' of slope as the 20-bar standard deviation of slope: slope_std = StdDev(slope, period=20). Define extreme thresholds as +/- (ThresholdMultiplier=2) * slope_std. Long setup: if slope was below -threshold on the prior bar and slope is now increasing (slope > slope(-1)), enter long. Short setup: if slope was above +threshold on the prior bar and slope is now decreasing (slope < slope(-1)), enter short. Place a protective stop-loss at StopLossPercent=2% from entry price (use stop orders or manual stop logic). Define VWAP baseline as a 20-period SMA of VWAP: vwap_base = SMA(VWAP, period=20). Exit/close long when VWAP crosses above vwap_base (mean reversion achieved); exit/close short when VWAP crosses below vwap_base. Allow both long and short trades; no additional filters.

Implement the protective stop as a stop order placed at entry using a parameterized stop distance (e.g., stop_pct of entry price). Trade one position at a time (no pyramiding), allow both long and short, and expose parameters: sma_period=20, stop_pct (optional, 0 disables).

Include a stop-loss order set at 2% below the entry price. Use all available capital for position sizing. Add a 20-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) for analysis.

reformulated_task: Short-term breakout strategy for AAPL on 5-minute historical data using the ADX indicator (period 14) and its rate of change (ROC) over 20 bars. Enter long when the ROC of ADX is above its 20-bar simple moving average by more than two standard deviations (calculated over 20 bars). Enter short when the ROC of ADX is below its 20-bar simple moving average by more than two standard deviations. Set a stop loss at 2% from entry price. Exit when the ROC of ADX crosses back to its 20-bar simple moving average.

reformulated_task: Create an intraday mean-reversion Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 5-minute bars. Compute a session VWAP that resets at the start of each trading day: VWAP = cumulative(typical_price * volume) / cumulative(volume), where typical_price = (high+low+close)/3. Use Williams %R with period=14. Entry rules: (1) Go long when close is below session VWAP AND Williams %R crosses below -80. (2) Go short when close is above session VWAP AND Williams %R crosses above -20. Exit rules: (1) Close long when close crosses above session VWAP. (2) Close short when close crosses below session VWAP. Risk management: On entry, place a stop-loss such that the stop is at most 2.0 price points away from the entry price, and also not farther than 2.0 points from the entry-time VWAP in the adverse direction; implement as a fixed stop (not trailing) using the entry bar’s VWAP value. Use one open position at a time (no pyramiding).

reformulated_task: Trading strategy for AAPL using 5-minute historical bars. The strategy uses three Simple Moving Averages (SMA) with periods 5, 20, and 50. It enters a long position when the 5-period SMA is above the 20-period SMA and the 20-period SMA is above the 50-period SMA, provided no existing long position is held. It enters a short position when the 5-period SMA is below the 20-period SMA and the 20-period SMA is below the 50-period SMA, provided no existing short position is held. Long positions are exited when the 5-period SMA crosses below the 20-period SMA or the price drops by 2% from the entry price. Short positions are exited when the 5-period SMA crosses above the 20-period SMA or the price rises by 2% from the entry price. The strategy uses market orders and commits the full available capital for each position.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Indicators: StochasticFull with period=14, period_dfast=3, period_dslow=3 (use %K and %D), and ATR(period=14) for sizing. Entry rules: (1) Go long when %K was below 20 on the prior bar and a bullish crossover occurs (%K crosses above %D). (2) Go short when %K was above 80 on the prior bar and a bearish crossover occurs (%K crosses below %D). Only one position at a time; if an opposite entry signal appears, close the current position first, then enter the new one on the next bar. Stop-loss: place a fixed stop at 2% from entry price (long stop = entry_price * 0.98; short stop = entry_price * 1.02). Exit rules (besides stop): close a long when %K crosses down through 50; close a short when %K crosses up through 50. Position sizing: risk a fixed fraction of equity per trade (e.g., risk_per_trade = 1% of current broker value). Compute size = floor((equity * risk_per_trade) / stop_distance_dollars), where stop_distance_dollars = abs(entry_price - stop_price); optionally cap size based on available cash/margin. Use Backtrader bracket orders (market entry + stop) or manage stops manually each bar.

reformulated_task: Intraday mean-reversion strategy for AAPL using 5-minute historical OHLCV data with Bollinger Bands (period 20, deviation 2), RSI, and ATR filters. Go short when price closes above the upper Bollinger Band and RSI is above 70. Go long when price closes below the lower Bollinger Band and RSI is below 30. Use a volatility-based stop loss set at 2 times the 14-period ATR from the entry price. Take profit when price re-enters the Bollinger Band channel (between upper and lower bands) or when an opposing entry signal occurs. Strategy works both long and short.

reformulated_task:
Create an intraday Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data and a separate 15-minute sentiment data series aligned by datetime (sentiment is a single value per bar). Indicators: Donchian Channel with period=20 (upper/lower bands), ATR period=14, and a 20-period SMA of the sentiment series. Entry rules (trend-following breakout):
- Go LONG when Close crosses above the Donchian upper band AND sentiment > sentiment_SMA20.
- Go SHORT when Close crosses below the Donchian lower band AND sentiment < sentiment_SMA20.
Risk management: On entry, set a stop-loss at entry_price - (2 * ATR) for long and entry_price + (2 * ATR) for short. Use a sentiment multiplier of 2 to scale position size based on sentiment strength: size_multiplier = 1 + 2 * abs((sentiment - sentiment_SMA20) / max(abs(sentiment_SMA20), 1e-6)); position_size = base_size * size_multiplier.
Exits: Close the position when an opposite entry signal occurs or when the stop-loss is hit. Only one position at a time (no pyramiding).

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader mean-reversion strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Compute RSI(14) and a 20-period SMA of RSI. Also compute a 20-period standard deviation of RSI. Define an upper band = RSI_SMA20 + 2 * RSI_STD20 and a lower band = RSI_SMA20 - 2 * RSI_STD20. Long setup: when RSI falls below the lower band and then crosses up above RSI_SMA20, enter long. Short setup: when RSI rises above the upper band and then crosses down below RSI_SMA20, enter short. Exit: close the position when RSI crosses the RSI_SMA20 in the opposite direction (i.e., for long, RSI crosses down below RSI_SMA20; for short, RSI crosses up above RSI_SMA20). Add a protective stop-loss at 2% from entry price (for long: stop = entry * 0.98; for short: stop = entry * 1.02) using bracket orders or an equivalent mechanism. Ensure only one position at a time.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 30-minute OHLCV data that approximates Ichimoku signals using SMAs. Compute Tenkan=SMA(9), Kijun=SMA(26), SpanB=SMA(52), and define SpanA=(Tenkan+Kijun)/2 (no forward shift). Define the cloud top as cloud_top=max(SpanA, SpanB) and cloud_bottom=min(SpanA, SpanB). Long entry: when Tenkan crosses above Kijun and close > cloud_top. Short entry: when Tenkan crosses below Kijun and close < cloud_bottom. Exits: close long if Tenkan crosses below Kijun or if price hits a stop-loss at the current Kijun level (implement as a trailing stop updated each bar to the latest Kijun). Close short on opposite cross or if price hits a stop-loss at the current Kijun level. Use built-in bt.indicators.SMA and bt.indicators.CrossOver; use bracket orders or manual stop management to implement the Kijun-based stop. Include position sizing (e.g., fixed stake) and ensure no overlapping long/short positions.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday breakout strategy for AAPL using 15-minute bars from a historical data feed (e.g., CSV). Use a Donchian Channel with a configurable period (default 20). Define breakout as: go long when the close crosses above the Donchian upper band; go short when the close crosses below the Donchian lower band. Require volume confirmation: current volume must be greater than the prior bar's volume and greater than an SMA of volume (default 20) to enter. Exit rules: for long positions, close the position when the close falls back below the Donchian upper band OR when volume falls below the volume SMA; for short positions, close when the close rises back above the Donchian lower band OR when volume falls below the volume SMA. Risk management: place a stop-loss on entry; for longs, set stop at (Donchian upper band at entry) * (1 - 0.02); for shorts, set stop at (Donchian lower band at entry) * (1 + 0.02). Include parameters for channel period, volume SMA period, and stop percent.

reformulated_task: Intraday 15-minute strategy for AAPL using Backtrader. Indicators: Williams %R(14), SMA(9), SMA(26), SMA(52). Define the 'MA cloud' as the band between cloud_top = max(SMA9, SMA26, SMA52) and cloud_bot = min(SMA9, SMA26, SMA52). Long entry: Williams %R crosses below -80 AND close > cloud_top AND SMA9 crosses above SMA26. Short entry: Williams %R crosses above -20 AND close < cloud_bot AND SMA9 is below SMA26 (or SMA9 crosses below SMA26 if using symmetric crossover). Exits: For a long, close the position when close < cloud_bot. For a short, close when close > cloud_top. Dynamic stop: use a trailing stop based on SMA26 with a fixed offset; for longs set stop_price = SMA26 * (1 - stop_pct), for shorts stop_price = SMA26 * (1 + stop_pct), updated each bar in the trade direction only (i.e., stop only moves up for longs and only moves down for shorts). Include stop_pct as a parameter (e.g., default 0.005 = 0.5%). No additional filters.

reformulated_task: Build an intraday (5-minute) trend-following strategy for AAPL in Backtrader. Define the volatility measure as a 20-bar Standard Deviation of close-to-close returns (or alternatively StdDev of close; choose one and state it explicitly) and compute it as a single indicator line. Run a stochastic oscillator on this volatility line using StochasticGeneric with parameters: period=14, period_dfast=3 (%K smoothing), period_dslow=3 (%D smoothing). Long entry: when %K crosses above %D AND %K was below 20 on the prior bar (i.e., crossover after leaving oversold) AND the volatility line is rising (volatility[0] > volatility[-1]). Short entry: when %K crosses below %D AND %K was above 80 on the prior bar (crossover after leaving overbought) AND the volatility line is falling (volatility[0] < volatility[-1]). Risk management: place an initial stop-loss at 2 * ATR(14) from the entry price (below for longs, above for shorts) using a stop or bracket order. Exit any open position on the opposite stochastic crossover signal or if the ATR stop is hit.

reformulated_task: PulseWave strategy for AAPL in Backtrader. Data feed must include OHLCV with Volume. Indicators: MACD(fast=12, slow=26, signal=9) and RSI(period=14). Define VWAP explicitly as a rolling VWAP over N bars (default N=20): VWAP = Sum(typical_price * volume, N) / Sum(volume, N), where typical_price = (high+low+close)/3. Entry (long): when close > VWAP AND MACD line crosses above MACD signal (bullish crossover) AND RSI < 70. reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday reversal strategy for AAPL using a provided 5-minute OHLCV data feed (no live API). Define an outside bar as: current high > prior high AND current low < prior low. Bullish outside bar: outside bar AND close > open. Bearish outside bar: outside bar AND close < open. Trend context filter: 'after a decline' means the last N=6 closes are lower than the close N bars ago (close[0] < close[-6]); 'after a rally' means close[0] > close[-6]. Entry: if not in position, go long on the close of a bullish outside bar that occurs after a decline; set an initial stop-loss at (outside_bar_low - 1 tick). If not in position, go short on the close of a bearish outside bar that occurs after a rally; set an initial stop-loss at (outside_bar_high + 1 tick). Position sizing: size so that (entry_price - stop_price) * size <= 1% of current portfolio value (use broker.getvalue()). Exit: for long positions, exit at market if price closes below the outside bar low (or stop-loss triggers); for short positions, exit at market if price closes above the outside bar high (or stop-loss triggers). Use bracket orders (entry + stop) and ensure only one open trade at a time. Assume regular US equity session and ignore signals outside market hours.

reformulated_task: Create an intraday 15-minute Backtrader strategy for AAPL. Indicators: Bollinger Bands (period=20, devfactor=2, based on close) and a trend filter defined as an EMA(period=50) on close. Long entry: if Close crosses above the Bollinger upper band AND Close > EMA50, then enter long (only if not already long). Short entry: if Close crosses below the Bollinger lower band AND Close < EMA50, then enter short (only if not already short). Exits use EMA50 as a dynamic trailing stop: exit long when Close crosses below EMA50; exit short when Close crosses above EMA50. reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Expose Bollinger parameters, ATR period, ATR average lookback, and ATR stop multiplier as strategy parameters.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 15-minute OHLCV data that trades long and short using an ATR-based trailing stop (Chandelier Exit) plus an ADX filter. Indicators: ATR(14), ADX(14). Parameters: lookback_n=22 bars for highest/lowest, atr_mult=3.0, adx_threshold=25. Compute trailing stop lines:
- LongStopRaw = Highest(high, lookback_n) - atr_mult * ATR(14)
- ShortStopRaw = Lowest(low, lookback_n) + atr_mult * ATR(14)
Trail (ratchet) them:
- LongStop = max(LongStop[1], LongStopRaw) when in/eligible for long; otherwise initialize to LongStopRaw
- ShortStop = min(ShortStop[1], ShortStopRaw) when in/eligible for short; otherwise initialize to ShortStopRaw
Entries (only if ADX(14) > adx_threshold):
- Enter long when close crosses above LongStop.
- Enter short when close crosses below ShortStop.
Exits:
- Exit long when close crosses below LongStop (or place a stop order at LongStop updated each bar).
- Exit short when close crosses above ShortStop (or place a stop order at ShortStop updated each bar).
Allow position reversals (close and flip) on opposite signals. Implement fully in Backtrader using built-in ATR/ADX and custom lines for the trailing stops.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader mean-reversion strategy for AAPL on 5-minute bars using Stochastic (StochPeriod=14, StochKPeriod=3, StochDPeriod=3). Use %K as the signal line. Compute a 20-bar SMA and 20-bar standard deviation of %K, and define a z-score: z = (%K - SMA20) / STD20. Also compute a 20-bar slope of %K using linear regression slope over the last 20 bars (or equivalently a dedicated slope indicator). Entry logic: (1) Go LONG when z < -2.0 and the slope of %K turns positive (current slope > 0 and previous slope <= 0). (2) Go SHORT when z > +2.0 and the slope of %K turns negative (current slope < 0 and previous slope >= 0). Exit logic: close LONG when %K crosses above 50 (or z >= 0), and close SHORT when %K crosses below 50 (or z <= 0). Risk management: for every entry, place a protective stop-loss at 2% from entry price (long stop = entry*0.98, short stop = entry*1.02). Ensure the strategy allows only one position at a time and uses bracket orders (or an attached stop order) to enforce the stop-loss.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on OHLCV data. On each bar, compute Fibonacci retracement levels between the highest high and lowest low over the last 200 bars (define swing high = Highest(high, 200), swing low = Lowest(low, 200); if swing high occurs before swing low, treat the move as uptrend and compute retracements from swing low to swing high; otherwise compute from swing high to swing low accordingly). Define the golden pocket as the zone between the 0.618 and 0.786 retracement levels. Generate a long entry signal when: (1) price (close or low) first goes below the 0.618 level, (2) later touches or crosses the 0.786 level (low <= level for a long setup), and (3) subsequently closes back above the 0.618 level. Use a state machine so the sequence must occur in order and only once per swing range. For exits, make the backtest deterministic by closing the position when a new N-bar local high is reached after entry (e.g., close >= Highest(high, N) with N=20) or use a trailing stop as an alternative if preferred. Recompute Fibonacci levels dynamically each bar based on the 200-bar range.

Long entry: when WR crosses up above the oversold threshold (-80) and the entry filter is true. Short entry: when WR crosses down below the overbought threshold (-20) and the entry filter is true. Use one position at a time (no pyramiding).

reformulated_task: Develop a short-term mean-reversion swing strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data in Backtrader. Compute EMA20 of close. Compute the 20-bar simple moving average of EMA20 (EMA_mean = SMA(EMA20, period=20)) and the 20-bar standard deviation of EMA20 (EMA_std = StdDev(EMA20, period=20)). Define upper_band = EMA_mean + 2*EMA_std and lower_band = EMA_mean - 2*EMA_std. Long setup: if EMA20 was below lower_band on the prior bar and crosses back above lower_band on the current bar, enter long at market. Short setup: if EMA20 was above upper_band on the prior bar and crosses back below upper_band on the current bar, enter short at market. Attach a protective stop-loss at 2% from entry price (long stop = entry_price*0.98; short stop = entry_price*1.02). Exit: close the position when EMA20 crosses EMA_mean (i.e., returns to its average). Strategy should support both long and short and only have one position open at a time.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader intraday trend-following strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Implement (or use if available) an intraday VWAP indicator that resets at each new trading day/session: VWAP = cumulative(sum(typical_price * volume)) / cumulative(sum(volume)), where typical_price = (high + low + close)/3. Use ATR (built-in) for volatility-based stops.

Parameters (user-adjustable):
- atr_period (default 14)
- atr_mult (default 2.0) for ATR stop distance
- stop_pct (default 0.01) fixed percent stop distance
- use_atr_stop (bool, default True)
- use_pct_stop (bool, default True)
- stake/position sizing (default fixed size)

Signals:
- Enter LONG when close crosses above VWAP (CrossOver(close, vwap) > 0) and no open position.
- Enter SHORT when close crosses below VWAP (CrossOver(close, vwap) < 0) and no open position.
- Exit LONG when close crosses below VWAP (opposite signal).
- Exit SHORT when close crosses above VWAP (opposite signal).

Risk management:
- On entry, record entry price.
- Maintain stop levels each bar:
  - For LONG: atr_stop = entry_price - atr_mult * ATR; pct_stop = entry_price * (1 - stop_pct).
  - For SHORT: atr_stop = entry_price + atr_mult * ATR; pct_stop = entry_price * (1 + stop_pct).
- If both stops enabled, use the tighter stop (i.e., the stop closer to current price).
- Close the position immediately if price touches/exceeds the stop level.

Backtest should support both long and short, and handle daily VWAP reset using the data datetime (new date => reset VWAP cumulative sums).

During the midday window, only consider entries when current bar volume is below the 20-bar SMA of volume (low-volume filter). Exit conditions: (1) stop-loss hit, or (2) opposite RSI signal (for shorts: RSI crosses up above 60; for longs: RSI crosses down below 40), or (3) time exit at 14:30. reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader intraday strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV bars. Build a Keltner Channel with midline = EMA(20) and bands = EMA(20) +/- 2 * ATR(14). Compute Williams %R(14). Entry rules: (1) Enter long with a market order when Close < lower Keltner band AND Williams %R < -80, only if not currently in a position. (2) Enter short with a market order when Close > upper Keltner band AND Williams %R > -20, only if not currently in a position. Exit rule: close any long or short when Close crosses/touches the Keltner midline (EMA20) in the direction of mean reversion (i.e., long exits when Close >= EMA20; short exits when Close <= EMA20). Risk management: place a protective stop-loss immediately after entry at 1.5 * ATR(14) away from the entry price (below entry for long, above entry for short) using stop orders; cancel/replace the stop if the position is closed. Make all key parameters configurable: ema_period=20, atr_period=14, keltner_mult=2.0, willr_period=14, stop_atr_mult=1.5. Support both long and short trading.
