reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 5-minute OHLCV data using RSI(14) divergences. Define pivots using a symmetric pivot window of N=5 bars: a pivot low occurs when low[-N] is the minimum of lows over the range [-2N..0], and a pivot high occurs when high[-N] is the maximum of highs over [-2N..0]. Bullish divergence: the most recent pivot low price is lower than the prior pivot low price, while the RSI value at the most recent pivot low is higher than the RSI at the prior pivot low. Bearish divergence: the most recent pivot high price is higher than the prior pivot high price, while the RSI at the most recent pivot high is lower than the RSI at the prior pivot high. Enter long (or short) on the bar immediately after the second pivot is confirmed; allow only one open position at a time. Exits: close long if RSI crosses above 70 OR if price hits a 2% stop-loss below the entry price; close short if RSI crosses below 30 OR if price hits a 2% stop-loss above the entry price. Use full available capital for each entry (size = cash / close for long; size = cash / close for short if shorting is allowed).

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 15-minute OHLCV data implementing an Intraday Midday Reversal concept. Define trading session times as 09:30-16:00 exchange time. Define a morning window 09:30-11:30 and a midday window 11:30-13:30 (both configurable). Determine the morning trend at 11:30 as follows: compute SMA(20); if Close at 11:30 is above SMA(20) and SMA(20) is rising versus its value 4 bars ago, classify as morning uptrend; if Close is below SMA(20) and SMA(20) is falling versus 4 bars ago, classify as morning downtrend; otherwise, do not trade that day. During the midday window only, use RSI(14) as the momentum confirmation: if morning uptrend and RSI crosses down through 50, enter a short; if morning downtrend and RSI crosses up through 50, enter a long. Use a fixed stop-loss at 2% from entry price (for long: entry*(1-0.02); for short: entry*(1+0.02)), checked each bar. Exit the position if the opposite RSI cross occurs (for long: RSI crosses down through 50; for short: RSI crosses up through 50) or at end-of-day liquidation time (e.g., 15:45), whichever comes first. Ensure only one position at a time and no new entries after 13:30.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader intraday breakout strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Compute a rolling 20-bar VWAP as VWAP20 = Sum(close * volume, period=20) / Sum(volume, period=20). Define VWAP slope as VWAP20 - VWAP20[-1]. Also compute SMA20 on close.

Entries:
- Go long when close crosses above VWAP20, VWAP slope > 0, and close > SMA20.
- Go short when close crosses below VWAP20, VWAP slope < 0, and close < SMA20.

Exits:
- For a long position, exit when close crosses below SMA20 OR a protective stop is hit.
- For a short position, exit when close crosses above SMA20 OR a protective stop is hit.

Risk management:
- Always place a stop-loss order immediately upon entry (use bracket orders). Use a fixed-percentage stop parameter (e.g., stop_pct=0.005): long stop = entry_price * (1 - stop_pct), short stop = entry_price * (1 + stop_pct). Ensure only one position at a time and cancel/replace any existing stops when positions change.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV data. Compute SMA20 on close. Define SMA slope as: slope = (SMA20[0] - SMA20[-20]) / 20. Compute slope_std = StdDev(slope, period=100). Define dynamic thresholds: pos_th = +2 * slope_std and neg_th = -2 * slope_std. Entry rules (reversal after extreme): (A) Long when slope crosses up above neg_th (i.e., slope[-1] <= neg_th[-1] and slope[0] > neg_th[0]). Risk: apply a fixed 2% stop-loss from entry price (for long: entry*0.98; for short: entry*1.02) using stop orders or manual exit logic. Exit rule: close any open position when slope crosses the neutral level 0 (i.e., for long: slope crosses up through 0 or simply crosses 0 in either direction; for short: same), or alternatively when abs(slope) falls below neutral_band where neutral_band = 0.2 * slope_std. Ensure the strategy uses Backtrader indicators (SMA, StdDev) and a custom line for slope, and includes parameters for sma_period=20, slope_lag=20, std_lookback=100, extreme_mult=2.0, stop_pct=0.02, and neutral_mult=0.2.

reformulated_task: Backtrader strategy for AAPL on 5-minute bars using EMA(5) breakout from a setup candle. Define a 'setup candle' for LONG when its high is below EMA(5) at that bar (i.e., candle is fully below EMA). After a setup candle appears, place a stop entry to go long if any later bar trades above the setup candle high (use next-bar stop order at setup_high). Define a 'setup candle' for SHORT when its low is above EMA(5) (candle fully above EMA). After a short setup candle, place a stop entry to go short if any later bar trades below the setup candle low (next-bar stop order at setup_low). On entry, set stop loss at the setup candle low (long) / setup candle high (short). Set take profit at 3R (target = entry + 3*(entry - stop) for long; entry - 3*(stop - entry) for short). Only one position at a time; cancel any pending entry order if an opposite setup occurs before entry. Risk/position sizing: size each trade such that maximum loss at stop equals 5% of current equity (broker value), i.e., size = (0.05 * equity) / stop_distance. Use Backtrader built-in EMA indicator and standard order types.

reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute OHLCV bars. Compute CCI with period=20. Define CCI slope as the linear regression slope of the CCI line over the last 20 bars (or equivalently use backtrader.indicators.LinearRegressionSlope applied to the CCI line with period=20). Maintain a rolling mean and rolling standard deviation of this slope over the last 20 bars (use SMA and StdDev on the slope line). Entry rule: when not in a position, compute z = (slope - mean_slope) / std_slope (skip signals when std_slope==0). If z > +2, enter long; if z < -2, enter short. Risk management: upon entry, place a protective stop-loss at 2% from entry price (long stop = entry * 0.98, short stop = entry * 1.02). Exit rule (mean reversion): close the position when the slope crosses back to the rolling mean (i.e., long exits when slope <= mean_slope; short exits when slope >= mean_slope). Strategy must allow both long and short trades.

reformulated_task: Mean reversion strategy on 5-minute AAPL data using a clearly defined correlation-based indicator. Define the indicator as rolling autocorrelation: the Pearson correlation over LookbackPeriod=20 between AAPL close and its 1-bar-lagged close (or alternatively between 1-bar returns and their 1-bar lag). Compute a rolling mean and rolling standard deviation of this autocorrelation over the same period (20). Compute z = (corr - mean) / std. Entry: if z > +2.0 and then z turns down (e.g., z[0] < z[-1]) enter SHORT; if z < -2.0 and then z turns up (z[0] > z[-1]) enter LONG. Place a stop loss at 2% from entry price. Exit: close the position when the indicator mean-reverts, defined as z crossing back through 0 (or corr crossing back above/below its rolling mean). Risk level medium (fixed position sizing acceptable).

Also exit and reverse only if an opposite gap signal occurs on a new trading day (i.e., close and open the opposite position based on that day's gap).

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using three timeframes: daily (trend filter), 4-hour (confirmation), and 60-minute (entry trigger). Use Backtrader resampledata to create 4H and 1D from a 60-minute base feed.

Indicators (all computed per timeframe as needed): RSI(14), Stochastic(14), CCI(20), Williams %R(14), MACD(12,26,9), Momentum(10), ROC(9), BollingerBands(20,2), SMA(10/20/50/100/200), EMA(10/20/50), ATR(10), Volume SMA(20), OBV, MFI(14), AccumDist.

Custom indicators (implement in-code):
1) Supertrend(period=10, multiplier=3): use ATR-based upper/lower bands and trend direction switching.
2) VPT (Volume Price Trend): cumulative VPT where VPT[t]=VPT[t-1] + Volume[t]*(Close[t]-Close[t-1])/Close[t-1].
3) Dynamic channel: use Donchian channel (highest high/lowest low) over 20 bars.
4) Order block proxy: define a bullish order block as the most recent down candle before a 20-bar breakout above Donchian high, and bearish order block as the most recent up candle before a 20-bar breakdown below Donchian low. Consider 'in order block zone' if current price is within that candle's high/low range.

Scoring:
- For each timeframe, compute a score in [-10, +10] by summing individual signals (+1 bullish / -1 bearish / 0 neutral) from: RSI (>55 bull, <45 bear), Stoch K crossing above D below 20 bull / crossing below above 80 bear, CCI (>100 bull, <-100 bear), Williams %R (>-50 bull, <-50 bear), MACD histogram >0 bull <0 bear, Momentum>0 bull <0 bear, ROC>0 bull <0 bear, price above mid Bollinger bull / below mid bear, EMA10>EMA20>EMA50 bull / EMA10<EMA20<EMA50 bear, OBV slope(5) up bull / down bear, MFI (>55 bull, <45 bear), AccumDist slope(5) up bull / down bear, Supertrend direction (up bull/down bear), VPT slope(5) up bull/down bear, and channel breakout (close above Donchian high bull / below Donchian low bear).

Multi-timeframe alignment:
- Daily trend filter: allow longs only if daily score >= +3; allow shorts only if daily score <= -3.
- 4H confirmation: require 4H score >= +2 for longs or <= -2 for shorts.
- 60m trigger: enter long when 60m score >= +2 AND price is not inside bearish order block; enter short when 60m score <= -2 AND price is not inside bullish order block.

Orders and risk management:
- Use bracket orders. Initial stop-loss = 2 * ATR(10) on the 60m timeframe from entry price. Take-profit = 3 * ATR(10). Optional trailing stop: after trade moves +1 * ATR in favor, trail stop at 2 * ATR from highest (long) / lowest (short).
- Exit early if 60m score flips to opposite side (<= -2 for a long, >= +2 for a short) or if daily filter no longer supports the position.

Implementation requirements:
- Provide complete Backtrader strategy code including custom indicator classes for Supertrend and VPT, multi-timeframe data handling, score computation, and bracket order placement. Keep parameters configurable.

reformulated_task:
Create a Backtrader strategy for AAPL using 5-minute bars that trades long/short based on Donchian Channel width mean reversion. Use bt.ind.DonchianChannel(period=20) and define width = (donchian.hi - donchian.lo). Compute width_sma = SMA(width, period=20). Define upper_extreme = width_sma * DeviationMultiplier (DeviationMultiplier=2.0) and lower_extreme = width_sma / DeviationMultiplier.

Regime detection: if width crosses above upper_extreme, set a flag upper_regime=True (lower_regime=False). If width crosses below lower_extreme, set lower_regime=True (upper_regime=False).

Entry (pivot back toward mean):
- If upper_regime is True, enter SHORT when width crosses downward below upper_extreme (i.e., CrossDown(width, upper_extreme)).
- If lower_regime is True, enter LONG when width crosses upward above lower_extreme (i.e., CrossUp(width, lower_extreme)).

Risk management: on entry, attach a protective stop-loss at 2% from entry price (for long: entry*(1-0.02); for short: entry*(1+0.02)).

Exit (mean reversion completion): close the position when width crosses the mean (width_sma) in the direction of reversion (for short: CrossDown(width, width_sma); for long: CrossUp(width, width_sma)), or when the stop-loss is hit. Ensure only one position at a time.

reformulated_task: Build a mid-term breakout strategy for AAPL on 5-minute historical data that can trade both long and short by combining a custom Parabolic SAR indicator (with acceleration 0.02 and max acceleration 0.2) and a 20-bar average volume filter using a Simple Moving Average on volume. Go long when the close price pushes above the SAR and the current bar's volume is greater than the 20-bar average volume; go short when the close drops below the SAR with volume likewise above that average. reformulated_task: Create a Backtrader mean-reversion strategy for AAPL on 5-minute bars. Indicators: HMA(9) on close; SMA_HMA = SMA(HMA, 20); STD_HMA = StdDev(HMA, 20); ATR = ATR(14). Exit logic: close LONG when HMA crosses above SMA_HMA (i.e., HMA returns to its 20-period mean); close SHORT when HMA crosses below SMA_HMA. Allow only one position at a time; support both long and short.

Use full capital only (no leverage, no shorting), no stop-loss, and no partial positions.
